I also want master to explain it fully. Qianfeng said: Only thus can one sit firmly
on the ground. Yunmen said: Yes, yes. Qianfeng said the dharma body has three illnesses; Yunmen
said the dharma body has two illnesses. When Wansong was traveling, people in various places discussed it, saying:
Before arriving, one runs about; after arriving, one gets stuck; after breakthrough, one rests with nothing to rely on. These are the three illnesses. Now
Yunmen speaks of two, omitting the first, the running about before arrival. The latter two illnesses are clearly much the same. Foyan
master said: Riding a donkey while searching for a donkey is one; after finding you are riding a donkey, not being willing to dismount is also
an illness. That reduces the first two illnesses and omits the last one. Teachers, for a time, prescribe remedies according to disease,
each hanging down a skillful means. As for the two kinds of light, they are no different from the two kinds of
illness in the failure of the light to break through. As for the phrase nothing is clear anywhere and before one there seems to be something, Dong
shan said: Clearly face to face, there is no other truth, but alas, deluded people still take shadows for the head. If
one possesses the eye that holds heaven and earth steady, continuous and leaving out not the slightest bit, only then does one gain a small measure of accord.
Again he says, one penetrates the emptiness of all dharmas, yet dimly there seems to be some thing
there. This too is a failure of the light to break through. This is what Guishan meant by, not a single dharma can match the mind,
yet seeing still remains within a realm. The Shurangama Sutra says: Even if one extinguishes all seeing, hearing, awareness, and knowing, and inwardly
abides in dark stillness, it is still only differentiating shadow-work of dharma dust. Nanyuan Yong said: At that time I was
like walking inside a lamp’s shadow. Therefore it is said, this too is a failure of the light to break through.
In the Caodong family style, stillness sinks into dead water; movement falls into the present age. These are called the two illnesses. You
need only go out without following conditions, enter without dwelling in emptiness, outwardly not seeking branches, inwardly not abiding in concentration.
Then naturally the three illnesses and two lights are all penetrated at once. After that, whether breakthrough or no breakthrough,
set both aside. Examine carefully and ask what breath or trace remains; that too is
illness. How then can one attain ease? Let us ask Tiantong to diagnose it further. The verse says:

lg.l:
The whole forest of myriad forms may rise in rugged splendor; breakthrough without fixed method still obstructs the eyeLet him speak, what hinders you? If you recognize it, there is no grievance.
s.In a flash, brandishing the cudgel.

Who has the strength to sweep that gate and courtyard? Hidden in a person’s breastBrushing away traces leaves marks; the more you hide, the more it shows.
a feeling forms of itself.Doubt in the mind breeds hidden ghosts.

A boat lies across a wild ford, holding autumn’s blue; the oar entersDead water has soaked it through.
reeds and blossoms shining bright as snow.Staying on the bank only confuses people.

An old fisherman, with brocade strung, heads toward the market, driftingPutting down capital to seek profit.
lightly, a single leaf moving atop the waves.Following the current, one finds the subtlety.

cb:div.p:
The master cited: The Dharma Phrase Sutra says, The whole forest and the myriad forms are stamped by the one dharma. One
is precisely ten thousand, and ten thousand precisely one. It is this thing, not another thing. Let it be as rugged and bold as it may.
In a wild field one does not choose among weeds; in a pure ground one instead confuses people. Even if breakthrough has no fixed method, that is exactly
where it obstructs the eyes. The Perfect Enlightenment Sutra says: As for all deluded minds, one does not even extinguish them.
Dongshan said: Spiritual sprouts and auspicious grasses make the old farmer worry about weeding. Why must one sweep that gate and courtyard?
If one empties all dharmas, Yunmen says, nothing is clear anywhere, and before one there seems to be
some thing. He is not teaching you to remove illusory objects and extinguish the illusory mind, then seek elsewhere for a place of breakthrough. The Third
Patriarch said: The six sense-objects are not evil; they are the same as right awakening. This matches the Perfect Enlightenment Sutra: Knowing illusion, one at once
departs from it, without using expedients. Departing from illusion is awakening, and there are no stages. Then one sees that acting and stopping,
letting be and extinguishing, are like a vajra rubbing the back of a clay man. Again it says, hidden in a person’s breast
a feeling forms of itself. This verse praises that dim sense that there seems to be some thing there; this is exactly the Perfect
Enlightenment’s clinging to I and what awakens in me, secretly continuing like life itself, the subtle illness of the four marks. Therefore Pujue said:
Great compassionate World-Honored One, please swiftly explain the illnesses of Chan. A boat lies across a wild ford, holding autumn’s blue. This verse praises
attaining the dharma body, mooring the boat in clear source and deep waters. Shushan took the dharma body to be
a dead stump. This is truly a donkey-tethering post. Only when one turns the boat around does one avoid, yet still not escape, rowing
into the place where reed blossoms shine bright as snow. At this point the clear light dazzles the eyes and one seems to lose one’s home. When one clearly
turns the body around, one still falls into position. This verse praises: even if you have penetrated it, letting it pass unchecked is not allowed.
At this point Yunmen has said all he can, and Tiantong’s verse too has exhausted it. After that, if you want to see Yunmen’s
intent and Tiantong’s eye, this is exactly the place where gain and loss are reckoned. What is
Yunmen’s intent? Have you not seen him say: Examine carefully and ask what breath or trace
remains; that too is illness. Yunmen only points out the illness and does not speak the cure. What is
Tiantong’s eye? He states Yunmen’s remedy, saying: An old fisherman, with brocade strung, heads toward the market.
Lightly, a single leaf moves atop the waves. Yunmen’s great meaning lies in entering the world with hanging hands and not
avoiding wind and waves. It may be said that once one’s own illness is removed, one then pities the illnesses of others. This is Vimalakirti’s heart
indeed. Do you understand? One long sick knows the nature of medicines; having found effect, one dares pass on the prescription.

cb:div.head: The Twelfth Case: Dizang Plants the Fields
cb:div.p:
He addressed the assembly, saying: Men of talent plow with the brush; eloquent men plow with the tongue. In our house of patched-robe monks, we are too lazy to look at
the white ox in the open ground and pay no heed to rootless auspicious grass. How do we pass our days?

Citation: Dizang asked Head Monk Xiu, Where have you come from? Xiu said:Can you say you do not know where it comes from?
From the south. Dizang said: How is the Buddha Dharma in the south these days?Best to strike it down.Go on, say fine words.
Xiu said: There is vast, bustling discussion. Dizang said: How does that compare with my plantingIn a low voice.
fields here to earn food to eat? Xiu said: But what about the three realms? Dizang said:Show off less.This is still here.
What do you call the three realms?The south is still passable; the north is even drier.

The master said: Chan Master Guichen of Luohan Monastery in Zhangzhou. The governor of Zhangzhou, Lord Wang, at
Stone Mountain west of Min City, built Dizang Monastery and invited the master to reside there. After more than a decade he moved to Zhangzhou
Luohan, so the master was also called Dizang. Head Monk Xiu, Fayan, Wukong, and Jin
the head monk, had made friends beyond the lakes. Reaching Zhangzhou, they were blocked by rain and snow; the streams had risen, so they lodged in the city’s
western Dizang Monastery. Sitting around the brazier, they looked at Dizang as if no one were there. Dizang wanted to test them and also
came near the fire. Then he said: May I borrow a question about something? Xiu said: If there is something, please ask.
Dizang said: Mountains, rivers, and the great earth, together with all the venerable monks, are they the same or different? Xiu said: They are
different. Dizang held up two fingers. Xiu hastily said: They are the same, the same. Dizang also held up two fingers
and got up to leave. Fayan said: What was the abbot’s meaning in holding up two fingers? Xiu said: Random
babble. Fayan said: You must not, with a coarse mind, deceive him. Xiu said: How could a rat’s mouth have elephant
tusks? The next day they took leave. Before reaching their lodging place, Fayan said: You brothers go on ahead. I will
stay with Dizang. If there is something excellent there, fine; if not, I will come back and find you. Once Fayan had long practiced there,
Xiu and the other two also came to Dizang. He then asked: How is the Buddha Dharma in the south these days?
At that time it would only have been fitting to say, It is just the same as here on ordinary days. Instead he said, bustling discussion
everywhere, and without knowing it he himself stuck his head out. Dizang said: How does that compare with my here
planting fields to earn food to eat? At that time it would have been good to say: If so, then not only in the south
but elsewhere as well. Instead he further said, But what about the three realms? implicating all those Chan travelers from the south; worldly habits
still not removed. Out of compassion, Dizang spoke in a rough, descending way, saying: What do you call
the three realms? Better simply to say, This old monk is busy planting fields, and thus avoid
receiving a formal charge from Tiantong. The verse says:

lg.l:
Sect and doctrine, all these various forms are forced contrivance; once they pass through mouth and ear, they become fragmented.If it does not fit today, thenmonks,
do not blame me.

Planting fields to earn food is an everyday matter; unless one’s practice is fully ripened, one does not know it.There must not be something separate.If you want to know,
what is it?

When practice is full, one clearly knows there is nothing to seek; Zhang Liang in the end did not prize enfeoffment asYou must still ask Tiantong for guidance once more.
marquis.It is also a spiritual turtle dragging its tail.

Forgetting all contrivance, he returns and goes with fish and birds; washing his feet in the Canglang, amid mist andFollowing the current, one finds the subtlety.
autumn trees.The use of it is inexhaustible.

cb:div.p:
The master said: Qingliang said, penetration in the school is for one’s own cultivation; penetration in explanation is to show the not-yet-awakened. This originally comes from the
Lankavatara Sutra. The Buddha told Mahamati: There are two kinds of penetration. Penetration in the school is, by relying on one’s own
realization, obtaining the marks of superior advance, far removed from delusive imaginings of speech and letters, proceeding toward the undefiled realm, relying on
self-awakened approach, with radiant light shining forth. This is called the mark of penetration in the school. What is the mark of penetration in explanation?
It means expounding the various teachings of the nine divisions, concerning marks such as difference and non-difference, being and non-being, and so on, using
skillful means to teach according to what is appropriate. This is called the mark of penetration in explanation. Lecture monks say: Explanation penetrates,
but the school does not penetrate, like the sun enclosed by clouds. The school penetrates, but explanation does not, like a snake entering a bamboo
tube. The school penetrates, and explanation also penetrates, like the sun in empty space. Neither school nor explanation penetrates,
like a dog barking at a thicket. Once one divides school and explanation, one has already split into two roads. How much worse that Chan is split into
five houses and doctrine arranged into three vehicles. Within this, not even one is established; all is forced contrivance. How
much more so when it comes out the mouth and enters the ear, asking for instruction, citing and versifying, vines and creepers trailing all the way past Silla. Gong
county’s tea-bottle soup never runs dry. It is not only the south where discussions bustle noisily. If one is
a person whose words do not burn the mouth, eloquence may be like a hanging river, yet originally there is not a single word. Planting fields
to earn food, though it is everyday life, still, unless practice is full, one does not know its flavor. In ancient times
people in deep mountains, beside the hoe, in a broken-legged pot, cooked coarse millet rice. Their wealth did not exceed
knowing sufficiency. All their lives they sought nothing from others. Their nobility did not exceed pure leisure. Why need seals as big as
a peck measure? Therefore it is said: When practice is full, one clearly knows there is nothing to seek; Zhang Liang in the end did not prize enfeoffment as
marquis. In the Records of the Grand Historian, in the sixth year of Han, merit officials were enfeoffed. Someone said Zhang Liang had never had military
achievements in battle. Emperor Gao said: He devised plans within the command tent and determined victory a thousand li away.
That was Zhang Liang’s merit. He was allowed to choose for himself thirty thousand households in Qi. Liang said: At first I, at Xiapi,
met Your Majesty at Liu. This was Heaven granting me to Your Majesty. Using my plans, and luckily at the right
times they succeeded. I wish only to be enfeoffed at Liu, and that is enough. I dare not accept thirty thousand households. This verse does not require
opening the hall and expounding the Dharma in the southern style. In the Li Sao, the Fisherman’s Song says: The waters of Canglang
if clear, may wash my tassels; the waters of Canglang if muddy, may
wash my feet. This is where apes and cranes dwell together, fish and birds roam together. Tell me, what sort of
person is this? A true plank-shouldered fellow.

cb:div.head: The Thirteenth: Linji’s Blind Donkey
cb:div.p:
He addressed the assembly, saying: Always acting for others, not knowing there is a self. One must utterly exhaust the Dharma and care not whether there are
people or no people. One must break the wooden pillow and have vicious hands and feet. At the moment of departure, what should one
do?

Citation: As Linji was about to manifest extinction, he instructed Sansheng: After I have passed away,The old woman, at death’s door, bids farewell three times.
you must not extinguish my Treasury of the True Dharma Eye. Sansheng said: How would I dare extinguishWhy be in such a hurry to die?
master’s Treasury of the True Dharma Eye? Linji said: If suddenly someone asks you, whatPretending to be cautious, yet actually bold.
will you say? Sansheng then gave a shout. Linji said: Who would have known? My trueLying crosswise in a tiger’s mouth.Meeting the moment, he yields not even to his father.
Dharma Eye Treasury has been extinguished by this blind donkey.Where the reward is great, there will surely be brave men.

The master said: Linji instructed Sansheng not to extinguish his Treasury of the True Dharma Eye. This
is like Xinghua saying to Vinaya Overseer Kebin, Before long you will become a teacher who proclaims the Way, and then punishing
After eating gruel and rice he left the hall; the functioning of the device was the same. In truth, this matter is not increased by a thousand Buddhas appearing in the world,
nor diminished by a thousand sages entering extinction. How could a single Sansheng make it rise or perish? The ancients,
at the time of death, revealed this matter; it also showed there was someone in the assembly. Sure enough, Sansheng stepped forth
and said, How would I dare extinguish the Master’s treasury of the true Dharma eye? Like a man insulted who cannot let it pass,
he takes it head-on. At that very time he was given his proper fodder. The treasury of the true Dharma eye had not yet
been extinguished, yet he said, If suddenly someone asked you, how would you answer? When one should cut but does not cut,
one only invites disorder. Sansheng then shouted. In former generations and later ages, those born from this gate have had ringing ears
for three days, but they were not like this present shout. Linji said, Who knew that my treasury of the true Dharma eye
would be extinguished at the side of this blind donkey? At that time the style of Linji’s house had its own
correct command. It is a pity he let him pass. How did Tiantong judge it? His verse says:

lg.l: At midnight the robe and bowl were entrusted to Huineng, stirring up the seven hundred monks of Huangmei.A thief's son, a thief's cunning.If the top beam is not straight,

Linji’s single branch, the true Dharma eye, was extinguished by a blind donkey, winning people’s contempt.half bright and half dark, all depends on today.
Sweet in heart, bitter in mouth.

Mind sealing mind, patriarchs transmitting the lamp, leveling seas and mountains.A smuggler of illicit salt.Boring through the wall to steal light.
One punch topples Yellow Crane Tower, one kick overturns Parrot Islet.

The transformations of the kun-fish and roc are only names and words, hard to compare with this.Turn the hand and it is clouds, turn it over and it is rain.
To try to liken itStill thinking it too little.
in the end takes skill and agile turning.The treasury of the true Dharma eye is still here.

cb:div.p:
The master said: Huangmei transmitted it secretly; for twenty years North and South contended. Linji transmitted it openly,
and to this day some still do not recognize it. Such a method can truly make the kun and roc transform, and the seas
and mountains level out. Dayui Xiu said: The ancients endured until death waiting for one to come; why then did the treasury of the true Dharma eye
instead get extinguished at the side of a blind donkey? Linji acted too quickly,
and Sansheng too was hasty. Thus father and son forgot their feeling, and later people were left disappointed.
If one cannot get flowing water, one should still pass over another mountain. In this record Sansheng then bowed.
That was not yet the best move. Linji then gave a verse, saying: The flow does not cease, so how ask what it is?
True illumination is boundless, and I speak of it to him. Those beyond form and beyond name do not receive it. Once the hair-blowing sword has been used,
it must at once be sharpened. When the verse was finished, he sat upright and passed away. Tiantong, taking up this koan,
stops right at the proper place. Sansheng’s bow and Linji’s verse both greatly let things go by,
lightly letting go. Is there anyone who can breathe out for the ancients? Dangerous.

cb:div.head: Case Fourteen: Kuo the Attendant Serves Tea
cb:div.p:
Raised: Attendant Kuo asked Deshan, Where have all the sages from above gone?
Deshan said, What? what? Kuo said, Ordered and selected, a flyingIt is in your nostrils.A thunderclap is too swift to cover the ears.
dragon-horse; a lame turtle sticks out its head. Deshan then let it rest. The next day,A rich household spoils its children.Letting people off is not stupidity.
after Deshan came out from bathing, Kuo brought tea to him. Deshan patted Kuo once on the back. Kuo said, Only now has this old fellow had a glimpse. Deshan again let it rest.Ruining it at the top of the pole.
At the very top.Overturned carts in the same ruts.Tiger head, tiger tail.
Gathered up all at once.

The master said: Deshan ordinarily knocks wind and beats rain, scolds Buddha and curses the patriarchs. This monk committed an offense
towering to the heavens—why then was he let go? He did not know that to seize an ox you need no rope; to kill
a man you need no knife. When was he ever really let off? Old Huanglong said: Deshan feigned deafness
and played dumb; though he secretly gained an advantage, Master Kuo covered his ears while stealing a bell—
but what can he do about the ugliness seen by bystanders? Wansong said: It is more than stealing a bell—like at the bottom of a ninefold deep abyss, beneath the black dragon’s jaw,
plucking the pearl just when the dragon sleeps; if it awoke, he would surely be ground to bits. Dayui
Zhe said: If you do not ascend the Dragon Gate, how would you know the vastness of the blue sea? Even if waves strike a thousand fathoms,
what can you do if the Dragon King does not care? Wansong said: Tiny scales and scraps of shell are nothing to marvel at.
Foguo said: Deshan truly had vicious hands and feet. Seeing this monk was not one to receive
hammer and tongs, he therefore just let it rest. Wansong said: When the ancients met situations and faced occasions,
each had expedient means. Deshan said to Yantou, Later on you will crap on this old monk’s head.
Later Yantou indeed said, Big or small, Deshan does not understand the final
phrase. The ancients raised and lowered, released and seized—how could they be bound by gain and loss, victory and defeat? Huanglong
and Dayui only lifted the broad outline. Look further: Tiantong’s verse brings out the deep subtleties. The verse says:

lg.l: When it comes face to face, the adept knows; in such a moment, spark-fire and lightning are too slow.The deluded do not notice.Already past Silla.
The strategist who yields the move has deep intent; deceiving the enemy, the military tactician has no far-reaching thought.Hiding troops and stirring conflict.Driving deep into the enemy court.

If one strikes, it must hit—whom could one deceive further? Seeing cheeks behind the head—such a person is hardWell used to taking advantage of it.Seized together with the stolen goods.
to provoke.Once bitten by a snake,
With eyes set beneath the brows, he gains the advantage.feigning a blow, pretending not to know.

cb:div.p:
The master said: Where have all the sages from above gone? It is very much like missing it right in front of your face.
Deshan said, What? what? Deshan hid himself in decoy grass, then produced a dazzling
mirror before the eyes. Formerly seven wise women toured the charnel forest. One woman said, The corpse is here—
where is the person? One woman said, What? what? The women looked at one another and all
awakened to the Way. It moved the heavenly emperor to scatter flowers in offering. Deshan used this same device,
passing by on a borrowed road; one absolutely must not understand it that way. Therefore it says: in that very midst, spark-fire
and lightning are slow. How could Deshan not know the attendant could not let him pass? He set a go-between to seek
the dove, laying out the capital to make profit. Sure enough, he could not escape his snare. If suddenly someone asked
Wansong, Where are you going?—I would slap him across the cheek and say, Right here. This would make the flying
dragon and lame turtle draw in necks and gather their feet; attendant and Deshan would both lose their edge and tie their tongues. Do you yet recognize
old fellow Deshan? In youth he once broke the dragon-serpent battle lines; in decline he still listens to a child’s
song.

cb:div.head: Case Fifteen: Yangshan Sticks in the Spade
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: Knowing before words are spoken is called silent discourse. Not clarifying yet revealing itself is called
the hidden pivot. Joining palms before the monastery gate, walking the way beneath the two corridors—there is a certain meaning there. In the
courtyard one dances; outside the rear gate one shakes the head—what about that?

Raised: Guishan asked Yangshan, Where have you come from? Yangshan said, From the fields.It is not that you do not know where it comes from.
Guishan said, How many people are in the fields? Yangshan stuck down the spadeWhy then do you fall among the outlaws?Only father and son, the two of them.
and stood with hands crossed. Guishan said, On South Mountain many are cutting thatch. Yangshan picked up the spade and went off.Letting it go is more dangerous.Beat the grass
and startle the snake.Pulling it in too quickly.

The master said: Teacher and disciple accorded with the Way; father and son matched in the pivot. The style of the Gui-Yang house is a thousand-year tortoise mirror.
Guishan asked Yangshan, Where have you come from? Did Guishan not know Yangshan came from the fields?
He let down this one question in order to meet Yangshan. Yangshan did not fail the question;
he only said, From the fields. Tell me, is there still some Buddhist doctrine in this or not?
Guishan went deep into the tiger’s den and again asked, How many people are in the fields? Yangshan stuck in the spade
and stood with folded hands; at once patch-robed monks met each other. Xuansha said, If I had been there then,
I would have kicked over the spade. Wansong said: Unable to suppress a laugh. Master Touzi Qing
in verse said: Few know the tune where Guishan questioned; thrusting the spade into the ground, he answered Buddha and patriarchs
and sank them. Xuansha’s kicking it over is a side refusal, lest the green mountains be burdened with deep spring. Wansong
said: When the grass is dry, the hawk’s eye is keen. Master Ping of Nanyue Falun wrote: On a narrow road
when they meet, there is no room to avoid each other. When he stuck down the spade he stood with folded hands; after crossing the bridge he came to the bank
and walked on; only then he realized his whole body was wet with mud and water. Wansong said: One cannot bear to look back in the moonlight.
In the verses of these two old worthies there is only a thousand-foot cold pine. Look further—Tiantong
lets loose a stone shoot sending out branches. His verse says:

lg.l:
The old awakened one, rich in feeling, thinks of his descendants; now, ashamed, they rise up to continue the household.Too much old-woman concern.For thirty years not
lacking salt and vinegar.

It is necessary to remember the words of South Mountain, carving them into bone and inscribing them in flesh, together repaying the grace.Noble people forget much.
A resentful mind does not let go.

cb:div.p:
The master said: This verse is like Han Yu’s Biography of Mao Ying—principle and event both are manifest, truth and convention both
are raised together. Looking at it one way, Guishan is the old awakened one, and Yangshan and those below are the descendants.
But within it, it is not so. A monk asked Changsha Cen the Great Insect, Can the original person still become
a Buddha? Changsha said, Tell me—does the Son of Heaven of Great Tang still cut thatch and mow grass?
Thus we know cutting thatch is the business of ministers and sons. Now, ashamed, they rise up to continue the household. A thousand-year
shadowless tree; in these times, bottomless boots. Upholding the moon over a thousand peaks; robe and bowl in a stream of
clouds—all these are capable descendants inheriting the family enterprise. Thus we know ruler and minister, father and son—
this was not first established by Caodong; the father-and-son pair of Gui and Yang had already carried out this command. If it were not for
Guishan pointing it out, one would only linger at the gate of light and shadow, playing with the breath of gruel-and-rice Zen, in front of donkeys and behind horses,
taking that for one’s whole life—most regrettable. Therefore Tiantong taught: Remember the South Mountain
phrase about cutting thatch; carve it into bone and inscribe it in flesh, and still you cannot exhaust repaying the grace. Fadeng said:
An old rustic carries firewood home, urging his wife to weave through the night. Look how busy his household affairs are—but tell me,
by whose strength is this sustained? Ask him and he does not know; for no reason doubt and confusion arise. Alas for people of past and present—
how many know grace and virtue? Once they truly know, what then? Cutting off an arm they do not feel pain; standing
in snow they dare not grow weary. Therefore in old age Wansong lived at Bao’en Monastery.

cb:div.head: Case Sixteen: Magu Shakes the Staff
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: Point to a deer and call it a horse; clutch earth and make gold. On the tongue rise wind and thunder; between the brows
a bloodied blade is hidden. Sitting, one watches success and failure; standing, one tests life and death. Tell me, what kind of samadhi
is this?

Raised: Magu carried his staff to Zhangjing, circled the meditation seat three times, shook the staff once,
and stood straight and still. Zhangjing said, Yes, yes. Magu then went to Nanquan.How laughable—he has “Zen.”For now, believe half of it.
He circled the meditation seat three times, shook the staff once, and stood straight and still.Tomorrow, present it again for the King of Chu to see.
Nanquan said, No, no. Magu said, Zhangjing said yes—why does masterAnd again, believe only half.
say no? Nanquan said, Zhangjing is yes; it is you who areOpening one’s eyes inside the coffin.
not. This is turned by the force of the wind and in the end becomes ruin and decay.Frost added on top of snow.To kill a man, you must see blood.

The master said: Formerly Yangshan went to Zhongyi to thank him for the precepts. Zhongyi, on the meditation seat, clapped his
hands and said, Ah, ah! Yangshan went from west to east, from east to west, and again
stood facing the center; only then did he thank him for the precepts. Zhongyi said, Where did you get this samadhi? Yangshan said,
I learned it from Caoqi’s cast-off seal. Zhongyi said, Tell me, Caoqi’s samadhi receives
what sort of person? Yangshan said, It receives One-Night Enlightenment. Yangshan then asked, Where did master get
this samadhi? Zhongyi said, I got this samadhi from Great Master Ma. Magu of Puzhou,
Chan master Baoche, was just like Yongjia when he first met the Sixth Patriarch. He carried his staff to Zhangjing,
circled the meditation seat three times, shook the staff once, and stood straight and still. It was also like learning from Caoqi’s
cast-off seal. This is called the Samadhi-King samadhi; all samadhis
are born from this. Zhangjing said, Yes, yes. Wansong said, What is wrong with that? Sheng
Moguang the monk said: In yes there is no fixed yes; in no there is no true no. Yes and no have no master.
The myriad goods return together. Owl and rooster, day and night, vainly split themselves apart. I have no three-inch tongue; can a turtle
be called a tortoise? Kasyapa would not agree; let him knit his brows as he likes. Wansong said: Much sorrow brings early
old age. Magu wanted to test and compare with the monasteries everywhere. Magu had also once gone to National Teacher Zhong,
where he circled the meditation seat three times, shook the staff, and stood. The National Teacher said, Since you can do thus,
what use is there in seeing this poor monk again? Magu shook the staff again. The National Teacher said, This wild fox spirit—
get out! Look at how host and guest meet: there is illumination and there is function, there is a head and there is a tail. Truly,
from long familiarity it is hard to forget, and habit makes it easy. He then went to Nanquan, circled the seat, and shook the staff,
standing as before. Nanquan, however, said, No, no. It was as if he had already made arrangements with Zhangjing.
Dayui Zhe said: When Zhangjing said yes, he fell into Magu’s
trap. When Nanquan said no, he also fell into Magu’s trap. As for Dayui, it was
not so. If suddenly someone carried a staff, circled the meditation seat three times, and stood straight and still, I would only
say to him, Better not come to this point, and give him thirty blows. Wansong said: One must not
merely speak of acting—just strike. Magu said, Zhangjing said yes; why does master
say no? It does no harm to leave him in doubt. Nanquan said, Zhangjing is yes; it is you who are
not. Acting on the pivot when it appears, pushing a man at the edge of danger. National Teacher Shanguo of Yuantong said: Magu
is yes; Nanquan is not. These words are exactly like when Yong, hermitage master of Dengfeng, asked
the monk Shenqi, We have not met for long—what have you been doing? Qi said, Recently I met Treasury Master Wei,
and there was a place of ease. Yong said, Try to tell it to me. Qi then related what he
had attained. Yong said, You are yes, Wei is not. Qi could not fathom it, and returned and told Wei.
Wei laughed loudly and said, You are not, and Yong is not-not. Qi ran to verify this with Nanchan of Jicui.
Nanchan also laughed loudly. When Yong heard of it, he made a verse, saying: Bright and dark intermingle in the killing
and life-giving pivot; only Samantabhadra knows the realm of great persons. On the same branch one lives, on a different branch one dies. Smile...
The old adept in Daoan Hermitage. Juefan said, observing his words, one can imagine the elegant charm of his Dharma-joy play at that time.
Wansong said, Magu, Zhangjing, and Nanquan all had no shallow inspiration here.
If one hears this as merely something turned by the force of wind and destined to decay and fail,
that is even more laughable. This Teacher Wang not only held heaven and earth firmly in hand, he also had a way out.
Monk Yong of Baoning praised it, saying: In color and form it seems exactly real; before people it is handled and displayed, newly gleaming.
But once it enters the fire and is smelted again, in the end it turns out to be false silver.
When Magu reached this point, tiles shattered and ice melted away. If you want icy rivers to burst into flame
and iron trees to bloom, you must have Tiantong provide another turning phrase. The verse says:

lg.l:
Is and is not, look well at the snare, seeming to press down, seeming to raise up.The slim-waisted drum is struck from both ends.The prickly point is right in there already.
Raising.Hands lift, hands press down.

Hard to be elder brother, hard to be younger brother. Even if he seizes in the moment,Heads high, heads low.Turn the hand and it is clouds.
if so, why should I make anything special of it?Turn it over and it is rain.

One shake of the golden staff, supremely solitary and lofty; around the rope-bedShedding dust and leaving the ordinary.
three circuits, leisurely at play.Swinging the arms while walking along.

In the monasteries, bustling and noisy, right and wrong give rise to imaginings;A short man watching the opera.
before a skull they see ghosts.If a house has the Baize diagram, there will be no such monsters.

cb:div.p:
The master said: This public case rests entirely on the point of is and is not. People nowadays all say
Magu was toyed with by Zhangjing and Nanquan. Only Dawei Zhe said:
When Zhangjing said is, he too fell into Magu's trap; when Nanquan said is not, he also fell into Magu's trap.
It is like a diamond jewel in the sun, its light and color unfixed.
Tiantong said, Look well at the snare. Magu falls into the snare; Nanquan falls into the snare.
Is it pressing down or raising up? Hard to be elder brother, hard to be younger brother. Tiantong means that at first glance it seems to suppress and seems to elevate;
but when checked in detail, hard to be elder brother, hard to be younger brother. In the Eastern Han, Chen Yuanfang's son was Changwen,
namely Chen Qun. Together with Jifang's son Xiaoguang, they each discussed their fathers' merits
and could not settle it, so they asked Taiqiu. Taiqiu was Chen Shi, the father of Yuanfang and Jifang.
Taiqiu said, Yuanfang is hard to be the elder brother; Jifang is hard to be the younger brother. This means Zhangjing's
left eye is half a catty and Nanquan's right eye is eight liang. One shake of the golden staff, supremely solitary and lofty.
The Yongjia Song of Enlightenment also says, It is not a matter of displaying outward form and handling empty affairs; it is the Tathagata's
precious staff, a personal trace. Xuedou said, The ancient staff's wind is high through the twelve gates; in every gate
there is a path, empty and desolate. The Sutra of the Staff says the twelve rings are for contemplating the twelve
links of dependent origination and cultivating the twelve-gate meditation. The twelve links are easy to know. The twelve gates are the four
dhyanas, the four immeasurables, and the four formless samadhis. The ancient staff's wind being high is precisely this supremely solitary loftiness.
The Sixth Patriarch also said, A renunciant possesses three thousand dignified deportments and eighty thousand subtle practices.
Great virtue, from where does this great arrogance arise? Tiantong's meaning is: it is not display of form,
nor is it arrogance. Three times around the rope-bed, leisurely at play. Zhangjing said is; Nanquan
said is not. The monasteries bustle and rush about inside right and wrong. If one lacks the eye that firmly holds
heaven and earth, then all of it is seeing ghosts before a skull. Have you not seen: a monk asked Jiufeng,
What is the eye that firmly holds heaven and earth? Feng said, Heaven and earth are right here.
The monk said, Then where is the heaven-and-earth eye? Feng said, That is exactly the heaven-and-earth eye.
The monk said, Why then did you just say heaven and earth are right here? Feng said, If not so, before a skull one sees
countless ghosts. Here Wansong has a spell-master. He struck the staff once and said:
Quickly, quickly, as by imperial command.

cb:juan.cb:jhead: Elder Wansong's commentary on Tiantong Jue's verses on old cases, Record of the Congrong Hermitage, volume one.

Elder Wansong's commentary on Tiantong Jue's verses on old cases.
Record of the Congrong Hermitage, volume two.

body.byline: Proofread by the later student Xingyi.

cb:div.head: Case Seventeen: Fayan and a Hair's Breadth.
cb:div.p:
Address to the assembly: A pair of lone geese beat the air and fly high; a pair of mandarin ducks stand alone by the pond's edge.
Let us leave aside arrow-points meeting tip to tip. What about when the saw cuts through the balance weight?

Raised: Fayan asked Head Monk Xiu, A hair's breadth of difference and heaven and earth are far apart. How do you understand it?
Xiu said, A hair's breadth of difference and heaven and earth are far apart. Fayan said, How can that do? Xiu said, I onlyWho dares to touch it?A hundred dǒu.
What is difficult about grass?An iron mountain lies across the road.
am like this. How about you, master? Fayan said, A hair's breadth of difference and heaven and earthTwist the nose around.
are far apart. Xiu then bowed.I thought there was something else.Take a mistake and make it into another.

The master said: Head Monk Xiu and Fayan studied together under Dizang, and deeply gained the power of close mutual polishing.
This case is like the head of awakening in Zhedao the superintendent.
Chan master Xuanze of Bao'en in Jinling: Fayan asked him, Whom have you seen come here?
Xuanze said, I saw Master Qingfeng. Fayan said, What words were spoken? Xuanze said,
I once asked, What is the student's self? Qingfeng said, A Bingding boy
comes seeking fire. Fayan said, How do you understand it, venerable one? Xuanze said, Bing and ding belong to fire.
To seek fire with fire is like seeking oneself with oneself. Fayan said, To understand it that way, how can that do?
Xuanze said, I am only like this. I do not know your venerable meaning, Master.
Fayan said, You ask me and I will tell you. Xuanze said, What is the student's
self? Fayan said, A Bingding boy comes seeking fire. Xuanze was suddenly enlightened at these words.
Fayan held hook and awl in hand: if one goes, he seals it shut; if one stays, he breaks the seal.
By breaking it open, he smashed the superintendent's emotional barrier and pulled open Head Monk Xiu's lock of consciousness. In the Third Patriarch's Faith in Mind it says:
The great Way is not difficult, only avoid choosing and selecting. If there is no hate and love, all is bright and clear. A hair's breadth of
difference and heaven and earth are far apart. Fayan took this and asked Head Monk Xiu, using it as a brick to knock at the gate.
If you ask a thousand people nowadays, a thousand will take it as doctrine.
Otherwise they just fall into the realm of nothing-to-do. Xiu did not fall into discursive thought; he only said
A hair's breadth of difference and heaven and earth are far apart. It had quite a bit of reserve and depth. Yet Fayan
still did not approve, saying, How can that do? This is why he became the source of the Fayan
lineage. When Wansong gets here, he always tells students to split themselves and look from both sides.
In the first part, Head Monk Xiu spoke this way, so why was he not approved? In the second part, Fayan in turn
spoke the same way, so why was that so? Meanwhile Head Monk Xiu said, I am only like this. And
how about you, Master? He was hoping for a fresh sun and moon, a different way of life. Yet he did not
miss by a thread, and still as before only said, A hair's breadth of difference and heaven and earth are far apart.
Dongchan Qi said, The head monk answered this way; why was he not accepted? Then when he asked again,
Fayan also only said it this way and it worked. Tell me then, where does the discrepancy lie?
If you can see through it, you can say the venerable one has a source. Wansong says,
How can that do? Therefore it is said: it is only the road one walked before, but when speaking of it to others
it immediately goes astray. Xiu then bowed. To get it is to get it, but feeling and reason cannot contain it. Wuzu Jie
spoke on Fayan's behalf: split his back and strike him at once. Wansong said, Indeed he has his basis from Fayan.
In Fayan's sayings it says, The head monk is through. Wansong said, These mud-ball fellows, neither of the two
is finished. If Wansong had been there and heard Fayan say How can that do, he would have said:
Long have I heard the Master has this pivotal device. Otherwise I would simply wave my hand and leave, and I guarantee for a time
he would be cut off where he sat. If he would not believe it, then ask Tiantong. The verse says:

lg.l:
A fly sitting on the scale's head makes it tilt; for ten thousand ages the balance illuminates what is uneven.Cheat him by not even a star's worthThe peck measure is full
The scale weight settles

Catty, liang, zi, zhu reveal the exact point; in the end all submit to my fixed balance-star.Do not mistake it
Star.Grasp the meaning at the hook tip

cb:div.p:
The master said: With one opening line Tiantong already praises A hair's breadth of difference and heaven and earth are far apart.
Huiyuan of Mount Lu said, From where does the root finally arise? Birth and death have no boundary.
The slightest involvement with the moving realm resembles a collapsing mountain. The Third Patriarch used the word avoid and first already
brought in hate and love, then said: only if there is no hate and love, all is bright and clear. All of you, step back
a pace and carefully examine yourselves. The Sanskrit samadhi is rendered as even holding.
It is neither sinking nor agitation, but equal sustaining. This can serve as the balance for ten thousand ages
to illuminate what is uneven. In the Illustrated Essentials of Government it says the scale has three meanings: the beam is what is bound,
the crossbar is what levels, and the weight is the hanging mass. The Surangama Sutra says: within one's own
abiding samadhi, seeing and the conditions of seeing, together with the forms one conceives, are like flowers in the sky,
originally without anything real. This seeing and its conditions are originally the wondrous, pure, luminous body of bodhi.
How could there be in it what is and what is not? At just such a time here, there is no need to avoid selecting,
nor to depart from hate and love. There is not even a hair's breadth of difference, so how could there be a gulf like cloud and mud?
As for liang, zi, and zhu: eight zhu make one zi, three zi make one liang, and sixteen liang make one catty.
For one who holds the balance in hand, if you bring one catty, I move once
and make it level. If you bring one liang, I also move once and make it level. If there is the slightest increase or decrease by zi or zhu,
it tilts at once. The various houses say, Grasp the meaning at the hook's head; do not fixate on the balance-star.
For on the balance-star itself there are originally no weights. It is also like the North Star, settled in its place.
Increase and decrease at the hook's head are reckoned according to the moment. Wansong says: To level things with intention is not as good as
having no intention and yet not being uneven. Therefore on a starless scale one lets people profit in selling; on the backgammon board
one trusts fortune to win. Do you understand Fayan's saying, The head monk is through? The scale weight has moved
to the very tip of throughness; suddenly it is squeezed off and turns a somersault.

cb:div.head: Case Eighteen: Zhaozhou's Dog.
cb:div.p:
Address to the assembly: A gourd on water turns as soon as you press it. A jewel in the sun has no fixed color or form.
It cannot be attained by no-mind, and cannot be known by mind. In the channels of a measureless great person,
words turn and turn. Is there anyone who can avoid it?

Raised: A monk asked Zhaozhou, Does a dog also have Buddha-nature or not? ZhouBlocking the street and chasing scraps
said, It has. The monk said, Since it has, why then did it crash into thisNot added in the least
skin bag? Zhou said, Because it knowingly offends.At one charge he confesses and himself steps forwardFor now do not confess and admit it is not so
Another monk asked, Does a dog also have Buddha-nature or not? Zhou said, No.Born of one mother
The monk said, All sentient beings all have Buddha-nature. Why then does the dog
Not diminished in the least
not have it? Zhou said, Because karmic consciousness is there.A foolish dog chases a hawkThe foregoing is fully stated; according to the confession the case is closed

The master said: If you say the dog's Buddha-nature truly is yes, later he still said no.
If you say it truly is no, earlier he still said yes. If you say saying yes and saying no was only
a temporary response to conditions, when pressed and spoken, each has its reason. Therefore it is said: a clear-eyed one
has no fixed nest. This monk's questioning sought broad learning and did not rely on his own portion. Zhao
Zhou said yes, using poison to remove poison, using sickness to cure sickness. The monk then said, Since it has it,
why crash into this skin bag? He did not realize he himself had already been born into a dog's belly.
Zhou said, Because it knowingly offends. One hammer blow settles both sides. Such swiftness is hard to meet.
That monk thought it was judging the effect by the cause. If you understand it that way, even as a lecturer's servant
you still will not do. Later another monk asked again, and he then said no. This is how one who has it
acts. Saying yes has a place to emerge; saying no also has a place to emerge. This monk
followed the text and pressed the root text: All sentient beings have Buddha-nature, so why does the dog
not have it? With a press like this, I dare say it is a hand that stirs Heaven's Gate, leaving no road to turn around.
Yet he gently said, Because karmic consciousness is there. Tell me,
is there still blood under this monk's skin? Tiantong could not avoid adding moxa
on top of the raw flesh scar. The verse says:

lg.l:
The dog's Buddha-nature yes. The dog's Buddha-nature no. A straight hook was originally setBeaten into one lump, forged into one piece
to seek fish willing to lose their lives.This monk deserves to die today
Wayfarers of cloud and water chase scent and seek traces.Even with his nostrils pierced he does not know it

Clamorous and noisy, they make distinctions; spread it all flat and display a greatCompeting to gnaw dry bones, snarling and barkingNo chance to bluff, so stop deceiving one another
broad layout.Great talent, bold words

Do not blame our house for not being careful at the start; pointing out flaws,Once a word leaves the mouth, four horses cannot return it
he even snatches away the jade disk.Barehanded he steals with clever skill
The King of Qin did not recognize Lin Xiangru.He misses it face to face

cb:div.p:
The master said: The dog's Buddha-nature yes and the dog's Buddha-nature no are two different sections.
Tiantong brings them up together, just as Xuedou said: one yes has many kinds, two no's are not of two sorts. Tian-
tong wanted to meet Zhaozhou face to face, so he praised it this way. Yingtian Zhendao said, A straight hook
To hook a fierce dragon with a bent hook is to catch only shrimp. Later people chase scents and traces
like hunting dogs. Noisy and scattered—what juice is there on dry bones? Though Zhaozhou greatly spread out
his mat, in the end it was only a level, open discussion. Tiantong unties wrists with Zhaozhou.
Do not blame this old fellow for not being cautious at the start. Guizong asked a scholar, What classics do you study? The scholar
said, I know the script styles of twenty-four schools. Zong pointed a dot in the air and said, Do you understand?
The scholar said, I do not. Zong said, And yet you say you know twenty-four script styles. Even the eight strokes of the character yong
you do not know. Prefect Li Bo asked, As for the Three Vehicles and the twelve divisions of the teachings,
I will not ask about them. What is the Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West? Zong likewise raised his fist and said, Do you understand?
Li said, I do not. Zong said, This pedant—even a fist he does not recognize. Wansong
said, Behold the lion turning over for all to see. It is not only in saying the dog has Buddha-nature
or saying it does not. Just this knowing and deliberately violating—karmic consciousness is still there. It is a great display of looking before
and glancing behind, being careful at the start and guarding the end. In the Records, King Hui of Zhao obtained the He family jade of Chu. King Zhao of Qin
offered fifteen cities to exchange for it. Lin Xiangru carried the jade in. The King of Qin rejoiced
and passed it around to the ladies and attendants. All the attendants cried, Long live the king. Xiangru saw the king
had no intention of ceding cities, so he stepped forward and said, The jade has a flaw; let me show it. The king handed over the
jade. Xiangru then held it, stood up, and leaning against a pillar, with hair bristling against his cap, said: The King of Zhao fasted
and purified himself for five days, then sent me to present the jade and deliver a letter at your court, honoring the majesty of the great state
to show respect. But now I see Your Majesty's manners are very arrogant. Having gotten the jade, you pass it to the ladies,
as if to toy with me, with no intent to cede the cities. Therefore I have taken back the jade. If you would force me,
then my head and the jade will both be shattered against this pillar. The king apologized and, looking at the map, assigned cities. He also fasted
five days. Xiangru then had an attendant in rough clothes hide the jade in his robe and take the direct road back to Zhao. Zhaozhou
first lets go and then snatches back—he has Xiangru's skill. Tiantong once had another verse saying: Zhao-
zhou says there is; Zhaozhou says there is not. The dog's Buddha-nature splits the world apart. A red face
is not as good as straight speech. If the heart is true, the words will surely be rough. A seven-hundred-cycle old Chan elder—
donkey dung, when meeting people, exchanged for eyeballs. Zhaozhou's heart is true and his speech straight: this is straight-hook fishing.
He was originally seeking fish fated to die. King Wen of Zhou went hunting and saw Jiang Ziya in the valley of Pan Stream,
three feet away from the water, fishing with a straight hook. The king found it strange and said, How can a straight hook
catch fish? Ziya said, I only seek fish bearing the fate to die. Donkey dung exchanged for eyeballs—
this is like Xiangru seizing back the jade. Fojian held up a rosary of soapberry seeds and said,
Do you all see it? After a long pause he said, This is what this old monk got in exchange when he came to the capital.
Each of you go back to your hall and feel for it yourselves. Fojian used soapberry beads; Zhaozhou
used donkey dung. Wansong, having no use for either, has never exchanged anything. If you all can truly trust it,
then as before your eyes are still beneath your eyebrows.

cb:div.head: Case Nineteen: Yunmen's Sumeru
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: I love Shaoyang's freshly-set devices; all his life he pulled nails and wedges for people.
Why then does he sometimes open the gate, set out a glue basin, and chisel a pitfall right in the road?
Try to choose and discern.

Raised: A monk asked Yunmen, If not a single thought arises, is there still fault? Yunmen said, Mount Sumeru.Words are pure, conduct is muddy
FellowDangerous

The master said: National Teacher Yuan-tong Shan said, Various places discuss this koan. Some
say, The very moment he asked like that, a thought had already arisen; the fault is like Sumeru. Others say, Like Sumeru,
unmoved by the eight winds, stable and ever at peace through the ages. Others say, For people it is hard to penetrate,
like Mount Sumeru. But all such discussion still fails to grasp where Yunmen's meaning lies.
If the bottom falls out of the bucket, if the red silk thread is cut, only then will you know it is not like that at all. Do you not see it said:
Three phrases illuminate one phrase; one phrase illuminates three phrases. Three and one do not interfere.
Clearly, this is the road upward. Foguo said, Yunmen's replies often stir people's discriminating consciousness.
Wansong said, Using discriminating consciousness to send off discriminating consciousness—unless one has great skill in dealing with people, one cannot
match it. This Mount Sumeru cannot be covered by heaven nor carried by earth. Wind cannot blow into it,
water cannot wet it. Only with vajra eyes, one glance straight through,
and you see seven holes and eight openings, smashing it to dust. Then on the eyebrows and eyelashes
it stands alone—lofty and steep. Chan Master Baiyunduan's verse says: Mount Sumeru
fills the universe; even Thousand-Hand Great Compassion cannot see through it. Unless you yourself can ride backward
on an ox, all your life you will not avoid trailing behind others. Wansong said: Face up, raise the brows alone; turn back
and clap your own hands. Master Qishanzhi's verse says: With nothing to do, you cast yourself at others seeking the name of guilt;
at once you are seized and lose your whole body. Before any confession is signed, the bustling ones
outside the gate—who knows how many there are? Only Tiantong is not in this category. His verse says:

lg.l:
Not raising a single thought—Mount Sumeru. Shaoyang's giving of Dharma was never stingy.One phrase and it is finishedTiantong also does not
fall short

If you are willing, both hands deliver it to you; if you start to think about it, a thousand fathoms cannotI only fear you cannot bear it
be climbed.In vain you split your brow

The blue sea is vast, the white clouds at ease—do not letEncompassing sky and bathing in sun, without shoreIn company with cranes, following the wind, attaining freedom
a single hairbreadth get lodged between them.Already too much has been born

A fake rooster's crow is hard to use to fool me—I will notThe true does not cover the false
muddle through and let you pass the barrier.The order from the Western Heaven is strict

cb:div.p:
The master said: You ask me whether, when no thought arises, there is fault or no fault. I then set out
a whole Mount Sumeru before your face. The benefit of that Dharma-gift
is certainly not stinginess. Yongjia said, The gate of great giving stands open without obstruction—not only today.
In Sanskrit it is Sumeru; here it is called Wondrous High. Formed of four jewels, it is wondrous; standing alone
above all peaks, it is high. Among the mountains of the four continents, Sumeru is foremost. If you
can affirm it yourself, I will hand it over with both hands. An old poem says: Wait for the place where the heart agrees—
that is when my fate and yours connect. In truth this matter is always plainly revealed, like Mount Sumeru
towering and rugged—who can cover it over? Before it is handed over, how could you have no share? When it is handed over
to you, how could it be something newly obtained? Do you not see? Changqing said: Amid the ten thousand forms, it alone reveals
itself; only when a person affirms it himself does it become intimate. Tiantong's verse, on reaching here, carries great reserved
power. If you cannot come without deliberating, then from a thousand or ten thousand miles away you can only look up and never reach.
Quyuan said: This matter is like collapsing cliffs and split rock, a sheer wall a thousand fathoms high, impossible to climb
or grasp. Yet in truth you have never been apart from it, nor have I ever taken it away. This, together with the previous line,
sets delusion and awakening in opposition, the pairing very clear. The teachings say Mount Sumeru goes into the water eighty
thousand yojanas, and rises above the water eighty thousand yojanas. Unless it is the Sagara sea, it cannot
contain it. Since the mountain is unmoving from old to now, the clouds too come and go in constant leisure. Dongshan said:
The green mountain is father to the white clouds; the white clouds are children of the green mountain. The white clouds lean on it all day,
yet the green mountain never knows. Tiantong, with leftover talent, praised Mount Sumeru as sea-vast and cloud-idle, completely
exhausting the marvelous subtlety. Is there room here for a single thought to arise and cease? Therefore he said: The blue sea
is vast, the white clouds at ease—do not lodge so much as a hair between them. This also accords with Xuedou's saying
that no sand can be tolerated in the eye. They study together. But if we speak of Shaoyang's unstinting Dharma-gift,
then he can even put Mount Sumeru into the eye. In this Sumeru verse, wherever it strikes and bumps,
the bloodlines run through. Every beat is command—not some vainly produced clever analysis that only increases
discriminating consciousness. In truth, a person in whom not one thought arises—how could he still ask whether there is fault or no
fault? Even if one is always abiding where no thought arises, when examined closely, what is it fit to be
used for? Therefore he said: A fake rooster's cry is hard to use to fool me; I am not willing to let you muddle through
the pass. Lord Mengchang entered Qin to serve as minister. Someone said to the king: Lord Mengchang is worthy, and moreover
he is of the Qi royal clan. If he serves Qin, he will surely put Qi before Qin. Qin will be endangered. The king
imprisoned him and wanted to kill him. Mengchang sought release through a favored consort. She said, I wish to have your fox-
white fur coat. By then the coat had already been presented to the king. A retainer skilled at stealing like a dog took the coat
and gave it to the consort. The lord gained release. At midnight they reached Hangu Pass. By law the gate opened only at rooster-crow
for travelers to leave. Another retainer, Pingxuan, was good at imitating a rooster; all the roosters crowed, and the lord escaped Qin's danger.
The master lifted his staff and said: Wansong today is also guarding the pass. If any among you can imitate a rooster's crow,
come forth. Then he leaned the staff back and said: I'll let one move pass.

cb:div.head: Case Twenty: Dizang's Intimacy
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Entering principle and speaking deeply, mocking this and grabbing that; on the great road to Chang'an, seven
vertical and eight horizontal. But if suddenly one opens the mouth and speaks it through, and at a step treads right on it, then one can hang up high
the bowl-bag and snap the staff. Tell me then: who is such a person?

Raised: Dizang asked Fayan, Reverend, where are you going? Fayan said, WanderingWhy frame people up
on pilgrimage. Dizang said, What is the matter of pilgrimage? FayanGo ask for the straw sandal moneySure enough, he does not let it pass
said, I do not know. Dizang said, Not knowing is most intimate. Fayan suddenlyWhy not say so earlierRobbing right on the spot
opened up and greatly awakened.Nearly cost the travel money

The master said: Yang Wuwei asked Master Furong Kai, How many years since we parted? Furong said,
Seven years. Yang said, Have you come studying the Way, or to practice Chan? Furong said, I do not beat that drum
and flute. Yang said, Then you've only wandered mountains and rivers in vain, good for nothing at all. Furong
said, We have not been apart long, yet your lofty discernment is excellent. Yang laughed aloud. Nanquan said, The Way
does not belong to knowing, nor to not knowing. Knowing is false awareness; not knowing is blank indifference. Now
when people hear Not knowing is most intimate, and that this was Fayan's point of awakening, they immediately go one-sidedly into
not knowing and not understanding, taking just that to be it. They do not realize that a single saying of the ancients is like
heaven universally covering, like earth universally supporting. Since not knowing is most intimate, then Heze's saying that knowing
is the gate of myriad wonders—what about that? If you say yes, then all is yes;
do not sit in the place of yes. If you say no, then all is no; do not sit in the place of no. Also,
you must penetrate the five ranks, the upright and the biased—how could you die beneath a phrase? As for Fayan's awakening here,
it too was merely words formed by chance. Master Dayin of Baishan said: Turning misfortune into blessing
is also here. Dizang's way of receiving people hooks them in the place before doubt, and with one sudden
cast Fayan fiercely awakens: so it was right here all along. The teacher of Cizhou said, You only need, in walking,
in sitting, when thought has not yet arisen, to sharply lift it up and look. The very moment you see,
whether seen or unseen, set that aside. Practicing like this, resting
does not hinder study, and study does not hinder rest. Master Qing of Touzi said,
Once the golden dragon has lost the water, the wondrous-winged bird quickly lifts it. Dizang's timing and conditions—
not the slightest gap. Tiantong had a tongue at the tip of his brush and proclaimed it again in verse:

lg.l:
Now, having fully practiced, it is like then: strip away every curtain-thread and arrive at not-knowing.I am still the man of old, yet not the man of old
This is still here

Let it be short or long—do not trim or patch; following high and low, it levels of itself.Wasted effort
It orders itself.No need to strain mind or strength

The household's plenty or thrift is used as the time requires; on the open ground one wanders at ease, trustingSalt and vinegar cannot be lacking
each step as it moves.If you want to go, then go

The pilgrimage business of thirty years ago clearly let down a pair ofNothing to deliberate
eyebrows.As before, it is right before your eyes

cb:div.p:
The master said: The Zongjing says, From the beginning delusion and awakening seemed like delusion; today awakening to delusion is not awakening.
Therefore it is said: Once awakened, one is still like one not yet awakened. When Dizang asked, he wanted to know the reason for setting out.
Fayan's answer was not mere modesty or polite refusal. Dizang then
immediately lifted it and said, Not knowing is most intimate. Fayan greatly awakened: this not-
knowing was precisely intimacy. Linji asked Luopu, Where have you come from? Luopu said, From Luancheng.
Linji said, May I ask you something? Luopu said, I do not understand. Linji said,
Break open the whole Tang empire and you still cannot find one who does not understand. Linji often used the killing
sword, and he also had the life-giving sword. It is not like Dizang, who in killing someone draws blood, yet for the person's sake
cuts through completely. This not knowing, not understanding, is utterly distinct in body. One must strip away every curtain-
thread before reaching the place of not knowing and not understanding. During communal field work at Weishan, Yangshan asked,
This side is so low; that side is so high—how is that? Wei said, Water can level
things; just use water to level it. Yang said, Water too has no fixed standard. Master, just level the high places
high, and the low places low. Wei assented. Sengzhao's Treatise on Prajna Without Knowing says:
That all dharmas are not different—does this mean lengthening a duck's legs, cutting a crane's, leveling mountains, filling valleys, and only then
calling them non-different? Therefore it is said: Let short be short and long be long; do not trim or patch. Follow the high,
follow the low, and it levels itself. Zhang Wujin said, All sorts of measuring and adjustment waste effort.
Let everything accord and follow, and skillful means are accomplished. Simply speak from trust and speak; use from trust and use.
Trust your feet and walk. In spring the flowers bloom; in autumn the leaves fall. If you understand in this way.
what donkey-legs are there to fuss over? Therefore Xuansha did not leave the ridge, and Baoshou did not cross the river.
Without going out the gate, one knows the affairs under heaven. Juefan praised it, saying: A face like a platter,
broad and wide, with eyes, ears, nose, and tongue dividing territories. Inside the skull none of them knows, while you listen
to them outside bickering and making strange claims. The mouth asked the nose: food and drink are in me, speech is in me.
What merit do you have, to be above me? The nose said: Among the Five Peaks, the central peak
holds the honored place. The nose then asked the eyes: why are you above? The eyes said: We are like sun and moon;
truly we have the function of illuminating and discerning. I dare ask the brows: what merit have you to be above us? The brows
said: I truly have no merit, ashamed to occupy the upper place. If I were allowed to be below, with the eyes above the brows,
just look what kind of face you would have. Therefore Chan Master Baoyue Ming ascended the hall and said: The ancients
said, in the eyes it is called seeing, in the ears it is called hearing. But tell me, in the eyebrows, what is it called?
What do you call it? After a long pause he said: In sorrow they share grief, in joy they rejoice together. People all know
the use of what is useful, but do not know the great use of the useless. Tell me then, Venerable Pindola
with both hands brushing his brows, what was his intent? The master brushed his brows and said: Cat.

cb:div.head: Case Twenty-One: Yunyan Sweeps the Ground
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: Casting off delusion and awakening, cutting off sage and ordinary, though there is not much to do, once host and guest are set up and noble and base distinguished,
it becomes another household altogether. Measuring ability and assigning roles is not without those of the same breath and joined branches.
How will you understand it?

Raised: As Yunyan was sweeping the ground, Daowu said, Too busy, too busy. Yunyan said, You should know there is one who is not busy. Wu said, In that case,Novices and attendants lack the strengthLaying hidden troops
Stirring up a fightWhat a pity the words got split in two
then there is a second moon. Yan lifted up the broom and said, ThisFar more than a second one, by the hundreds of millions
one, which moon is it? Wu then let it rest and left. XuanshaHe sticks his head out from the crystal palaceAll lies within what is not said
said, Precisely the second moon. Yunmen said, A maid seeing another maid, all diligenceOne person passes on a rumor, ten thousand pass it on as fact
and fuss.Following error and pouncing on the winnowing basket

The master said: Daowu bent and turned Yunyan, like Foguo urging and sharpening Fojian. This is what is meant by
If one is not indignant, do not enlighten him; if not struggling to speak, do not bring it forth. Yunyan was sweeping the ground, and Daowu casually
checked him. Yunyan said, You should know there is one who is not busy. Good people,
when you eat rice, brew tea, hold a needle, sweep the ground, at such times recognize the one that is not busy.
Then worldly dharma and Buddha-dharma become one piece. In the Dongshan house this is called going with both together.
Then your time will not pass in vain. Daowu immediately saw the seam and said, If so, then there is
a second moon. Xuedou added instead: Just then they should have let it pass. These two old men feared people
would leave the physical body and establish a separate dharma-body. National Teacher Zhong said to a southern Chan traveler:
Here our Buddha-nature is wholly unborn and undying. In your South, Buddha-nature is half born and half
dying, half unborn and undying. The traveler said, How do you distinguish them? The master said, Here, body
and mind are one suchness; outside mind there is nothing else, therefore it is wholly unborn and undying. In your South, the body
is impermanent, while spirit-nature is permanent, therefore half born and dying, half unborn and undying.
Reaching here, you must know there is a time to turn yourself and go to the father. Yan then raised
the broom and said, This one, which moon is it? These words originally come from the Shurangama Sutra.
The sutra says: As with the second moon, which is the moon, and which is not the moon? Manjushri
says there is only one true moon; in between there is no this-is-the-moon and that-is-not-the-moon. Daowu then stopped
and left. Some versions say Daowu flicked his sleeves and departed. Wansong said: One had seen through the other.
But tell me, was it Yunyan who saw through Daowu, or Daowu who saw through Yunyan? Those with clear eyes,
try inspecting it. Xuansha said, Precisely the second moon. This old fellow's mouth
carries yellow pigment for corrections; on his tongue is a sharp sword. Changqing said, If he had reversed the broom
and barred his face, then what? Sha let it rest. Luoshan said, Ah! These two
old men do not know good from bad. That fellow Yunyan, with hands and feet bound,
how long till he dies? Wansong said: Under Deshan's gate, one cannot fail to speak; under Dongshan's gate,
still it is not there yet. When Xuefeng was traveling, he went three times to Touzi and nine times up to Dongshan.
One day while rinsing rice, Dongshan asked: Are you washing away the sand and keeping the rice, or washing away the rice and
keeping the sand? Feng said: Sand and rice go away together. Shan said: Then what will the assembly eat?
Feng then overturned the basin. Shan said: It is attained, yes, but you still must see someone else before it will do. Later
he indeed succeeded Deshan. Xuansha and Changqing succeeded Xuefeng; Luoshan succeeded Yantou. All
came from Deshan's line. Thus one suppresses and one raises, words contrary but intent in accord. And now the Yunmen
and Dongshan two branches go forth together. How could there be superior or inferior? Yunmen said, A maid
sees another maid being overly diligent. Baofu said, Yunyan is very much like pushing a cart through mud, step by step
all busy and toilsome. These two old worthies also succeeded Xuefeng, so naturally their speech and tone agree. Their meaning is that
Yunyan could not use the broom to cut off the entangling vines. Yet they do not know that in rotten mud
there are thorns. Wansong, while unfolding and playing with this, before even finishing his lifting and bringing out, could not help laughing and said: Yun-
yan and Daowu clarified the Dongshan family's workings, yet this whole pack of old fellows, with many mouths melting
gold, not one of them righted the grievance for them. Luckily Tiantong drew his sword to help, and praised it
saying:

lg.l: Borrow it for the moment to settle the gate-front; once its use is gained as fitting, then at once let it go.Arising right where you areCeasing completely wherever you are
Before Elephant-Bone Cliff, the snake-handling trick, what one did in youth, in old age one knows to be ashamed of.If you want to speak of othersFirst set yourself right

cb:div.p:
The master said: If we discuss this matter, it is like a spark from stone or a flash of lightning. Yunyan raised it up to show people.
Changqing blocked him in the face and used it. Though their points of use differ, both return to transformation and cessation. What the Dongshan school
prizes is turning the pivot and shifting the position. Xuefeng had Elephant-Bone Cliff. Xuefeng once addressed
the assembly saying: On South Mountain there is a turtle-nosed snake; all of you must be very careful
and watch it well. Yunmen thrust his staff toward the front and made a gesture of fright. Is this not
precisely a maid seeing a maid being diligent? Yunyan swept the ground, raised the broom and said, This one
is which moon? All of them turned from the old trade and became capable descendants. Yet Yun-
men, to this day, still stands under the eaves and fence, so Tiantong would not let him pass, saying:
Before Elephant-Bone Cliff, the snake-handling hand; what was done in youth, in old age one knows shame. Monk Shengmo
said: This verse exposes people's shortcomings and also brings out their strengths. Here Tiantong
and Shengmo censure Yunmen, but today Wansong overturns the verdict. Have you not seen
Tiantong's verse on the snake-handling story, praising Yunmen's complete pivot and great functioning, a cloud in which one does not
descend? Why is it so? To suppress or raise is all in me; who else controls killing and giving life?

cb:div.head: Case Twenty-Two: Yantou Bows to the Shout
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: People probe with words, water is tested with a staff. Parting grass to watch the wind is an everyday
tool. But if suddenly a scorched-tail tiger leaps out, then what?

Raised: Yantou went to Deshan. Straddling the threshold, he immediately asked, Is it ordinary or holy? ShanThis thief
immediately shouted. Tou bowed. Dongshan heard of it and said, If not for Huo-Splitting the skull openNot exactly goodhearted
gong, it would have been hard to bear. Tou said, That old fellow Dongshan does not know good from bad.Rich gifts and sweet words
At that time I raised with one hand and pressed with the other.Yet again he gets flusteredHow could I not know

The master said: Deshan ordinarily struck like wind and rain. One day Yantou spread out his sitting cloth.
Deshan hooked it down the steps with his staff. Tou went down, gathered the cloth, and left.
The next day he went up to attend on Deshan. Shan said, Where did you learn this empty-headedness?
Tou said, I would never dare deceive myself. Shan said, Later on, toward this old monk,
you may shit on my head. When a father hears his son is strong, he regrets only that he cannot kill himself. When one sees beyond the teacher,
only then is one fit to receive transmission. This question is what the various houses call the threshold-crossing device. It may not be that he truly
asked it right at the threshold in the beginning. Formerly there was a non-Buddhist who hid a live sparrow in his hand and asked
the World-Honored One: Is the sparrow in my hand alive or dead? The World-Honored One crossed the threshold with his foot
and said, You tell me, do I wish to go out or to come in? So to ask ordinary or holy truly has this
principle. Formerly Puhua once pointed to a monk-image and asked Linji, Tell me, is this one
ordinary or holy? Ji immediately shouted. Hua said, Heyang's new bride, Muta's old woman
Chan! This little rascal Linji yet has one eye. Ji said, This old thief!
Hua went out of the monks' hall shouting, Thief! Thief! Shoushan said, Among these two thieves there is one true
thief. Tell me, which one is the true thief? In his place I say: Liu Penzi. Yantou asked,
Is it ordinary or holy? Deshan immediately shouted. This is discussing and comparing with Linji, examining how alike they are.
Tou bowed; this too is like Linji, the little rascal who yet had one
eye. Xuedou said: The moment he bowed, he should have struck him across the back. Not only
would this have cut off Dongshan, it would also have pinned down old Huo. This again matches Linji saying, This
old thief is a fellow practitioner. Dongshan heard it raised and said, If not for Huogong, it would have been hard to bear.
Foguo added a comment: The bystander has eyes. He also said: He knows only one, not
the second. Wansong's view is not so. Foguo said: Dongshan, as bystander,
though he has eyes, sees only the awl-tip's sharpness. Wansong said: Though Master Foguo has
eyes, he does not see the chisel-head's squareness. Dongshan deliberately used bearing-it to frame Yantou, wanting
to reveal that Yantou's original bow had both expedient and real. Sure enough, when the fire reached Yantou's head,
he hurriedly scattered it off, saying, That old fellow Dongshan does not know good from bad. At that time I
raised with one hand and pressed with the other. Only then do we light the lamp and eat rice. The two houses are now clearly distinct.
Have you not seen Master Yong of Baoning's verse: Across the level plain a hare runs, and the hawk is loosed. This
praises the threshold question. One press and he pecks out both eyes. This praises Deshan's
shout and Yantou's bow. With a poisonous hand he snatches it and someone buys it off. This praises Dongshan setting a price.
Alas, the weight and measure are not yet clear. This is exactly what Wansong says. If you want the weight
and measure made clear, in truth Foguo and Wansong are not contending over whose head is higher or lower. Rather
look at Tiantong placing it on the scale. He praised it, saying:

lg.l:
The thrusting device comes, all holding authority; in affairs there is the majesty that must be carried out.When wind moves, the grass bendsWhen the warrant arrives, it is carried outEven the Buddha hand cannot block it
at all
In the state there are commands not to be violated; the guest still offers service while the host is proud.Who dares face it head-onBelow uses satire to admonish above

The ruler shuns remonstrance and the minister flatters. As for the underlying meaning of Yantou questioning Deshan,Above uses influence to transform belowThough they are father and son
they raise troops
one raise and one press reveal the mind's activity.They still cannot avoid meeting with weapons drawn

cb:div.p:
The master said: In this verse, all three men thrust in with devices and each holds authority. Only Yantou
and Deshan have the majesty that must be carried out and commands not to be violated. Guest serving, host proud, lies in
Dongshan and Yantou. Ruler avoiding remonstrance, minister flattering, lies in Yantou and Deshan. The last two lines
lie in Dongshan and Yantou. This model the various houses call picking apart old cases. In water,
to separate milk one must be a goose-king. Then you see Tiantong's needle-and-thread workmanship. Yantou knew
Deshan had the majesty that must act and commands not to be violated, so he did not snatch the device, but for the moment followed the situation and bowed.
Dongshan knew Yantou would accept neither probing and pulling nor censure and punishment. The son inherits the father's
work; he too had majesty that must act and commands not to be violated. So he set the bait on the hook and said, If not
for Huogong, it would have been hard to bear. Is this not guest serving host, ruler shunning remonstrance, minister flattering?
In the Linji school's style, this is called a red-cord snare, a jade-green pitfall, a device
