a rare meeting of adepts. These two old ones, one releasing and one taking away, each had a road of emergence.
Master Ding of Gaoyou was once asked, What does the golden scale that passes through the net feed on? You said:
A dry dung stick. My late teacher Xueyan heard this and said: Thanks for the offering. This Dharma-joy and Chan-delight
does not fall short of the ancients. But on Tiantong’s side, how is it? The verse says:

lg.l:
The wave-steps first rise; clouds and thunder escort him. Leaping and soaring, sharp-edged and bold, behold the great function.Resenting that heaven cannot be reached.
Quickly bow three times.

Burning tail, clearly he crosses Yu Gate; this splendid-scaled one is not willing to be pickled in a brine jar.Hurry and look carefully.
There is also Marquis Hei.

The seasoned elder does not startle the crowd; long used to facing great foes, from the start he has noProperly and steadily, secure and settled.
fear.Receiving humiliation as honor, regarding death as life.

Drifting lightly, it seems no more than five liang in weight; piled up, how is it onlySeen from afar, not clear.
a thousand jun heavy?Seen up close, distinct.

High fame fills the four seas; who again is his equal? Standing firm, the eight windsPicking the moon from the sky.
cannot blow him.Just as if never

cb:div.p:
The master said: Dragon Gate Mountain in Jiangzhou was cut open by Yu, and is also called Yu Gate. It has three
steps. The Water Classic says: sturgeon and fish come from the Gong caves, and in the third month they ascend and cross Dragon Gate. If they succeed in crossing, they
become dragons; if not, they strike their foreheads and return. Wave-steps first rising means the three-tiered waves.
The Commentary on the Yi says: Clouds follow the dragon, wind follows the tiger. Clouds and thunder escort him and he becomes a dragon.
These two great adepts leap with majestic power. Sansheng is like the wave-steps first rising; Xuefeng is like
clouds and thunder escorting. Since Sansheng has already crossed Yu Gate, would Xuefeng let him be pickled in a brine jar? Lin
ji, when seeing Luopu off, said: Under Linji there is a red-tailed carp who shakes his head
and swings his tail as he heads south. In whose brine jar will he be pickled to death? Below this
it is precisely praising Xuefeng’s old monk being busy with abbacy affairs, and also praising Sansheng’s two questions. The seasoned
elder does not alarm the assembly; long accustomed to facing great enemies, he has no fear at the outset. In the Annals of Guangwu, Wang Xun and
Wang Yi had troops numbering in the hundreds of thousands and advanced to besiege Kunyang. Guangwu personally led the vanguard.
He killed several dozen men. The generals were all delighted and said: General Liu, in ordinary times when facing small enemies,
was timid, yet today facing a great enemy he is brave. Very strange indeed. At first glance it is not even five liang,
but examined closely and carefully, on a thousand-jin scale it cannot be budged. Later, under Xuefeng’s gate
emerged the Yunmen and Fayan two branches. Is this not because the source was deep and the stream long? Gain and decline,
blame and praise, acclaim and ridicule, suffering and joy these are the eight winds. To a true ancestral master, they are like
wind passing by the ear. Master Xing of Tanzhe said to Master Yi of Qingshou: If I call you
an old man, then what?

cb:div.head: Case Thirty-Four: Fengxue’s One Dust
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Bare hands and empty fists, with a thousand transformations and ten thousand changes though this is making something from nothing,
how can one play false and make it seem real? Tell me, is there still any foundation or not?

Raised: Fengxue offered these words, saying: If one establishes a single dust, the nation and household flourish;Gaining it, it was originally there.
if one does not establish a single dust, the nation and household perish. Xuedou lifted his staff and said:Losing it, it was originally absent.Is it established or not established?
Is there any patched-robed monk who lives and dies together with this?Not saying there is none, only that it is few.

The master said: Xuedou raised the staff, and his intent was at the point of establishing dust. The verse says: The old rustic, from
start to finish, does not unknit his brow, yet he still plans to set a heroic foundation for house and state. Clever ministers and fierce generals now where
are they? This verse praises, Is there a patched-robed monk who can live and die together with this? The clear wind for ten thousand li
only knows itself. The old rustic does not unknit his brow this saying was not fully quoted. In the record, Fengxue
ascended the hall and said: If one establishes a single dust, house and state flourish, and the old rustic frowns. If one does not establish
a single dust, house and state perish, and the old rustic is at ease. If you understand this, you monks have no
share it is entirely the old monk. If you do not understand this, then the old monk is just you monks. You monks
and the old monk can also make all people in the world awaken, and can also make all people in the world be deluded.
Do you want to know the monks? He struck once on the left and said: Right here. Do you want to know the old
monk? He struck once on the right and said: Right here. Yunmen said: Here is
easy; there is hard. Langya Jue said: Ladle divination, listening to empty sounds. Wansong
said: Yunmen adds a point to an arrow-tip; Langya pulls a wedge from the back of the head. This too is the establishing and abolishing of one dust,
the rise and fall of house and state. Yet in truth, the tree at the center has never moved in the least.
Xuedou, in the gate of Buddha-work, does not abandon a single dharma. Tiantong, together with the principle of ultimate reality,
does not accept a single dust. The two dharmas proceed together, and he lifts them both out at once. The verse says:

lg.l: White-haired by the Wei River, he rises and lets down his fishing line how can he compare to the pure starving men of Shouyang?Old and still the mind does not rest.Young and not striving.

It is only in one dust that transformations divide high fame and meritorious achievement are both hard to match.Lifting the staff, he says: Look.
Setting down the staff, he says: Xuedou is still here.

cb:div.p:
The master said: When the Earl of the West was about to go hunting, he divined and it said: What you will obtain is no bear, no grizzly,
no leopard, no tiger, but the assistant of a king. Indeed he found Lü Shang on the north bank of the Wei River.
Speaking with him, he was greatly delighted and said: My former lord, the Grand Duke, once said that a sage would
come to Zhou. My Grand Duke has long looked for you. Therefore he was called Grand Duke Wang, and was established as teacher
and adviser. Boyi and Shuqi were the two sons of the Lord of Guzhu. Yielding the state, both departed. When King Wu attacked
Zhou, they struck the horses and remonstrated, saying: Your father has died and is not yet buried, yet you take up spear and shield can this be called filial?
And to have a minister slay his lord can this be called benevolent? Those at his sides wanted to seize them, but the Grand Duke
said: These are righteous men. Support them and let them go. King Wu pacified Yin, and all under heaven honored
Zhou. Boyi and Shuqi were ashamed of it, would not eat Zhou grain, gathered vetch on Shouyang, and starved to death. The Grand Duke
helped conquer Yin and establish Zhou this is the flourishing of house and state. Boyi and Shuqi yielded the throne and starved to death this is
the perishing of house and state. National Teacher Xianshou, simply by establishing one dust and its transformations, spoke of the hundred
gates and the sea of meanings. High fame belongs to Boyi and Shuqi; meritorious achievement belongs to the Grand Duke. Luopu said: Before the old rustic’s
gate one does not speak of affairs of court; therefore he rests at ease with farming and mulberry and has never once furrowed his brow.
Why is that? Where there is no use, true use is accomplished. Good conditions are bad conditions.

cb:div.head: Case Thirty-Five: Luopu Submits
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Swift function and quick eloquence can break the assaults of outsiders and heavenly demons. A style beyond standards and above the schools
bends itself for those of highest faculties and sharp wisdom. But if one suddenly meets a fellow who, with one blow, will not turn his head back,
what then?

Raised: Luopu visited Jiashan. Without bowing, he stood face to face. Jiashan said: A chicken roosting in a phoenix nest is not of the same kind get out.Meeting each other without dismounting, each has his own
road ahead.One hand pushes, one hand pulls.
Pu said: I have come from afar, drawn by your wind; I beg the master for a single reception. Shan said: Before meThe probing pole is in hand.
there is no monk. Here there is no old monk. Pu then gave a shout. Shan said:The decoy grass follows at the body.Exhausting sinew and cutting off strength.
Stop, stop, do not be so hasty and rushed. Clouds and moon are the same, but streams andOne who understands is not hurried; one who is hurried does not understand.
mountains differ. Cutting off the tongues of everyone under heaven is not impossible but how will you make a tongueless man speak? Pu was speechless. Shan then struck him. From then on Pu submitted.In slanting streets and dark alleys, a newcomer loses his head.Only seeing
the awl tip sharp,not seeing the chisel head square.Before the long-snake formation, the bow-tip strikes
the ground.Unexpectedly, Jiashan instead acts like Linji.Skill overwhelms the whole profession.

The master said: The various Patriarch Lamp records all say that before Jiashan met the Boatman, he had already come out
into the world and lived at Bamboo Grove in Jingkou, Runzhou, but they do not record the name of his Dharma heir. Only Foguo
Foguo clapped in praise and said: Chuanming at first inherited from Shilou that is Shilou of Fenzhou. Chuan
ming is Jiashan’s posthumous title. Chan Master Yuan’an of Luopu Mountain in Lizhou long studied under
Linji and served as his attendant. Linji once said: Under Linji there is a single arrow who dares
face its point? One day he took leave of Linji. Linji asked: Where are you going? He said: To the south. Linji drew his staff
and made one stroke, saying: If you can get past this, then go. Pu then shouted; Linji immediately struck him. Pu
He made his bows and departed. After finishing his travels, he came to the summit of Mount Jia and set up a hut there for a year.
Jia-shan, knowing of this, sent an attendant monk with a letter in haste. When Pu received it, he just sat there, then held out his hand again
asking for more. The monk had no reply. Pu struck him and said, Go back and report this to master. The monk returned and
told him. Shan said, If this monk reads the letter, he will surely come within three days. If he does not read it, he cannot
be saved. Three days later Pu came. He did not bow, but stood face to face. Shan said,
A chicken roosts in a phoenix nest; if it is not of the same kind, get out. Each of us bears eyes that part the grass and watch the wind.
Pu saw that although Jia-shan sent him away, it was really to keep him. Since he had come, how could he return empty-handed? He also saw
that their styles at the gate were both steep and hard, neither yielding. So he used a soft tactic and went along with him, saying, From afar I have hastened
after the wind, begging the master for one reception. Shan had another whole set of bellows and furnace. He said, Before my eyes there is no acharya;
in this place there is no old monk. Shan knew that a long-practiced one would surely use Linji’s proper command, and then
he used a hammer and tongs beyond the school and beyond convention. Pu indeed immediately gave a shout. Tell me then,
besides this, was there yet something else? Shan said, Stop, stop, do not be hasty and rushed.
No need to hurry. Clouds and moon are the same; streams and mountains differ. The same flour and grass are made by people
into whatever they make. To cut off the tongues of everyone under heaven is not impossible. But with only a thousand-foot cold
pine, how can you make a tongueless man speak? You still need to draw out a stone shoot. Shan once
had a saying: speaking of the gate and its devices, and deep discourse entering principle. Pu was gate and devices;
Shan was deep discourse entering principle. Pu, greedy to watch the white waves, lost his oar in hand and in the end could not make it fit.
Shan was good too, yet he took Linji’s proper command and used it as a raw, stiff key
to fit an old lock. Pu had long eaten strong household vinegar and knew its sourness, so he submitted. Xinghua said,
Just know how to become Buddha; why worry about sentient beings? Wansong said, But how can a single
tree make a forest? Xuedou said, This monk is pitiable and lamentable; he dulled and bogged down Linji.
Wansong said, A foster son does not equal the father; the family gate declines in one generation. Since he said clouds
and moon are the same, I too say streams and mountains differ. Wansong said, The autumn colors of South Mountain rise in force;
their vigor towers. Why speak of the tongueless man not being able to speak? Wansong said, He is still only a functionary
handling affairs. His sitting mat struck him across the mouth. Wansong said, Caught by him, he got a rotten beating
all at once. Then what? Jia-shan was a man who knew the square and rule; surely beneath the bright window
he had arranged it all beforehand. Wansong said, Better return him his proper fodder. Wuzu Jie said,
If you speak more reason, see, I will leave at once. Wansong said, The nature and spirit of snakes and scorpions
are poisonous at birth. Dayang Yan said, Still, master must certify it. Wansong said, The fragrance of iris and orchid
grows old yet remains fragrant. The master again said, Yaoshan’s lineage is truly hard to continue and uphold.
Yunyan swept the ground, and dust filled the sky. Luopu submitted, yet kept crying injustice without end. Fortunately
the tongueless can speak and the handless can throw punches. Even if blows and shouts race back and forth, one can only
raise it from the side by half. To support this Way, he entrusted it to Tiantong, who versified:

lg.l:
Shaking head and swishing tail, the red-tailed scaled fish, utterly without reliance, knows how to turn its body.The mouth covets fragrant bait, the body hangs in the net.
Now the eyes are dragged to the bottom of the net.

Cutting off tongues is indeed a skill, but to tug back the nostrilsYou are just sweeping snow looking for pine nuts.
wondrously reaches the divine.I have already split the stick and gotten poria.

Outside the night-bright curtain, wind and moon are like day;Not borrowing the three lights.
Force.

before the dead-tree cliff, flowers and grasses are forever spring. The tongueless man, the tonguelessSecretly dissolving the work of one color.
man,Assenting from inside the nostrils.

with the whole proper command fully raised, one intimate phrase, strides alone through the world,In the dark, pulling out the crossbone.
bright and utterly clear within.True light does not shine forth.
Let all under heaven rejoice in delight as they please.The bustle is over there; what is it to me?

cb:div.p:
The master said: When Luopu took leave of Linji, Linji said, Under the gate of Linji there is a red-
tailed carp, shaking its head and swishing its tail, heading south. Utterly without reliance,
it knows how to turn its body. This is Linji’s own matter; it is not entirely the same as shifting position and shifting function. In the Linji
Record it says: Only the person of no reliance who hears the Dharma is the mother of all Buddhas. Therefore
Buddhas arise from no reliance. If one awakens to no reliance, Buddha too is not attained. If one thus
sees, that is true and proper understanding. Wansong said, If Luopu could not turn his body,
how could he shout at Jia-shan? If you say he did turn his body, why then at the end
was he speechless? Try and judge it. Tiantong grants him eyes and skill, and Jia-shan too
was not without that power to cut off the tongues of all under heaven, skillfully standing on the command. The only issue was making
the tongueless man speak. He teased the nostrils of heaven and lightly tugged them back. Foguo, seeing Jia-
shan’s words seeming like muttered incantations, added a comment saying, Where did you get this whole bundle
of clutter? Wansong said, This itself is it: Jia-shan teaching the tongueless man to speak.
Foguo lacked this bundle; he could only cut off the tongues of all under heaven.
Even if there is another place to turn the body and exhale breath, it may not yet be the speech of a tongueless man. Tell me then,
what is this person’s realm? The verse says: Outside the night-bright curtain, wind and moon
are like day; before the dead-tree cliff, flowers ever spring. This is the tongueless man’s use and enjoyment.
Emperor Ming of Han built the Hall of Brightness, using pearls and gems for curtains, with golden thresholds
and jade steps, bright day and night. Tong’an Cha said, Before the dead-tree cliff there are many branching roads.
Dongshan said, You must sprinkle some flower seeds on the dead tree. This verse says that at the tip of the staff and beneath the shout there is indeed a lonely steepness,
but having reached the top of a hundred-foot pole, one must still take one more step.
Only then does the tongueless man speak. Thus we know: words coming from the tongueless man are precisely
the full raising of the proper command, one intimate and cutting phrase. Here one’s eyes tower over the four seas,
striding alone through the world. Later Luopu said, Let everyone under heaven rejoice as they like.
I alone do not consent. Even if all under heaven willingly let him cut off their tongues,
Jia-shan said, Above this there is yet another opening. What is that higher opening? The
tongueless man speaking, that is what I tell you.

cb:div.head: Case Thirty-Six: Master Ma Is Unwell
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: To investigate apart from mind, thought, and consciousness, there is this. To study beyond the road of ordinary and holy,
that is already too lofty. From the red furnace bursts an iron caltrop; with tongue-sword and lip-spear it is hard to open
the mouth. Without touching the sharp edge, try to bring it forth.

Raised: Master Ma was unwell. Yuanzhu asked, master, these days your honoredNot necessarily like Vimalakīrti.
condition, how is it? The great master said, Sun-Faced Buddha, Moon-Faced Buddha.Temple affairs keep one busy; there is little chance to ask after you.Could it be a cramp?
Or is it cholera?

The master said: The ancients, even while ill, still did Buddha-work. Nanyue Huisi, when a grave illness suddenly
arose, made from that illness a case of causation, contemplating thus: Illness arises from karma; karma arises from
delusion; delusion arises from mind. Mind is originally unborn. From where then does illness come? Having thought thus,
he suddenly recovered. Wansong said, From Tathagata Zen he found a place of peace and ease.
Chan Master Shen of Fengsheng in the Western Capital had a nun, Zongchi, of notable capacity. When illness came she composed a verse,
saying: Breath cut off, all feelings spent; raise an intention and there is no road for intention. Even blinking lacks strength; all year
I do not leave the door. Though this is Patriarchs’ Zen, it is much like an old crow in a cloth sack. Furong
Kai master said, This single verse naturally inherits our school. Wansong
said, Even so, it is already too much; still, there is yet more to it. Master Ma, however,
was not like that. The Yuanzhu did not dare directly ask whether the illness was better or worse; he tactfully asked master
how his honored condition had been recently. He did not speak of Tathagata Zen or Patriarchs’ Zen, but only
said, Sun-Faced Buddha, Moon-Faced Buddha. Tell me, what was his meaning? Foguo
said, Nowadays many people say Master Ma answered the Yuanzhu. Some strain their
eyes and say, Here it is: the left and right eyes are Sun-Faced Buddha and Moon-Faced Buddha. Some
say, Bring some stomach-settling powder. What handle is there in that? Chan Master Shou said, There is not one
name that does not broadcast the Tathagata’s title; there is not one thing that does not reveal Vairocana’s form. Wan-
song said, In the Sutra of Buddha Names there are indeed these two Buddha names. But Master Ma’s intent, after all,
what was it? Have you not heard: Ma’s colt tramples the people of the world to death. Tianjue’s verse says: The colt of Shifang,
fierce in spirit from birth, kicks and tramples as it walks upon Vairocana’s crown. Just when the spleen aches
and the fingers hurt, when illness comes there is still a deft state of mind. Wansong said, Original nature is hard
to change; mountains and rivers are easier altered. This verse says that though Mazu was in sick leave, he still used the fundamental matter
to help people. Our own physical bodies are strong and healthy; by no means should we fail Mazu and treat
Tiantong carelessly. The verse says:

lg.l:
Sun-face, moon-face, stars streaming, lightning curling; a mirror faces forms and has no partiality.Look at it and you go blind at once.Already past Silla.
A single point is hard to conceal.

A pearl in a tray turns of itself. Do you not see? Before hammer and tongs, the hundred-It cannot be seized or caught.
refined gold;Bottles, basins, hairpins, bracelets, bowls, and trays.
beneath knife and shears, the one-bolt silk.Quilts and covers, robes and caps, lapels, collars, and sleeves.

cb:div.p:
The master said: These two Buddhas, Sun-Faced and Moon-Faced, are like stars streaming and lightning curling, leaving no room for deliberation.
Long ago, the Qin palace used jade as mirrors. When the officials were reflected, their liver, gall, and organs all appeared.
And when foxes transformed into people, in the mirror only their true form appeared. This is concealment without partiality. Things of like kind
respond to each other. At Langfeng Ford a pearl emerged; placed in a vessel, it turned by itself. This is called a rolling
pearl. This verse praises Mazu’s mind as an ancient mirror, his function like a rolling pearl, leaving no trace or shadow.
Hundred-refined gold belongs under the hammer and tongs of a master craftsman; one-bolt silk belongs under the skillful
knife and shears. A monk asked Yunyan, The person of great assurance and that one, are they one
or two? Yan said, One-bolt silk. Is it one piece or two pieces? Dongshan
answered in his stead, Like a person grafting wood. This is where circumstance and spirit meet, wisdom and principle merge in darkness. Heaven
and water share one autumn; ruler and minister are in accord with the Way. If silk gets knife and shears, cutting is left to the person; if gold
gets hammer and tongs, tempering lies in oneself. Tell me, on the patch-robed monk’s side, what thing is accomplished?
What sort of matter is it? Sun-Faced Buddha, Moon-Faced Buddha.

cb:div.head: Case Thirty-Seven: Weishan on Karmic Consciousness
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Drive off the plowman’s ox and yank back its nose-ring; snatch food from a hungry man.
Clamp his throat tight. Is there anyone who can lay on such a poisonous hand?

Raised: Weishan asked Yangshan, Suppose someone suddenly asks: All sentient beings have only
karmic consciousness, vast and dim, with no basis to rely on. How would you test him?The horse is an official horse, no brand needed.
Yang said, If a monk comes, I immediately call out, Monk so-and-so. When he turns hisA blow behind the head, and he does not know where it came from.
head, I then say, What is it? If he starts to deliberate, I tell him, Not only is karmic consciousness vast and dim, it also has no basis to rely on.Up through the crown-gate go the three souls.While the stove is hot, give another blow.The soles of the feet
are pierced through to the seven spirits.
Wei said, Excellent.Seized alive and captured living.Bitter words come from true concern.

The master cited: A monk asked Yun’an, The Huayan Treatise says that the afflictions of fundamental ignorance
are precisely the unmoving wisdom of all Buddhas. The principle is extremely deep and mysterious, very hard to understand. An
said, This is the clearest and easiest to understand. At that time a boy was sweeping. He called
to him, and the boy turned his head. An pointed and said, Is this not unmoving wisdom? When Yangshan called the monk and he turned his head,
it was exactly this moment. Yet Yun’an then asked, What is your Buddha-nature? The boy
looked left and right in confusion and went away. An said, Is this not the affliction abiding at the ground? If one can
understand it, one becomes Buddha right now. The boy’s confusion is no different from deliberation. Fundamental ignorance,
ground-abiding affliction, and karmic consciousness vast and dim are the same. Yun’an and Yangshan examined monks and tested
people, exact like this. Wansong’s own view is otherwise: the boy and the monk, through and through,
are both unmoving wisdom. Yun’an and Yangqiao, from the very start, are karmic consciousness vast and dim. If a person
can discern this, he meets Tiantong face to face. The verse says:

lg.l:
Called once, he turns his head. Do you know me or not? Dim through gauze, the moon again becomes a hook.Truly plain as day; how hard is it to spot the thief?
Hiding the body, exposing the shadow.

The son of a thousand in gold has only just fallen into exile; on the vast and desolate poor road there isThough the wind shatters it, the frame still remains.
such sorrow.A small vessel lacks great capacity.

cb:div.p:
The master cited: Baizhang ascended the hall. As the assembly was gathering, he struck with his staff all at once, then
called the great assembly. The assembly turned their heads. Zhang said, What is it? Various places call this Baizhang’s
down-from-the-hall line; it is worth studying. Wang Jinggong said, I gained one phrase from Xuefeng
and became prime minister. People pressed him for more, and he said, That old fellow always tells people,
What is it? This line of the verse praises calling the monk and his turning his head, together with What is it? Know me
or not? The word not here is read as fu, meaning not. The intent is, Do you know me or not? Yangshan
used the hand of no meaning and struck an unguarded household. If this monk had, in the flash of flint-fire, caught it at once,
it could be called recognizing the Son of Heaven in a bustling market. But if he starts deliberating and cannot come forth,
then the dim moon through gauze again becomes a hook. Huangbo ascended the hall as the assembly gathered, and with his
staff struck all at once, then again called the assembly. The assembly turned their heads. Bo said, The moon is like a bent
bow, little rain and much wind. The verse’s meaning uses this. Stone Chamber Shandao, together with Yangshan, was admiring the
moon. Yang asked, When the moon is pointed, where does its round form go? When it is round, where does the pointed form
Where does it go? Shishi said, When pointed, the round aspect is hidden; when round, the pointed aspect remains. Yun
yan said, When pointed, the round aspect is present; when round, the pointed aspect is absent. Daowu said, When pointed,
it is also not pointed; when round, it is also not round. Made into a hook, it is a pointed form. In Luo Binwang's poem:
Since it can be round like a mirror, what use is being curved like a hook? The Huayan school calls this the gate where hidden and manifest
are both accomplished together in secret. The teachings also say the bodhisattvas of the ten stages see nature as though through gauze,
like viewing the moon through thin silk. If one reads it as vine-moon, that also works. And in Li Bai's poem there is, a vine-moon hangs
on the morning mirror; pine wind sounds on the night strings. The meaning of vine is rich. Tiantong uses the dim new
moon, half veiled by misty vines. Though not full and bright, it already shows its edges. The verse brings out this monk's
half-bright, half-dark state, as if present, as if absent. Wansong is very much like an official judge of salt and iron.
Truly, Tiantong's stitching is deep and fine. If the thread-end does not remain unbroken, it is hard to weave
the pattern of brocade. Elder Mi was walking with Dongshan, and they saw a white rabbit pass by. Mi
said, Splendid! Shan said, How so? Mi said, Like a commoner appointed minister. Shan
said, So old and yet you speak like this. Mi said, Then how would you put it?
Shan said, A house of generations in caps and ornaments, temporarily fallen into poverty. In Sima Xiangru's Shanglin Fu:
The son of wealth does not sit beneath a crumbling hall. Ruan Ji often rode a firewood cart, and when he met a dead end
on the road, he would weep bitterly and return. Wansong says, If one can return home right on the road, then one gains
turning the body and going back to the father. Have you not heard: with one thought turning the light around, one is at once the same as original attainment. Since
it is so, why is it that the unmoving wisdom of all buddhas, when it comes to the share of all beings,
is called vast and dim karmic consciousness? Had you known earlier that the lamp was fire, the rice would have been cooked long ago.

cb:div.head: Case Thirty-Eight: Linji's True Person
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: Taking a thief for your son, recognizing a servant as your heir, how could a broken wooden ladle be your ancestors'
skull? A donkey saddle is not your father's jaw either. When land is divided and fiefs bestowed,
how do you distinguish the true master?

Citation. Linji addressed the assembly, saying: There is one true person of no rank who alwaysThe foundation is set and the footing fixed.
goes in and out through the gate of your face. Those who have not yet verified it, look, look.What about the one behind your back?
At that time a monk asked, What is the true person of no rank? Ji got down fromDo you yet have eyes?Do you yet understand speech?
the meditation seat, seized the monk. As the monk hesitated, Ji pushed him away and said, The true person of noAnd you still evade it.You blunt and obstruct that true person.
rank, what kind of dried shit-stick is that?It is just like being unable to hold the bowl.

The master said: In Linji's fuller discourse he says, Within the field of the five-skandha body there is a true person of no rank.
Openly and plainly revealed, without even a hair's breadth of separation. Why not recognize it? The mind-dharma has no
form; it penetrates throughout the ten directions. Since it is said to penetrate the ten directions, it is not limited to the five-skandha body
or to coming and going through the face-gate. Those who have not verified it, look. Wansong says: The true person of no
rank looks at the monks; the monks look at the true person. Then a monk asked, What
is the true person of no rank? Various places call this harmonizing the voice and carrying things along. But how can the rider of the donkey
fail to see the one sitting below? Linji got down from the seat and seized him. Tell me, where was the true person
then? He deserved a slap. The monk hesitated, and the true person was no longer there. What a pity.
Linji shoved him away and said, The true person of no rank, what dried shit-stick is that? Right before his face it was hidden,
yet he turned away. Xuefeng said, Linji is very much like a bare-handed thief. Wansong says, He is exposed.
Xuedou said, A truly skilled thief is unknown even to ghosts and spirits. Since Xuefeng saw through him,
Linji was not a good hand. Then he called to the assembly again: Today Xuedou has changed all your
eyes for you. If you do not believe it, each go back to your quarters and feel around
and see. Wansong says, Xuedou lost his eyebrows too. If you would learn to play thief in the thief's own hands,
you must ask Tiantong. The verse says:

lg.l:
Delusion and awakening turn back upon each other, subtle transmission yet simple; spring splits a hundred flowers, and with oneNot separated by even a hair.Already stained by dust and smoke.
blowLetting it go is more perilous.

strength turns nine oxen, and with one pull; yet there is no help for mud and sand that cannot bePulling it back is too hasty.
clearedMy eye was originally straight.

Clearly blocking off the sweet spring's source, then suddenly bursting forth and wildlyBecause of the teacher, it turned crooked.
flowing.If he overturns the Chan seat, no wonder.

cb:div.p: The master added: Dangerous.He throws down his staff and says, I let this move pass.

The master said: The Complete Enlightenment Sutra says, It is like a confused man who changes places in the four directions, though the directions themselves
never moved. At awakening too, it is just as before. The Mirror of the School says, From the start delusion and awakening were like delusion;
today awakening to delusion is not awakening. This is truly subtle transmission and simplicity, beneficent without cost. If one realizes it,
then this lump of red flesh is precisely the true person of no rank. If one does not realize it, the true person still
goes in and out through the face-gate as before. This monk also had it; he simply could not bring it forth or use it.
Instead he made himself the true person's servant, passing messages, delivering words, asking this and that, troubling Linji
to lower himself and meet him directly, using the whole body. Since the monk could not respond in time, Linji
also withdrew and did not look back. Seeing the spoon could not ladle it up, he then said, The true person of no
rank, what dried shit-stick is that? Here he could release and gather, call and they assembled, shout and they scattered.
He never tied things down dead under a phrase and made a sickness in people's chests. Tiantai says:
Blow once and a world is formed; shout once and a world is destroyed. It further says: When it is time to blow, then shout; when
it is time to shout, then blow. In Liezi, Gongyizi was famed for strength.
King Xuan of Zhou invited him with full ceremony. When he arrived, he looked timid. The king asked, Your strength,
what is it like? He replied, I can break the leg of a spring insect and bear the wing of an autumn cicada. The king
changed color and said, My strength can tear rhinoceros hide and drag the tails of nine oxen, and I still
regret that it is weak. How is it that you are famed for strength being as you are? He replied, My reputation
does not come from bearing strength, but from being able to use strength. This verse praises Linji's
power in gathering and releasing. Fayan opened a well, but sand blocked the spring-source. He asked a monk, The spring-source
is blocked by sand and does not flow. If the eye of the Way does not penetrate, by what thing is it obstructed? The monk had no reply.
He answered for him: Obstructed by the eye. Tell me then, was it this monk who blocked the spring-source, or was Linji
the one who blocked the spring-source? When it suddenly bursts forth, what then? The master lifted his staff and came down
from the seat. The whole assembly scattered at once.

cb:div.head: Case Thirty-Nine: Zhaozhou Washes the Bowl
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: When food comes, open your mouth; when sleep comes, close your eyes. At the place of washing your face, if you pick up your nose,
and when putting on shoes, touch your heel, yet at that moment you miss the word-head, then carrying fire at night
and searching elsewhere in the dark, how will you ever come into accord?

Citation. A monk asked Zhaozhou, A student has just entered the monastery and asks for instruction. Zhou said, Have you eaten your gruel yet? The monk said, I have eaten. Zhou said, Go wash your bowl.The monastery grove
is not bad for you either.Solid gold, uncut jade.Long accustomed to patch-robed monks.
Not equal to the senior monk.Do not guess sideways.

The master said: Fishing for a dragon with a straight hook is already not skillful. When the lacquer bucket is still three inches from the hook, it is already
the boatman and Jiashan cutting it off. It is not that people today have no share in it; mostly they just greedily take the bait
and swallow the hook. Look, Zhaozhou neither snapped the fishing rod nor overturned the boat;
he sat at ease on the stone bridge, and when passing by the little footbridge, there were naturally those who came ashore and fell
into his hands. In the original record, that monk is said to have awakened on this account. It can be said the thread on the rod-tip
was yours to play with; without disturbing the clear waves, the meaning was naturally distinct. Zhaozhou, like Ren Gong, found success
in front; now watch Tiantong beat the oar behind in the verse:

lg.l: After the gruel, he orders him to wash the bowl; suddenly the ground of mind accords of itself.Such a swift opening is hard to meet.Not only today.

Now, you monastery guests stuffed full of meditation, say then, within this is there awakeningAs always, after eating porridge, wash your bowl and go.
or not?One person passes on hearsay, ten thousand pass it on as fact.

cb:div.p:
The master said: Lingyun saw peach blossoms and awakened to the Way, then presented a verse to Weishan. Shan said,
One who enters from conditions will never retreat or lose it. Xuansha heard this and said, Truly right, very truly right.
Yet I dare guarantee our elder brother has not fully penetrated. Lingyun heard and said, Has master fully penetrated or not? Sha said,
Only thus is it so. Tiantong's verse praises this monk's awakening, the mind-ground mutually matching. This monk,
newly entering the monastery, is called greatly awakened, greatly penetrated. You well-fed Chan guests of the monastery, tell me: is there
awakening or no awakening? This is called a probing question. Xuedou said, Originally there is no fixed count of delusion and awakening as many as hemp;
only Lingyun is allowed to be a master hand. Xuansha said not yet through, yet Xuedou alone approved him as
a master hand. Xu Liu carrying a plank, each sees only one side. Tell me, in washing the bowl, did the monk
awaken or not? Peace is originally brought by the general, but the general is not permitted to see the peace
he brought.

cb:div.head: Case Fortieth: Yunmen White and Black
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: Where the pivot-wheel turns, even the eye of wisdom is still confused. When the jeweled mirror opens, not a mote of dust
passes through. When the fist opens, it does not fall to the ground. When responding to things and knowing the right time, when two blades meet,
how do they turn and interpenetrate?

Citation. Yunmen asked Qianfeng, Please, Master, answer a saying. Feng said, Even old monk has not yet arrived.Empty-headed, submerged to the crown.
Men said, Then I am late.I already answered you long ago.
Feng said, So then, so then. Men said, I had thoughtYielding leaves more than enough.Above all, do not understand it this way.
there was only Hou Bai, but there is also a Hou Hei.In the hands of a master, there is no masterly hand.

The master said: King Milinda asked the venerable Nagasena, I am about to put a question; can the master answer?
Nagasena said, Please let the king ask. The king said, I have already asked. Nagasena said, I
have already answered. The king said, What did the master answer? Nagasena said, What did the king ask? The king
said, I asked nothing. Nagasena said, I answered nothing. This can still be investigated.
Yunmen's question was like lightning flashing in a clear sky. Qianfeng's answer was like thunder running over dry ground.
When it comes to both releasing and both gathering, then one sees head and tail. Therefore it is not something
ordinary patch-robed monks can know; unless one is a master hand, one cannot see it. Master Tiantong penetrated deeply into this gate.
The verse says:

lg.l:
Bowstring and nock answering each other, net-jewels facing one another; a hundred shots, and every arrowHigh and low are answered alike.Left and right both meet the source.
does not miss.In exchange and expression, there is a standard.

Gathering all scenes, every light is unobstructed; obtaining the dharani of words and phrases,Alone it shines, without partiality.
total retention.When words come out, they form patterns.

Abiding in the samadhi of play, wondrous within it, turning and shifting,Every motion matches the beat.
partial and complete.Like pearls rolling on a tray.
It must be thus, free in every direction.Watch when the command is carried out.

cb:div.p:
The master said: Once missed on the string, it must still be released. This verse praises the sharpness of Yunmen's question,
whose edge cannot be touched. Net-jewels facing one another praises Qianfeng's answer, where host and guest interweave.
The question is in the answer; the answer is in the question. A hundred shots, a hundred hits, praising Yunmen's I
am late. Zhijue said, If a man shoots at the earth, there is no principle by which he can miss. Interlaced
lights net each other, and every thing is unobstructed. This praises Qianfeng's so then. In the Huayan commentary, Indra's
palace is strung with pearls into a net; lights and reflections appear in one another, layer upon layer without end. This verse gives
the general meaning of the koan; there is no need to force every line to match point by point. Yunmen said,
I had thought there was Hou Bai, but there is also Hou Hei. In the Sui dynasty there was Hou Bai, styled Junsu, a witty
and eloquent man. The great general Yang Su recognized him. He compiled the Jingyi Ji.
Its accounts of human and spirit retribution are very detailed and worthy. In the Tang there was Li Bai, skilled in poetry.
Later there was Li Chi who imitated him, but was nothing like him, and people laughed. So now to say Hou
Hei is of that kind too. Some editions say, I was already Hou Bai, and he is even more Hou Hei.
Meaning, even more so. Dharani has three kinds: many-word, one-word, and no-word. Dharani is to retain all
dharma-gates. Samadhi is right concentration. Tiantong's partial and complete still refer to phenomena and principle. Guoshi
said, Principle is complete; words are partial. When words arise, principle is lost. Tiantai's Calming and Contemplation says:
The three dots of the rounded syllable are not vertical like drops of water, nor horizontal like a row of fire. Also,
to extend vertically through the three times is called lofty; to spread horizontally through the ten directions is called vast. Therefore the Lotus says, its
carriage is lofty and broad. Tiantong ranges through the ocean of teaching and penetrates the sky of meaning. Yunmen and Qianfeng
set up a wordless stele; Tiantong sings and chants it into a poem without words. It may be said, as Yang Xiu saw
the young wife, one glance and he knew the subtlety.

cb:div.head: Case Forty-One: Luopu at the End of Life
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: At times one is loyal and sincere, knocking in bitterness, with injustice hard to voice. At times calamity reaches him,
and before others he cannot bear it. On the verge of departure he collapses cheaply; at the very end he is most earnest, tears
coming from the painful bowels, harder still to conceal. Is there anyone here with a cool eye?

Citation. As Luopu was nearing death, he addressed the assembly, saying: Now there is one matter. I ask all of you.
If this is so, then it is putting a head on top of a head; if it is not so,Still talking of military stratagems.Like this will not do.
Just when cutting off the head to seek life, the head monk said, Green mountains are always lifting their feet.Not like this will not do either.
In broad daylight one does not carry a lamp. Luopu said, What time is this, to makeSpeak it clearly, and it becomes even harder to get out.
such a statement? There was Senior Monk Yancong, who stepped out and said, Leaving aside these two paths,Lose money and incur punishment.
I ask the master not to question them. Luopu said, Not there yet. Speak further.Easy to open one’s mouth at the start and finish; hard to keep one’s resolve through winter’s cold.Only when a poem is chanted again and again does its skill appear.
Cong said, I cannot finish speaking it. Luopu said, I do not care whether youNot letting others see it makes it all the more elegant.
finish speaking or not. Cong said, I have no attendant, I onlyLet it go to the bottomless depth—there is no choice but to stop.
respond directly to master. By evening he called Senior Monk Cong: What was your response todayShadow and grass follow the body.
based on? It must accord with and embody the former master's saying: Before the eyes there is no dharma; the meaningHe just keeps practicing sticking his head in.
is in what is before the eyes, yet it is not a dharma before the eyes; it is not reached by ear and eye.Chop down the cassia in the moon; the clear light should be even greater.
Which phrase is guest, and which phrase is host? If you can distinguish them,When the moon sets, we meet.Strictly avoid splitting it into two pieces.
I will entrust you with the robe and bowl. Cong said, I do not understand. Luopu said,Take up a stick and call the dog.Exactly fits the charge.
You ought to understand. Cong said, I truly do not understand. Luopu shouted, Bitter!A mountain of nine rèn is about to be completed,yet you do not add even one basket of earth.
Bitter indeed, bitter indeed! A monk asked, What is the venerable meaning of master? LuopuYou swindle a whole boatload of people to death.Picking up bits of charcoal where there was a fire.
said, The compassionate boat is not rowed upon clear waves; at Sword Gorge, it is vainly laboring to release the wooden goose.Trying to be clever and ending up clumsy.

The master said: At Luopu's death, his grandmotherly concern was too urgent. The head monk poured everything out, yet was instead
reproved for untimeliness. Yancong did not let it pass his lips, and was acknowledged as one who should understand. He was pressed again and again,
dredged and fished for it. Pitifully, he remained buried all along. Kebin willingly paid the penalty of meal money; the blind donkey
thus extinguished the true Dharma eye. Xuanzhao said: Tell me, did Senior Monk Cong truly not understand, or
was he afraid the robe and bowl would stick to him? Therefore the Lamp Record includes Cong among the line
of Dharma heirs. Luopu once instructed the assembly, One must clarify the school outside the intent; do not from words seek
rules. A monk asked, What is it like to act in the inconceivable place? Luopu said, Green mountains are always lifting their feet.
In broad daylight the sun-wheel does not move. Judged by this, the head monk and Master Cong are clearly seen.
On Luopu's side, was there anyone to gather up what came later, or not? A hundred years later there was indeed
Tiantong, who composed a verse, saying:

lg.l:
With cloud for bait and moon for hook, he fishes the clear ford; old in years, solitary in heart, he has not yet caughtWithout entering startling waves, it is hard to find a fish to your liking.
a scaled one.Why the panic?

After one song of the Li Sao and going back, on the Miluo River he alone remained awake,Where is it?
that lone awakened man.Luopu is still here.

cb:div.p:
The master said: The ancients took the long rainbow as rod, the new moon as hook, and broken clouds as bait.
At the clear ford one may row the compassionate boat; at Sword Gorge one must first release the wooden goose. Hangzhou's Wuyun
master's Meditation Admonition says: At the flowing Sword Pavilion no wooden goose may linger. For the sword-
waters are dangerous, narrow, and swift; if two boats collide they must shatter. Therefore one first cuts wood and lets it float
downstream; this is called a wooden goose. Various explanations in the monasteries are hard to trust; the Meditation Admonition is the best
evidence. Old in years, solitary in heart, not yet catching a fish: those who do not know take this to mean Luopu had no
heirs. Luopu in fact had eleven heirs; Wuya, Qingfeng, and the rest were all white-browed elders,
accomplished men. Monk Ne of Momo Hermitage wrote: In all ages, profit, fame, and wine make heroes drunkenly sink;
the haggard one by the marsh is not enough to count as solely awake. Qu Yuan, styled Ping, served the Chu
King Huai as Grandee of Sanlü. Slandered by Jin Shang, he was banished to Changsha. Walking alone by the riverbank,
he said to the fisherman: All the world is drunk; only I alone am awake. All the world is
muddy; only I alone am clear. He drowned himself in the Miluo River and died. That river is in Luo County of Tanzhou.
The Wenxuan includes the Li Sao, composed by Qu Yuan. At Luopu's death, Yancong was dull and sluggish.
Casting the line below, he got not a coin in hand; matching himself against it, in the end there was no exchange even of water and rice. Do you understand?
Not being enfeoffed as a marquis is also leisure.

cb:div.head: Case Forty-Two: Nanyang's Pure Vase
cb:div.p:
He instructed the assembly: Washing bowls and filling the vase are all Dharma-gate Buddha-work. Carrying firewood and hauling water
are nothing but marvelous function and spiritual power. Why then can you not emit light and shake the earth?

He cited: A monk asked National Teacher Zhong of Nanyang, What is the original-body Vairocana?
The National Teacher said, Bring me the pure vase. The monk brought the pureAre you merely standing in for the name?Do not forget the word-head.
vase to him. The National Teacher said, Put it back in its old place. The monk again asked,Do not mistake it.Proclaim this meaning again.
What is the original-body Vairocana? The National Teacher said, The ancient Buddha passed awayFrom where do you come and go?
long ago.Not far from here.

The master cited: Shishuang asked Daowu, What is Bodhi before the eyes? Wu called to a novice.
The novice answered, Yes. Wu said, Add water to the pure vase. After a while he asked Shishuang,
What was your question just now? As Shishuang was about to repeat it, Wu returned to his room. Shishuang
then had an awakening. Daowu first used a phrase that separated the body, then a gesture that cast off the body. If he had not
wounded the point and risked his hands, Shishuang would not have awakened. Because of the National Teacher's compassion, there was talk of entering the weeds,
but few know gratitude. This is why Tiantong drew and gathered the flower-water.
The verse says:

lg.l: Birds move through the sky, fish dwell in the water; in rivers and lakes they forget one another.Bumping and striking into it.Using left, using right.This side, that side.

In cloud and sky they fulfill their will; if the mind intends by a single thread, face to face becomes a thousand miles.Neither permissible nor impermissible.It is only in this mountain.The clouds are deep,
so the place is unknown.
To know grace and repay grace, how many in the human world are there?Mindful of it here, present in it here.One son personally attains it.

cb:div.p:
The master said: The more secure the place birds trust in the sky and fish trust in the water, the more fitting their life.
Zhuangzi says: When the spring dries up, fish stay together on land, breathing moisture on one another and wetting one another
with froth; better to forget one another in rivers and lakes. Chan Master Gui of Baizhao Tonghui said,
It is like birds flying in the sky, not knowing the sky is their homeland; fish swimming in the water forget
that water is their life. Guifeng said, Fish do not know water; people do not know wind.
In delusion one does not know nature; in awakening one does not know emptiness. The ordinary original-body Vairocana, completely pure
and awakened, appears among people. As soon as a question is raised, its shadow suddenly appears. Forgetting grace, one loses conduct,
turning from kin toward strangers. If one can truly remove the spirit-bed, then one begins to understand the son inheriting the father's trade.
Tell me, what is the father's trade? Pick it up and none of it is not it; in use, do not give rise to doubt.

cb:div.head: Case Forty-Three: Luoshan on Arising and Ceasing
cb:div.p:
He instructed the assembly: One pellet of the elixir turns iron into gold. One word of ultimate principle turns the ordinary
into the sage. If you know gold and iron are not two, and ordinary and sage are originally the same, then truly even one point
is of no use. Tell me, what is that one point?

He cited: Luoshan asked Yantou, What about when arising and ceasing do not stop?Scraping gold and rubbing the back of a clay man.
Yantou barked, Who is it that arises and ceases?Stars fall, clouds scatter.If you recognize it, there is no injustice.

Chan Master Daoxian of Luoshan in Shenzhou first asked Shishuang, When arising and ceasing do not stop,
what then? Shishuang said, You must become cold ashes and dead wood; let one thought be ten thousand years.
Let box and lid match; let there be pure clarity without a speck. The master did not agree, and went to ask Yantou.
Yantou shouted, Who is it that arises and ceases? At this Luoshan awakened. Yantou valued only
clarity of insight. Shishuang set up the Hall of Dead Wood, wanting him personally to arrive there once before
it would do. Do you not see? Ruiyan asked Yantou, What is the fundamental constant principle? Yantou said,
It moves. Ruiyan said, When it moves, what then? Yantou said, Then you do not see the fundamental constant principle. Ruiyan
stood thinking. Yantou said, If you assent, you have not yet escaped sense-roots and dusts; if you do not assent, you sink forever in birth and death.
Ruiyan too understood. Yantou was bold and heroic in spirit; in dealing with students he was exact and keen,
no less than Deshan. Later, Luoshan produced a Dharma jewel, ice colder than water. Guishan
is the one who said, I value only your place of seeing, not your conduct. Luoshan's point of questioning
is a model for people under heaven. But beginners nowadays often here make a living plan,
like pressing a gourd under water. They suppress and cut off afflictions. Zhijue said, Do not take the mind
as companion. In no-mind, mind settles itself. If you take mind as companion, whenever it moves you are deceived by mind.
To companion is to companion the deluded mind; no-mind too has no deluded mind. The Patriarch came from the West to point directly to the human
mind, see nature, and become Buddha. Was it to teach you, like the people of Puzhou, to escort the thief and take the servant for
the master? In Luoshan's question there is delusion of the true and grasping at the false; in Yantou's bark, the false is itself the true.
If it were Wansong, after the bark I would stop. Above true and false there is still business. In the Shurangama
Sutra, Ananda said, The Tathagata is now probing where the mind is, and I use mind
to infer, exhaust, seek, and pursue. That which can infer, I take to be mind. The Buddha said, Bah! An-
anda, this is not your mind. Ananda was startled, left his seat, joined his palms, stood, and said to the Buddha,
If this is not my mind, what should it be called? The Buddha told Ananda, This is external dust, a false
and illusory conception-image, confounding your true nature. From beginningless time to this life, you have taken the thief
for your son, lost your original constant nature, and so undergo transmigration. This shout is like the vajra king's precious
sword. Yantou's single shout is like a lion crouching on the ground, complete in majesty and great function, not relying on
borrowed power. Layman Pang said, A gang of six thieves, life after life they cheat and kill people. I
now know you; I will not be your neighbor. If you do not submit to me, then wherever I go
I will tell people to know you all, and cut off your roads. If you are willing to submit to me,
I will then not discriminate; I will dwell with you in one place and together realize no birth and no death. Yantou
said, Who is it that arises and ceases? Yunyan raised his whisk: Which month is this, sharing
practice together? Tiantong valued his skill in apt transformation, and again spoke a verse, saying:

lg.l:
Chop through the old vine-tangles, smash the fox den and cave; the leopard wears the mist andIt turns and grows branches and vines.Then it spits out more stubborn drool.
changes its markings,Strip off skin and hair.

the dragon rides thunder and trades its bones. Bah! ArisingChange the whole body-shell.One shout halts ten thousand workings; for three mornings both ears ring deaf.
and ceasing in endless confusion, what thing is that?A good host has no neglected companion.

cb:div.p:
The master said: He cut off the speech-head and shoveled away the intent of the question. Illumination and function were simultaneous; person and environment both
were taken away. Yantou produced work surpassing his teacher. Yangzi said, The sage changes like a tiger; its markings
blaze forth. The gentleman changes like a leopard; its markings flourish. The clever man changes like a civet; its markings gather.
When the civet changes, it becomes leopard; when the leopard changes, it becomes tiger. The dark leopard of South Mountain hides in mist and changes
its markings. Liu Xiang's Biographies of Exemplary Women of Han says: Tao Dazi governed Tao for three years, yet his reputation
did not rise, while his household wealth tripled. His wife held their child and wept. Her mother-in-law angrily took it as inauspicious.
The wife said, I have heard that on South Mountain there is a dark leopard that hides in mist and for seven days does not
eat, wishing thereby to moisten its fur and perfect its markings. As for dogs and pigs, they do not choose
what they eat, and so grow fat; through that fatness they meet disaster. Within a year he was indeed executed. Ren Fang's Records of Strange Things
says: In the summer of Emperor Hui of Han's seventh year, thunder struck South Mountain; the forest burned with fire, and the ground was scorched yellow.
After a violent rain, a dragon skeleton was found. Luoshan encountered a household-ruining enemy-thief, presented his case
and statement; Yantou accepted his surrender. Thereafter he became a capable lad. Tell me,
what is the place where surrender was accepted? Bah! In all the confusion of arising and ceasing, who is it after all?

cb:div.head: Case Forty-Four: Xingyang's Wondrous Wings
cb:div.p:
He instructed the assembly: A lion strikes elephants; the wondrous-winged bird seizes dragons. Even among fliers and runners there is distinction of ruler and minister.
A patch-robed monk must preserve host and guest. As for one who offends heavenly majesty,
how do you judge and decide?

He cited: A monk asked Monk Pou of Xingyang, When Sagara comes forth from the sea and heaven and earth grow still, face to
face, how is the matter presented? The master said, The king of wondrous-winged birds stands overA scaled eel, a horned mud loach.
the universe. In this, who is the one who comes out on top? The monk said,Spreading wings, surging and leaping, it scatters the clouds of the six directions, beats the wind, and stirs the waters of the four seas.
If suddenly one meets the moment of coming out on top, then what? Xingyang said, Like a hawk seizingI grant that your courage is broken.
a dove. Have you not heard? Verification before the tower proves what is real. The monk said, ThenGood advice is not heeded.
in that case, I fold my hands at my chest and step back three paces. Xingyang said, Beneath Mount SumeruThen wait for the second hammer blow.
sits a turtle spawn; do not wait to be marked on the forehead again.A second offense is not tolerated.

The master said: Chan Master Qingpou of Xingyang Mountain in Yingzhou was heir to Dayang Ming'an. In all he had
fifteen heirs, and all passed away before him. Later, because of Fushan Yuanjian, he obtained Touzi
Qing master; Xingyang was one of the fifteen, and Qing was his elder Dharma-brother. This monk's question
is like when the dragon king's minister challenged Shariputra at the founding of Jetavana, contending for victory.
The minister manifested a fierce dragon to harm Shariputra. Shariputra manifested a wondrous-winged bird, seized it, tore it, and ate it.
The dragon too is chief among scaled creatures, but the wondrous-winged bird feeds only on dragons. Sagara is Sanskrit,
and here means sea. Lord Pingyuan, Zhao Sheng, served as minister to King Huiwen of Zhao and King Xiaocheng.
His household built a high tower overlooking common homes. There was a cripple among the people, and a beauty laughed at him. The cripple
asked for the beauty's head. The lord promised but did not carry it out, and half his retainers left. The lord beheaded
a condemned prisoner in her place, but the guests still did not return. So he finally cut off the beauty's head and hung it before the tower
to prove it was real. After a year, the guests gathered again. In this Caodong house style, blows and shouts are not prized for direct use;
in person. Yet he had to borrow another to convey the message. This monk’s offense is not treated as grave; only then can he turn
and respond. Do you know? A cattail switch shows shame and is all the harder to violate; drawing the ground as a prison, one cannot bear to
deceive. The law of the state is like a furnace, the heart like iron. From here Tiantong spends hammer and drill. The verse says:

lg.l:
Silken edicts descend; commands divide the realm. Within the borders, the Son of Heaven; beyond the frontier,Hear the imperial decree.Whoever disobeys is beheaded.The ruler presides over ten thousand realms.
the general.Alone he secures one quarter.

Without waiting for thunder to rouse the hibernating, who knew the wind would halt the movingRising before dawn in the fifth watch,
clouds?there are already travelers in the night.

Beneath the loom, continuity upon continuity — there are naturally golden needles and jade thread; the sealHard to deceive one with eyes.
before the stamp is vast and open — originally there are no bird-script or insect-script characters.The meaning of the words is clear and bright.

cb:div.p:
The master said: The imperial order spreads through all under heaven; the kingly way does not circulate in vain. In the Liji, in the Ziyi chapter, the Master said: A king’s
words are like silk; when issued, they are like a cord. A king’s words are like a cord; when issued, they are like a great rope. Therefore a great
man does not utter wandering talk. When Sagara emerges from the sea, the Garuda holds authority. Once commands have been
divided, ruler and minister each take their place. Feng Tang said: When kings of high antiquity dispatched a general, they knelt and
pushed the axle and said, Within the gate-threshold, I govern; beyond the threshold, the general
governs. Wei Zhao said: This threshold is the outer city gate. Thunder startles the hibernating forth — the verse praises this
monk for sticking out his head too far. He did not wait for the Jingzhe season in the second month, but raised the dragon’s head too early.
Not knowing that drifting clouds follow the dragon as it moves, he was cut off by the Garuda’s mighty wind,
and without realizing it, crashed headlong. Some say: If not for the monk’s falling into words, how could Xingyang’s sharp function
have appeared? But the four words without waiting, who knew are extremely unstable in sequence. Continuity beneath the loom — this
is not the ji of clever edge, but the loom of brocade: beneath it there must be a skillful woman’s needle and thread. Xueyan,
my late teacher, once cited: The fine-eyed golden needle just shows its tip; the long-fibered jade thread subtly
enters the pass. This is the very bloodline of Dongshan; unless one is one of them, it is not easy to know. As for
the seal not facing the wind: if one stamps the empty sky, no patterned marks appear. Cangjie looked up and observed the Kui
constellation’s rounded and curved forms; looking down he examined turtle markings and bird tracks. Broadly gathering many beauties,
he combined them into writing. Later, from tadpole script came the two seal scripts. Under King Xuan of Zhou, Grand Historian Zhou
made the large seal script; Qin minister Li Si made the small seal script. The seal script used for seals now is called square-
filled. But tell me: did Xingyang still carve and incise, or not? Pure jade originally has no flaw; carve it by the foot and
you lose the sovereign virtue.

cb:div.cb:mulu: 45. Four Sections from the Perfect Enlightenment Sutra
cb:div.head: Case Forty-Five: Four Sections from the Perfect Enlightenment Sutra
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: A ready-made kōan rests only on what is present now. The proper family style does not seek
anything outside its part. If you forcefully create sections and clauses, you waste effort in vain; it is all giving handles to chaos.
Painting eyebrows on a begging bowl, attaching a handle to it — how can that be steady and secure?

He cited: The Perfect Enlightenment Sutra says, At all times, do not give rise to deluded thoughts. Toward allNo.
deluded minds, also do not extinguish them. Abiding in the realm of delusive imaginings, do not add discriminating knowing. TowardNo.No.
the absence of such knowing, do not distinguish true reality.No.

The master said: Guifeng classified this passage and called it sudden realization through deluded mind, also named entering enlightenment by forgetting
mind. Wansong adds four nots: not-arising, not-extinguishing, not-knowing,
not-discriminating. These four times eight, thirty-two characters, are everywhere else treated as illness; here they are
medicine. As for the illnesses elsewhere: not arising deluded thought — is this not scorched sprouts and ruined seeds? Not
extinguishing deluded mind — is this not nourishing sickness and losing the body? Not relying on discriminating knowing — is this not, for the time being,
not being present, like a dead man? Not distinguishing reality — is this not muddling Buddha-nature into a
bucket with thusness? Tell me then: what are the four medicines? It must be Tiantong who compounds and blends them
and brings them forth. The verse says:

lg.l:
Lofty, hall-like, rugged and open — in the noisy place he sticks out his head.To probe further, one must speak the searching word.A nose that pokes the sky.
The bed is narrow; lie down first.
In the stable place he sets his foot; the line beneath his feet is cut, and I am free.The porridge is thin; sit later.Strolling at ease past Cangzhou.

When the mud on the nose-tip is gone, sir, stop chopping. Do not stir the thousand-year oldIt fits both sides.Already flailing hands in confusion.
paper in which the medicine is compounded.It has great effect.

cb:div.p:
The master said: When Huangbo first visited Baizhang, Baizhang said, Lofty and hall-like — what have you come for?
Huangbo said, Lofty and hall-like, I have not come for anything else. Lofty, hall-like, rugged, open — all are the marks of a great
man. Amid spears and halberds he turns his body sideways and goes straight through; in a forest of thorns he swings his arms
and simply walks on. Under his heels there is no five-colored line; on his tongue there is no crossbar gate. At the nose-tip
there is no trace of mud; in the eyes no flecks of gold. Is he not a fellow at ease and delight,
carefree and lively? Try taking Tiantong’s three words do not say it and swapping them for Wansong’s four nots.
Then you will see that the gate of a single character cannot be exhausted even with ocean-ink writing. Deshan said, The whole
treasury of teachings is merely old paper for wiping filth. For those already finished, I fear it would pierce even oxhide.
As for compounding medicine in thousand-year-old paper, for those not yet finished, it does no harm to cover the eyes.
Cijue said: The Perfect Enlightenment and Śūraṅgama are always my companions. Besides, from the World-Honored One’s passing until
the gengchen year, it has already been 2,170 years. Why speak only of thousand-year-old paper?
In immortals’ lore, Ge You could carve a wooden sheep and ride the sheep up Mount Sui. Later he met Fuqiu
Gong, who said: If the line beneath your feet is not cut, you too cannot be free. It secretly accords with Yongjia’s
Release the four great elements, do not clutch them. In the quiescent nature, follow drinking and pecking. All conditioned things are impermanent, all is empty —
this is precisely the Tathāgata’s great perfect enlightenment. Though it is thus, still one phrase is lacking:
How then is Brahmā present?

cb:div.head: Case Forty-Six: Deshan Finished with Study
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: For ten thousand miles not an inch of grass — the pure ground misleads people. In the eight directions not a scrap of cloud —
the clear sky deceives you. Though it is a wedge removing a wedge, still it does no harm to lift emptiness and hang emptiness. Behind the head,
one hammer-blow — look at the expedient in another way.

He cited: Great Master Yuanming of Deshan addressed the assembly, saying, When all is exhausted,With this present,
such that the mouths of the Buddhas of the three times hang on the wall, still there is one person who goes ha-ha,keep it for eating when hungry.
laughing loudly. If you recognize this person, the matter of study is finished.Tell me, who is it?What face is it?Give him a bowl of tea to drink.

The master said: Yuanming of Deshan in Dingzhou was the ninth-generation master there, personal name Yuanmi. Among Yunmen’s
heirs, only this master’s line spread most widely. The master created the three phrases: covering heaven and earth,
cutting off all streams, following waves and chasing swells. What is now transmitted as the Three Phrases of Yunmen — on examination,
is not exact. One day he addressed the assembly, saying: When all is exhausted, to the point that the Buddhas of the three times
have their mouths hanging on the wall — this means even the broad-long tongue-mark cannot reach it in speech. Still
there is one person laughing ha-ha. Tell me, who is this person, and what is he laughing at? If
you recognize this person, the matter of study is finished. Truly, there is yet another matter present. Touzi
Qing master commented: Though the moon in Chu skies is hidden away, stars still remain in Han lands. Wansong
said: The cart is already gone — what use is the axle-grease jar? This may be said to one who advances atop a hundred-foot pole.
Baofeng Zhao said: You must be like one greatly dead — having died, die again.
A monk asked, Could it be coming alive again in death? The master said, For now, you just die — do not
come alive. You ought first to eat your meal and then hurry off to shit. You have not even eaten yet, and already
you ask about shitting — what for? This values great rest and great cessation, personally arrived at and self-verified. To speak
a full ten feet is not as good as practicing one foot. But when it comes to where one cannot act,
how do you speak of it? Ask Tiantong. The verse says:

lg.l:
Gather it in and cut off the throat; wind polishes, clouds wipe — the waters are cold,Where will you put it?Just right for turning around and breathing out.Even the finest dust must go.
and the sky is autumn.Beat it into one piece.

Do not say the brocade-scaled fish has no savor — hook away the blue waves,There is no lack of stench.
one moon-hook.Without troubling the clear waves, the meaning is naturally distinct.

cb:div.p:
The master said: Yuanming’s address did not need much; Tiantong adds a single gather, and with Yuanming
packs it all into a cloth sack. Si Da said: The Buddhas of the three times are swallowed by me in one gulp.
Once all are swallowed, where are there any beings left to save? Here the water cannot leak through; the road of ordinary and sage
is cut off. Right at such a time, the whole empty Dharma-realm is like an ancient mirror, using
the kalpa-destroying Vairambha wind to polish it bright, and the kalpa-forming golden-store clouds to wipe it clean. Water and sky become one
color; cloud and moon interweave their light. All of this takes up the utterly pure matter beyond any speck. Here the words are bland
and without flavor, like a moon-hook and cloud-bait; fish and dragons have nothing to swallow. King Cheng Tang’s prayer over the net
said, Let it go according to your will. Lü Wang hanging his hook trusted my affinity. Have you not heard: On mountain fields, husked
millet rice and wild greens, pale yellow pickles — if you eat, then eat as you wish; if you do not, go whichever way you please.

cb:div.head: Case Forty-Seven: Zhaozhou’s Cypress Tree
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: The cypress tree in the courtyard, the wind-banner on the pole — like one flower speaking boundless
spring, like one drop speaking the waters of the great sea. A primordial Buddha born among men, far beyond the common stream, not
falling into words or thought — how will you meet it in speech?

He cited: A monk asked Zhaozhou, What is the Patriarch’s meaning in coming from the West? ZhouDoluo fusses idly.
said, The cypress tree in the courtyard.Struck by a burnt brick, frozen through to the bottom.

The master cited: One day Zhaozhou ascended the hall and said, In this matter, people of truly great capacity beyond measure
cannot get out of here. When this old monk was at Weishan, a monk asked, What is the Patriarch’s
meaning in coming from the West? Wei said, Bring me the bench. If one is a true lineage teacher, one must use
one’s proper matter to receive people. Then the monk asked, What is the Patriarch’s meaning in coming from the West?
Zhou said, The cypress tree in the courtyard. The monk said, Master, do not use objects to show
people. The master said, I do not use objects to show people. The monk said, Then what is the Patriarch’s
meaning in coming from the West? The master said, The cypress tree in the courtyard. At Guangxiao Monastery east of Yangzhou city,
Chan Master Huijue went to Fayan. Fayan asked, Where have you come from lately? Jue said,
Zhaozhou. Fayan said, I have heard Zhaozhou has a cypress-tree saying — is it so? Jue said,
No. Fayan said, Everyone coming and going says that when a monk asked the Patriarch’s meaning in coming from the West,
Zhou said, The cypress tree in the courtyard. How can you, Reverend, say there is none? Jue said, My former teacher
truly had no such saying. Master, please do not slander my former teacher. Monasteries everywhere called him Jue Iron-
Beak. Shengmo always had to make people first pass through this saying to wash out their views and knowledge.
He once said, The Three Mysteries and Five Ranks are all within it. Chan Master Zhenru Fang awakened to this
saying and went straight into the abbot’s room to see Chan Master Guangzhao Jue of Langya. Zhao asked, How do you
understand it? Fang said, Last night the bed mat was warm; in one sleep I reached dawn. Guangzhao
approved him. Zhenru understood this saying best; Tiantong’s verse on this saying is also
not bad. The verse says:

lg.l:
Bank-brows cross with snow, river-eyes contain autumn, a sea-mouth beats up waves;He has eaten more salt than rice.A single point is hard to conceal.There is a phrase,
but it is not the school’s meaning.

a boat-tongue rides the current; the hand that quells disorder, the strategy of great peace —No words, and saints and ordinary are cut off.It is also the cypress tree.
the plan.It is also the cypress tree.

Old Zhaozhou, old Zhaozhou — stirring and churning the Chan groves, never yet at rest.Why does it not answer?
Tiantong, second

Mere wasted effort! Yet building the cart to fit the ruts is originally no special skill;Bring it forth for use, just right
it too is filling ravines and stuffing ditches.Buy up all elegance without spending a coin

cb:div.p:
The master said: Seven hundred sexagenary cycles — he has seen many things. Therefore bank-brows crossed with snow. The ancients
took brows and eyes as cliff and lightning. Tiantong uses the old image of river-pupils and sea-mouth to form
a four-line verse, as if seeing living Zhaozhou pointing to the cypress tree. Brows like reed-
flower banks; eyes like autumn waters, blue. An old verse says, Wild waters are clearer than the blue of a monk’s eyes; distant
mountains are deeper than the green of a Buddha’s head. Sea-mouth beating waves, boat-tongue riding currents — waves can
overturn a boat, and a boat can ride waves. One word can raise a state; one word can
ruin a state. Therefore he follows with the hand that quells disorder and the plan of peace. Zhou once
said, Sometimes I use one blade of grass as a sixteen-foot golden body; sometimes I use the sixteen-foot
golden body as one blade of grass. This saying was originally to settle people’s doubts, but now
how many people are stuck in doubt over it. Did Zhaozhou wish to stir up the Chan groves? People see Zhaozhou
answer on the spot, responding the instant the question sounds, as if it required no effort. Only Tiantong knew that eighty
years of pilgrimage means even a three-year-old child surpasses me, and I learn from him. This is what one fashions in idle times,
to use when busy. If one has not endured hardship, one does not know Wolun’s trickery:
He can cut off a hundred thoughts; facing conditions, the mind does not arise; bodhi grows day by day. The Sixth
Patriarch said, Huineng has no tricks: he does not cut off a hundred thoughts; facing conditions, the mind repeatedly arises.
How could bodhi grow? Seen this way, the business of filling ravines and stuffing ditches — then how
is it? Now tossed into West Lake, below it the clear wind — to whom shall it be entrusted?

cb:div.head: Case Forty-Eight: Vimalakīrti Sutra on Nonduality
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Wondrous function has no fixed way; there are places where one cannot lay a hand. Eloquence is unhindered,
yet there are times when one cannot open the mouth. Longya was like a man without hands throwing punches; Jiashan taught
a tongueless man to speak. Who is the one who withdraws halfway along the road?

Raised: Vimalakirti asked Manjushri, What is a bodhisattva's entry into the gate of nonduality?
Manjushri said, As I see it: in regard to all dharmas, no words, no explanation, no indication, no knowing, apart from all questioning and answering — this is entry into the gate of nonduality.At the point of questioning, which rankBest to strike straight at the mouthBrewed
Bring it forthStill think it too littleTend the fire and watchThere is
ComeHow thick is your hideWhat is
Thereupon Manjushri asked Vimalakirti, saying: We have each spoken already;
benevolent one, now you should speak. What is a bodhisattva's entry into the gate of nonduality?If you can speak, speak quickly
Vimalakirti remained silent.One pass, one scrape, not fearing a foul flare-upWhere are you going

The master said: In Sanskrit, Vimalakirti; here it is rendered Stainless Fame, also called Pure Name. His wife
was named Golden Lady; his son, Good Thought; his daughter, Moon Above. A monk asked Yunju Jian:
Vimalakirti is the Tathagata Golden Millet — why then did he still join Shakyamuni's assembly
to hear the Dharma? Ju said: He did not contend over self and others. In the expanded Vimalakirti Sutra, thirty-two thousand
bodhisattvas each expound the gate of nonduality. Here there are only thirty-two bodhisattvas. At the end,
