I do not know where the roadhead and gateway are. If one explains this meaning according to the sutra,
the Tathagata himself speaks it as a preface to Perfect Penetration; in fascicle six, Manjusri then selects
the Perfect Penetration. But on the side of patch-robed monks, Tiantong once said: In the ten directions there are no walls.
From the start there has never been any barrier. On all four sides there is no gate either. Just here itself is the
place of entry. Therefore Qianfeng made one stroke and said, It is here. Those who do not know often
take it as Qianfeng pointing out the road to this monk. Or else they say he drew a line to cut off
any further wandering. This is definitely not the principle. Do you not see? Yunmen explained it
as eight parts rice, nine parts chaff. Huanglong Nan said, Qianfeng for one occasion pointed out the road, bending himself
for beginners. Yunmen then made its transformation flow through, and thus later people do not tire of it. Wansong
said, The waves at Caoxi seem alike, yet countless ordinary people are drowned on dry land. Yunmen
had long been with Qianfeng, Caoshan, and Shushan. This monk thought he must surely know Qianfeng's use,
so he sought instruction. But if he had merely used Qianfeng's needle and thread, it would have been just a donkey-tethering stake. Suddenly
pulling out Muzhou's Qin-era awl, all that can happen is the cup falls to the ground and the plate
breaks into seven pieces. This monk did not grasp Qianfeng's meaning, so Yunmen gave him another
living road. It is very much like throwing oil on a fire to put it out, or fanning to melt ice. Zhugan long ago already pointed
this out, with a verse: Qianfeng need not point and explain; Yunmen need not peddle curios. Naturally
the East Sea carp strikes Indra's nostril. Zhugan, compared to Yunmen, is even too
openly compassionate; people find it all the harder to understand. Better Tiantong's cold-eyed way, unexpectedly
a bit closer. The verse says:

lg.l:
Taking it in hand, he still treats the dead horse; the reviving-soul incense is about to stir your peril.Use thunderbolt hands and wolf-tiger medicine.
To pry open the coffin and save the dead, there is another divine prescription.

In one pressing, sweat bursts from the whole body; only then do you trust our houseIf the medicine does not stun and daze, the grave illness will not heal.
spares not its eyebrows.Swallowed together with the top.

cb:div.p:
The master said: Qianfeng is a dead-horse doctor; the cure cannot be done. This monk was already one who had lost body and life.
Yunmen obtained the reviving-soul incense, able to make the already dead come back to life.
The Record of Collected Omissions says: In the first year of Han Yanhe, the Western Hu state of the Yuezhi sent envoysGive voice to it.
to present four liang of incense, as large as sparrow eggs, colored like mulberries. By the first year of Shiyuan, the capital
suffered a great plague, and more than half had died. The emperor took the incense and burned it; those dead less than three days
all revived. The fragrance did not cease for three months. The incense came from Reniao Mountain on Juku Isle. The tree
was like a maple; its scent could be smelled for several li. It was called the Reviving-Soul Tree. They cut the root and boiled the sap in a jade cauldron into a black
resin. One name was Startle-Essence Soul; a second, Return-to-Life; a third, Shaking Sandal; a fourth,
Repel-Death. In a brief talk Tiantong said: Fully obtaining the reviving-soul incense, all alike come
to beat the poison-smeared drum. Yunmen's single loose drop, Tiantong says, is reviving-soul incense. In all quarters
it changes into a poison-smeared drum, making you unable to turn around or breathe out, cutting off the throat
and plugging the nostrils, yet all the limbs pour out sweat in one great burst. Zhigong said, Suddenly awakening to
the original nature's emptiness is just like a fever breaking in sweat. Though Wansong's eyebrows have
only a few hairs.

cb:div.head: Case Sixty-Two: Did Mihu Awaken or Not
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Bodhidharma's highest meaning left Emperor Wu of Liang bewildered. Vimalakirti's gate of nonduality
made Manjusri miss with words. Is there still any share in entering and acting, or not?

Raised: Mihu had a monk ask Yangshan, Do people nowadays still need awakening or not?Have you ever been confused.
Shan said, Awakening is certainly not absent, but what can be done about falling into the second level? The monkHow can it be avoided.
returned and reported this to Mihu. Hu deeply approved it.Which number is it.Assent is not absent, but how avoid second place.

The master said: Chan Master Mi of Jingzhao was also called Master Mi Seven, and also Mihu. In lay life
he was the seventh son and had a beautiful beard, hence the two names. A jewel among the eight directions, he was heir to Xuefeng. Now
according to Yangshan, who was a fellow student and heir to Weishan, this monk was precisely asking whether the superior worthies of old still
Have you reached the true principle or not? Hu said, Reached. The monk said, Just as for the true principle, how does one
come to reach it? This is no different from the occasion of false awakening. Hu said, At that time Huo Guang
sold a fake Silver City and made a treaty document with the Chanyu. What sort of man did that? Foguo praised
Mi Hu as a great spiritual friend; his name was not falsely handed down. The monk said, I am simply brought to seal
my mouth, with no words. Hu said, On level ground you make a man stand guarantor. As for Mi Hu saying Reached.
Yangshan said, Awakening is not absent, but what can be done about falling into the second head? If one does not rely on
false awakening, then again it is said only when one accepts for oneself is it intimate. Monk Shengmo often said, Touzi
handling old cases had inward elegance and graceful turns beyond compare. Once he raised this saying and said, Still, Yangshan
can speak this way well enough, but did he avoid falling himself or not? If he avoided it, then
there is another person who utterly refuses to agree. If he did not avoid it, then he too falls into the second head. Mi
Hu, though he approved him, did he himself still have a road of emergence or not? All of you
try to inspect it. If you can inspect it clearly, the two men shatter like tiles and melt like ice. If you inspect
and cannot, then for now do not be hasty. The verse says: On the peak of green ridges he borrows a question from a man, pointing
to where the mountain ends, yet he has not settled his body. Though he escapes the Double Ninth command, how can it compare to the numinous sprout
that does not violate spring? Wansong said, Without spring wind the flowers do not open, but when the flowers open
they are blown down again. National Teacher Zhong asked Attendant Zilin, What is the meaning of Buddha? Lin said,
It means awakening. The National Teacher said, Was Buddha ever deluded? Lin said, Never deluded. The National
Teacher said, Then what use is awakening? Lin had no reply. This too originally has no meaning of delusion and awakening
in it. I have always loved Xuedou saying, Originally there is no delusion or awakening, yet their numbers are like hemp; only Ling
yun is acknowledged as a master. If awakened, one falls into the second head; if not awakened, then only self acceptance.
What then is it? Tiantong has his own expedient means. The verse says.

lg.l:
In the second head, dividing awakening and breaking delusion, one must quickly let go and cast away fish trap and rabbit snareA man of Pu Province escorts the thief.Put it down.
Now.

If effort is not yet exhausted, it becomes a joined toe; and by knowledge it is hard to know, awakening only bites the navel too lateIn the end it is still extra.Yu's strength
where it does not reach, the river's sound flows westward.

The rabbit is old, on the ice platter autumn dew weeps; the bird is cold, on the jade treeCling to it and it cannot be borne.
the dawn wind is bleakSit on it and it will not do.

Bringing it forth, Great Yang discerns true and false; traces and blemishes are whollyOne point is hard to conceal.
absent, precious as flawless white jadeAbove all, avoid poking it through.

cb:div.p:
The master said, In the second head, dividing awakening and breaking delusion means when light comes darkness departs, when knowledge arises confusion dies.
All are matters on the road. In the Brief Commentary to the Zhouyi: The snare exists for the rabbit; once the rabbit is gotten
the snare is forgotten. The fish trap exists for the fish; once the fish is gotten the trap is forgotten. Thus words
are the snare for images, and images are the trap for meaning. One who keeps the words has not gotten the image,
and one who keeps the image has not gotten the meaning. If toward the second head one says one has some
awakening, then not even one time is true penetration attained. Yet it is said, Even if there is marvelous awakening, it too must be spat
out. One must quickly let go; arriving home there is not a single thing. Only then are snare and trap forgotten and cast away. Effort
and knowledge all belong to the second head. When effort is exhausted, it cannot be known by knowledge. Only then
is there a small measure of accord. In the Outer Chapters of Zhuangzi, Joined Toes chapter eight, it says: What is joined on the foot
is attached useless flesh; what branches on the hand is a useless finger grown out. The note says joined
toe means the big toe joined to the second toe; branch finger means a sixth finger. If effort is not
exhausted, it is like joined toes attaching useless flesh. In the Spring and Autumn: King Wen of Chu attacked Shen
and passed through Deng. Marquis Qi of Deng said, He is my nephew, and stopped to feast him. Zui-nephew and Dan-nephew
and Yang-nephew asked to kill the ruler of Chu, but the marquis of Deng would not permit it. The three nephews said, The one who will destroy Deng
will surely be this man. If you do not plan early, later my lord will bite his navel. The note says this means it will be too late.
Where knowledge does not reach, knowledge cannot know. The old rabbit means the full moon. Monk Danxia Chunhe
once said, When water is clear and the moon is full, the practitioner grieves. On the ice platter autumn dew weeps. If one clings to it, then
it is unbearable. In the Classic of the Great Wilderness, atop Kunlun Hill there is a jade tree of langgan, bearing fruit
small like pearls. In the Inscription of the Mysterious Center: The numinous tree stands far off, and the phoenix has nowhere to rest. And
the crane does not stop its loom. All this does not permit guarding, clinging, and sitting fixed. The bird is cold and mournful, not
wanting to land on another's roots, trunk, branches, and leaves. In the Shi, the Yi section: A blemish on white jade may still
be polished away. An inner flaw in jade is called a defect, a break in substance; an outer flaw is called a blemish, a stain of color.
This verse esteems Yangshan's white jade without blemish, not falling into the second head. But what
is the first head? Only after great awakening does one know it is not so.

cb:div.head: Case Sixty Three: Zhaozhou Asks About Death
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly says: Sansheng and Xuefeng are like spring orchids and autumn chrysanthemums; Zhaozhou and Touzi are like Bian's jade disk and
Yan gold. On a starless scale both ends are level. In a bottomless boat, they cross at one place. When two
people meet, how is it?

The case: Zhaozhou asked Touzi, When a person greatly dead comes back to life, how is it?
Touzi said, Night travel is not allowed; you must arrive at dawn.The probing pole is in hand.Shadow grass follows the body.

The master said, Chan Master Datong of Touzi Mountain in Shu Prefecture first visited Cuiwei Wuxue
Chan Master. He happened to meet him pacing in the hall, and stepped forward, bowed, and asked, The hidden meaning of coming from the West,
Master, how is it shown to people? Cuiwei paused his steps and looked at him. Touzi said, I beg
the Master to point it out. Cuiwei said, Do you want a second ladle of foul water too? Touzi suddenly awakened in accord.
He bowed in thanks and withdrew. Cuiwei said, Do not fall away. Touzi said, When the time comes, root and sprout of themselves
grow. Another day he happened to ask, What is Buddha principle? Cuiwei said, Buddha does not reason.
Touzi said, Does that not fall into emptiness? Cuiwei said, True emptiness is not empty. Thereupon he gave a prophetic verse,
saying, When has Buddha principle ever reasoned? True emptiness is again not empty. Datong will dwell at Jizhu and spread
and expound my school. Touzi returned to his home region at Tongcheng, to Touzi Mountain. Zhaozhou first in
Tongcheng met him. Zhou said, Could you be the hermitage master of Touzi? Touzi said, Tea overseer,
give me one coin in alms. Zhou went up the mountain first; Touzi carried an oil bottle and came after. Zhou said,
I long heard of Touzi, but on arriving I see only an old oil seller. Touzi said, You only see the oil seller,
and do not yet know Touzi. Zhou said, What is Touzi? Touzi lifted up
the oil bottle and said, Oil, oil. Touzi set out a tea seat to receive him, and himself passed a sesame cake to Zhao
zhou. Zhou paid no attention. Touzi had the attendant pass the sesame cake. Zhou bowed to the attendant three times.
Tell me, what was his meaning? Chan Master Yongguang Zhen of Suzhou, ascending the hall, said, If the point of speech
is off even slightly, your home country is ten thousand li away. One must directly let go over the cliff, and personally accept and bear it.
Cut off, then revive again; you cannot deceive the sovereign. Zhaozhou asked with this meaning. If not for Tou
zi, in the end it would be hard to match it. So he immediately said, Night travel is not allowed; you must arrive at dawn.
This is like the usual saying about not escaping a beating with the white willow stick; in wording and intent they seem alike. But in terms of
principle it exactly corresponds to the thrust of Zhaozhou's question. Zhou said, I had already waited for white, and he still
waited for black. From this his reputation gathered a following, and a memorial was submitted to fulfill the prophecy, naming it Jizhu
Monastery. Baiyun Duan's verse says, Dead and then alive again, the fangs still show; must arrive at dawn, already
he has gone first. In what household's detached lodge pond, a pair of mandarin ducks cannot be painted? Try
looking at Tiantong's single brushstroke painting. The verse says.

lg.l:
Mustard city and kalpa stone, marvelously exhausted at the beginning, the living eye in the ring center shines through vast emptinessOnly when this present time is exhausted can it be established.
Revived after death, one cannot deceive you.

Night travel is not allowed; at dawn you arrive home, yet the family message is still not willing to be entrustedAlready on the road.
to goose and fishThis is already falsely transmitting news.

cb:div.p:
The master said, In the Treatise on Great Wisdom: there is a city a hundred yojanas square on each side, filled with mustard seeds; every hundred
years one seed is taken, and when the mustard seeds are exhausted the kalpa is still not exhausted. As for kalpa stone, the Sanskrit is kalpa.
This means a division of time. In the Lou Tan scripture, there is a great stone forty li square. Every hundred years
the devas come and brush it with gauze cloth; the stone is worn away, yet the kalpa is still not exhausted. Exhausting these
mustard city and kalpa stone only reaches the exhaustion of the present time, and only then returns to before the empty kalpa.
Only then is the eye alive. Ring center comes from Zhuangzi: The hinge first gets its ring center, thereby
responding to the inexhaustible. This means cycling without end and obtaining its ring center. The empty place in the ring
center is the body, and endless cycling is its function. The Poetry Commentary says: the large one is called hong.
The small one is called yan. An envoy of the Western Han said to the Chanyu, The Son of Heaven shot in Shanglin and got
a goose, and on its foot Su Wu had tied a letter. Because of this the Chanyu did not dare deceive him. Han's Cai
Bojie had a daughter named Yan, style Wenji, wife of Dong Si. At the frontier there was disorder.
While Si was on patrol he was seized by tribesmen, and Yan became consort to their king. Longing for home, she wrote a letter and sealed it in wax
pellet and tied it to a goose's neck. When the goose reached Han territory, it drank water, the pellet fell, and a fish swallowed it. A fisher
man cut open the fish and got the letter, thus learning where Yan was. In this verse, Night travel is not allowed; at
dawn you must arrive. One must never spread family shame abroad by recklessly sending messages. Even so, to report upward to
Tiantong, just now the attendant thanked him for transmitting Dharma instruction.

cb:div.head: Case Sixty Four: Zizhao Inherits the Lineage
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly says: Shaoyang personally saw Muzhou and offered incense to old Xue; Touzi directly
received Yuanjian and inherited the Dharma at Dayang. On coral branches jade flowers bloom; in the champak grove
golden fruit ripens. Tell me, how does this transformation come?

The case: Head monk Zizhao asked Fayan, Reverend, when you opened the hall, whose lineage did you inherit
Fayan said, Dizang. Zhao said, You have too deeplyHad I known it would come to this idle meddling today, I would regret not using good intent then.Grace returns to where it belongs.
wronged former teacher Changqing. Fayan said, I do not understand oneThe elbow does not bend outward.
turning word of Changqing. Zhao said, Why not ask? Fayan said, In the midst of the myriad forms,Feigning a strike, pretending not to know.It draws the wolf into the house to foul it.
the body alone is revealed what is the meaning? Zhao then raised his fly whiskPresenting it face to face.A double koan.
Fayan said, This is what you learned at Changqing's place. On the head monk's own part, how
is it? Zhao had no words. Fayan said, Just as in the midst of the myriad forms, aloneSplit the arrow nock and seize the nest.It only manages one jump.
the body is revealed, is that sweeping away the myriad forms or not sweeping them away? Zhao said, NotYet the gourd vine coils back and binds it.
sweeping away. Fayan said, The two attendants at your sides both say, Sweeping away the myriadThe saying splits into two stumps.Clear eyes are hard to fool.
forms. Fayan said, Then what about the body alone revealed in the midst of the myriad forms?The more it turns, the less bearable it looks.Two colors in one match.

The master said, Fayan long studied under Changqing Leng, yet after inheriting Dizang, Zhao, head monk under Changqing,
who had formerly discussed old and new matters with him, was inwardly angered and immediately led a group
specially to Fuzhou to challenge him. Fayan, knowing this, led the assembly out to greet them and gave special courtesy, receiving them as guests.
In host and guest seats, each hung a whisk. During tea, Zhao suddenly changed expression and loudly said,
Elder, when you opened the hall, whose lineage did you inherit? The master said, Dizang. Zhao said, Why so greatly
wrong former teacher Changqing? I was together under him for several decades, discussing ancient
and current matters, never with any gap between us. Why then did you instead inherit Dizang? This matter is not in many years,
nor is it in long study. As with Yisu Jue and Gaoting Jian, how could outsiders judge
and measure? Head monk Zhao, taking sides to protect his school style, could not discuss the matter and only raised slander. Fa
yan at that time deeply pitied these people who did not understand the great method, and wrote the Treatise on Ten Regulations to admonish them.
Students must not fail to read it. Human sentiment and the power of the Way differ as widely as heaven and earth.
Therefore he answered him with the matter of his own portion, saying, I do not understand one turning word and its causal nexus. This is the bearing of one from a
great house, not debating and not contending. Yet Zhao took what they had formerly long discussed under Changqing
and used it as proof. Because Fayan treated him by the old friendship, once pressed a little, Zhao
split apart in all directions. His attendants rushed to rescue him, making it look even more unbearable. It may be said that in rout they could not withstand
even a broom of reeds. Zhao and the group withdrew in shame. Fayan then somewhat checked them and stopped them,
saying, Head monk, killing father or mother still allows repentance, but slandering Great Prajna is truly hard
to repent. Zhao in the end had no reply. From then on he instead studied under Fayan, and clarified his own understanding, and never again
opened a hall. The ancients answered evil with goodness, anger with compassion, and then with the equal
wisdom of Buddha brought people forth. Even if this head monk Zizhao later inherited Fayan, it still was not enough
to repay that deep virtue and wash clean his original intention. Tiantong only takes where Elder Zhao questioned,
and Fayan's final line, and verses them so that naturally head and tail are both right. The verse says.

lg.l:
Leaving thoughts, one sees Buddha; breaking dust, the scripture comes forth; the house style is present and completeWhen the grass dries, the hawk's eye is sharp.When the snow is gone, the horse's hooves are light.
Neither lacking nor surplus.

Who sets up gate and court? The moon follows the moving boat; the river silk is clearAll flows out from here.One and many unhindered in going and staying.
Freedom.

Spring follows the grass, green over the burn marks; sweep away or not sweep away, listenAbove your head, recognize Jiashan.When it turns, it surely runs to both ends.
to the earnest warningAffairs do not disdain fineness.

The three paths may have grown wild, but on returning one finds the old pines and chrysanthemums stillIt does not run downhill.
fragrantA good chance is hard to meet.

cb:div.p:
The master said, The preface to Perfect Awakening says: Mind is originally Buddha, but because thoughts arise it drifts and sinks. The shore
truly does not move, yet because the boat moves it seems to race. The Avatamsaka Sutra says: There is a great scripture
scroll, equal in measure to the three thousand worlds, within a single mote of dust. All dust motes are likewise. There is
a clear eyed person who breaks the dust and brings out the scripture scroll, benefiting and saving all people. Tiantong draws on
two great scriptures and gathers them into one paired verse. As for sweeping away the myriad forms, tell me, who are the myriad forms
the myriad appearances. Uniquely exposed, who is uniquely exposed? This is a ready-made koan. The family style is always preserved. Who
would further set up gates and courtyards, open doors and windows? In the Huayan school, three boats viewing the moon each follow their own boat.
Traveling one course, the clear river for a thousand li answers alone. In Huichong's poem: The river divides, the ridge's force is cut off.
Spring enters and the burn marks turn green. In Xie Xuanhui's poem: The remaining glow scatters into brocade; the clear river is pure
like white silk. The moon follows three boats, spring follows the hundred grasses. The three boats and hundred grasses are the myriad appearances.
The moon and spring are what is uniquely exposed. In Tiantong's verse, seeing whether to brush away or not brush away, there is much rough
mindedness. Here one should only be careful and detailed in admonition. Have you not seen? Senior monk Zifang also
came from Changqing to Fayan. Fayan also brought up the earlier saying and asked; Fang also raised the whisk. Yan said:
If it is like this, how can it do? Fang said: What is the honored meaning of the master? Yan said: What do you call
the myriad appearances? Fang said: The ancients did not brush away the myriad appearances. Yan said: Amid the myriad appearances,
the body is uniquely exposed. What talk is there of brushing away or not brushing away? Fang suddenly awakened. In Fayan's earlier saying
at the end he said, Amid the myriad appearances, the body is uniquely exposed, eh? At the end of this saying he further said,
Amid the myriad appearances, the body is uniquely exposed; what talk is there of brushing away or not brushing away? It can be said, wishing to leave,
then return. Returning, one then attains. Reckoned out, the field is bitterly not much. Master Zhao and Master Fang, in the end,
though subtle, missed the source. It is the fault of turbid intellect spinning on. In Tao Yuanming's Return Home:
The three paths are nearly overgrown, yet pines and chrysanthemums still remain. Jiang Xu, styled Yuanqing, opened three paths, and only
Yang Zhong and Qiu Zhong came and went as friends. This verse praises Fayan's meeting the school through the words, and opens forth
the marvel of the two masters without losing Changqing's intent. What is Changqing's intent? The myriad
appearances amid which the body is uniquely exposed.

cb:div.head: Case Sixty-Five: Shoushan's Bride
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: Cha-cha, sha-sha; peeling and falling; knife-knife, stumbling-stumbling; all spread out,
sweating and dripping. There is nothing to bite or chew. Hard to draw near. Tell me, what saying is this?

Raised: A monk asked Shoushan, What is Buddha? Shoushan said, The bride ridesGood for airing something fresh.
the donkey, the mother-in-law leads it.What principle is this.

The master said: Chan master Xingnian of Baoying in Ruzhou was a man of Caizhou, surnamed Di. He studied with
Fengxue. Fengxue addressed the assembly, saying: In olden days the World-Honored One, with blue-lotus eyes, looked over
Kashyapa. Right at that moment, tell me, what was said? Shoushan then stepped down
and left. The attendant entered the room and asked for instruction, saying: Why did Nian Fahua not answer
the master? Fengxue said: Nian Fahua understood. Next day Shoushan and head gardener Zhen stood attending together. Fengxue said: How is it that the World-Honored One spoke without speaking?Guanghui of Ruzhou.
True indeed.
Zhen said: The turtledove cries atop the tree; its meaning is in the hemp field. Fengxue said: What use is it for you to make so much
foolish blessing? Why not investigate the words and phrases? He then asked Shoushan: What do you make of it?
Shoushan said: In stirring countenance he reveals the ancient road; he does not fall into the silent mechanism. Fengxue said to Zhen:
Why do you not look at Nian Fahua's reply? Later, after Shoushan came forth in the world, he ascended the hall and said:
If you want intimacy, do not bring a question. The question is in the place of the answer; the answer is in the place of the question. If
you bring a question, this old monk is under your feet. If you deliberate, there is no connection.
One day he held up a bamboo comb and said: If you call it a bamboo comb, you hit it; if you do not call it a bamboo
comb, you turn away from it. All of you, what will you call it? Master Sheng of Yexian snatched it
and broke it into two pieces, threw it down the steps, and then said: What is this? Shoushan said: Blind
fellow! Xian then bowed. Various places call this the barrier of turning away and hitting. A common saying goes: Upside down,
topsy-turvy: the bride rides the donkey, the mother-in-law leads it. Foguo's verse says: Shoushan has a saying, through old and
modern times transmitted. Do not say this saying is merely upside-down inversion. The bride, drunk, rides the donkey away; at that time
people laugh the mother-in-law to death as she leads it. It is not like Tiantong's verse, which is delightfully apt. The verse says:

lg.l:
The bride rides the donkey, the mother-in-law leads; in form and style the grace is perfectly natural.No need to bring up grasses and trees.It cannot be sketched,
and the painting cannot be completed.

Laughable is the neighboring girl who imitates a frown; before others she adds ugliness and fails to make beauty.Cleverness turns into clumsiness.
It becomes a joke to onlookers.

cb:div.p:
The master cited Yuantong Xiu's Iron Wall verse, saying: Why toil to comb the hair three times a day? If tied up
firmly at the root, then let it rest. In the end, what wins is natural flesh and bone; without applying rouge and powder
there is still grace. Shoushan's answer uses no embellishment; naturally it has the bearing of a woman of the household,
charmingly placed. It is like Xi Shi's heart pain: she clutches her chest and frowns, which only adds to her beauty. An ugly woman
imitates the frown, and only adds to her ugliness. This reproaches learning by mouth and ear, those who do not strive for subtle awakening.
With one mind they still want to act graceful, but the four limbs and eight channels refuse to go along.

cb:div.head: Case Sixty-Six: Jiufeng's Head and Tail
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: Those in supernatural functioning and subtle use cannot set their feet down. Those who forget conditions and cut off thought
cannot lift their feet up. It can be said: sometimes killed by running, sometimes killed by sitting. How can one attain
just-rightness?

Raised: A monk asked Jiufeng, What is the head? Jiufeng said, Opening the eyesLofty beyond the time before Awesome Sound.
one does not notice dawn. The monk said: What is the tail? Jiufeng said, NotYet its clarity does not go beyond the door.Walking alone after the kalpa is empty.
sitting on the ten-thousand-year bed. The monk said: When there is head without tail, how is it?In the cave, it does not nest.Going first does not arrive.
Jiufeng said: In the end it is not precious. The monk said: When there is tail without head, how is it?The maid sees the servant girl being diligent.
Jiufeng said: Though full, there is no strength. The monk said: If one reaches where headAt the end, a great mistake.What use is it.
and tail match, how is it? Jiufeng said: Sons and grandsons gain strength; within the roomRuler and minister joined in the Way, above and below in harmony.
it is not known.Each rests in his own place.

The master said: Chan master Daoqian of Jiufeng in Yunzhou was a man of Guanhuai in Fuzhou, surnamed Liu.
Though he had gone through the Dharma seats everywhere, he received the seal from Shishuang. At first he resided at Jiufeng, and dark-clad disciples
were especially numerous. Later he lived at Letan in Hongzhou and died there. By imperial decree he was posthumously named Great Awakening. One day
a monk asked, What is the head? If one's eye of the Way is not clear and penetrating, and discriminating wisdom is not
complete, then when speaking of head and tail, before and after, the mind is muddled and does not know where it lands. Jiufeng said:
Opening the eyes, one does not notice dawn. Once a monk asked: Everyone speaks of seeking instruction, but I do not yet know,
by what Dharma does the master save and aid? Jiufeng said: Tell me, has a great mountain ever lacked an inch of earth?
The monk said: If so, then why do people all over the world come in search? Jiufeng said:
Yajnadatta lost his head and his mind went mad by itself. The monk said: Is there one who is not mad? Jiufeng said:
There is. The monk said: What is the one who is not mad? Jiufeng said: On the road at break of dawn, the eyes do not
open. This is precisely the model of opening the eyes and not noticing dawn. The monk said: What
is the tail? Jiufeng said: Not sitting on the ten-thousand-year bed. Another monk asked: What is before the lamp is lit?
Jiufeng said: Toil and labor, yet no strength. The monk said: What is the proper lighting
of the lamp? Jiufeng said: Head large, tail small. The monk said: What is after the lamp is lit? Jiufeng
said: Having withdrawn from position, one does not know leisure. This is the model of not sitting on the ten-thousand-year bed.
The monk said: When there is head without tail, how is it? Jiufeng said: In the end it is not precious. Opening the eyes
and noticing dawn, that is it. The monk said: When there is tail without head, how is it? Jiufeng said: Though full, there is no
strength. Having withdrawn from position and knowing leisure, that is it. The monk said: If one reaches where head and tail match, how is it?
Jiufeng said: Sons and grandsons gain strength, full and yet strong. Within the room it is not known; in the end
it is noble and precious. The Record of the Source Mirror says: Entering our school, first one must know there is this. Then
afterward preserve and foster it. It also says: Head and tail must be proportionate; principle and practice must not be lacking.
Mind and mouth must not contradict each other. If one enters the Source Mirror, principle and practice are both complete. Shishuang was Jiufeng's teacher,
and he instructed the assembly saying: Beginners who have not yet grasped the great matter must first recognize the head. The
tail will come by itself. Shushan came forth and asked: What is the head? Shishuang said: You must directly know
that there is this. Shu said: What is the tail? Shishuang said: Exhaust this present time. Shu said: When there is
head without tail, how is it? Shishuang said: Even if you spit out gold, what can it be used for? Shu
said: When there is tail without head, how is it? Shishuang said: There is still something relied upon. Shu
said: If one reaches where head and tail match, how is it? Shishuang said: He does not make an understanding of it,
yet still I do not approve him. Therefore Jiufeng said: Worthies, when the ancients spoke of the
head, it was only to make you know there is this. When they spoke of the tail, it was only to teach you to exhaust the present time.
There are so many things in you that do not correspond, therefore they teach you here to set aside,
block off, and wear them away, so that today you may become corresponding and complete the matter. If it is
your own body realizing it, truly and constantly thus, then such talk can no longer be used. Even
so, it must be the right person for it to work. Do not merely share one sound with different mouths. You must quickly
make effort and take care. Wansong uses Jiufeng's koan to verify Jiufeng's koan. The commentary
is already commented; the saying has already been fully explained. The remaining flavor is entrusted to Tiantong. The verse says:

lg.l:
Compass for circles, square for squares; in use one goes, in withdrawal one hides; dull and stumbling,
The bowl is round, the tray is square-cornered.Rising in the peck, turning in the bushel
a bird perching in reeds;How could it know to soar high and far

advancing or retreating, a ram butting a fence; eating another'sIt cannot stride alone in the great square
household rice,Quickly spit it out

lying on one's own bed; clouds rise and bring rain, dew congealsAbove all avoid letting roots growIn spring it sprouts, in summer it grows
into frost;In autumn it is harvested, in winter it is stored

jade threads meet and pass through the needle's eye, brocade silk unbroken,Continuous without gap
the shuttle spits out its gut;Reversing and overturning, all interconnected
the stone woman's loom stops, and the night color turns toward noon;The patterned phrases crisscross, yet the meaning is distinct
the wooden man's road turns, and the moon's shadow shifts past the center.Understanding and practice do not touch the Way of this very time

cb:div.p:
The master said: One who gains the marvel in all directions, turning left or right and meeting the source, is what Zhuangzi meant by saying,
The round accords with the compass, the square accords with the rule. The Master said to Yan Yuan: Used, one goes;
set aside, one hides. Only you and I have this. If not so, then it is like gluing
the tuning pegs to tune the strings, or notching a boat to mark a sword. The Treatise on the Precious Treasury says: As for the path of advancing in cultivation, within it
there are ten thousand routes. A trapped fish comes to rest in shallow water; a dull bird perches in reeds. These two do not know
the great sea, nor do they know the forest. When people head toward petty paths, the meaning is likewise so. In the Zhou
Changes, the hexagram Great Strength, top six: the ram butts the fence, unable to retreat, unable to advance. There is no
advantage. In difficulty there is good fortune. Eating another's rice and lying on one's own bed is like saying, eating
government rice while tending one's private camel. It is also said: One who knows how to use a cold mouth to eat another's hot food is hard
to find. Coming out, one becomes cloud and rain; going in, ice forms and frost congeals. This is merely just coming out
and just going in, not yet the work of a true master. One must reach the point where needle and thread pass through, fine hairs are close-knit, and the loom's
silk does not snag, while pattern and color spread freely. Just when the stone woman's loom stops, already the wooden
man's road has turned. Just when the night's color tends toward noon, already the moon's shadow has shifted past center. These
final two lines are really one line. Scholars of Confucian learning and literature nowadays call this
a separated-line pairing. Wansong, in this way separating what is joined and joining what is different, wants to meet Tiantong face to face.
All of you must not let Jiufeng down.

cb:juan.cb:jhead:
Elder Wansong's Commentary and Verses on Tiantong Jue's Ancient Cases, Congrong Hermitage Record, Volume
Four

Elder Wansong's Commentary and Verses on Tiantong Jue's Ancient Cases
Congrong Hermitage Record, Five

cb:div.head: Case Sixty-Seven: Wisdom in the Huayan Sutra
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: One mote of dust contains the myriad appearances; one thought contains three thousand. How much more so for a man who props up heaven
and stands upon earth, who knows the head of the Way and knows the tail, a keen and nimble fellow. Do not betray your own
numinousness and bury the family treasure.

Raised: The Huayan Sutra says, I now universally see all sentient beings as possessing
the Tathagata's wisdom, virtue, and marks; only because of deluded thoughts and attachments they do notBears turn somersaults and donkeys dance the zhezhi
realize it.Delusive thoughts and attachments are not bad either

The master said: In the beginning and end of the Great Commentary to the Huayan, Master Qingliang classified this passage of scripture and named it
opening the causal nature. In the Commentary on Samantabhadra's Practices and Vows, it is called opening the source of beings' nature. How is it opened?
The Appearance chapter says: Children of Buddha, there is not a single sentient being that does not possess the Tathagata's
wisdom. Only because of deluded thinking, inversion, and attachment, they do not realize it. If they depart from deluded thinking, all-knowledge, natural wisdom, and unobstructed wisdom thenNote says ordinary folk have delusive thoughts; the provisional and lesser cling in inversion
This applies to the two kinds above
appear before them. Then it raises the analogy of one mote of dust containing the scripture scrolls of a great thousand worlds. The earlier verse on the myriad
appearances and the uniquely exposed body, seeing Buddha apart from thought, breaking the dust and bringing forth the sutra, is exactly this
scriptural section. It also says: At that time the Tathagata, with unobstructed and pure wisdom-eyes,
universally surveyed all beings in the Dharma realm and spoke these words: Wondrous indeed, wondrous indeed! These
sentient beings, how is it that they possess the Tathagata's wisdom, yet in foolish delusion they do not know
and do not see? I shall teach them the right path, causing them forever to leave deluded thoughts and attachments, and themselves
Within one’s own body one can see the Tathagata’s vast great wisdom, no different from the Buddha’s. Qingliang
in the Great Commentary says: Sentient beings take inherent virtue as their substance and gather the sea of wisdom as their source.
Only the appearances change and the substance differs; feeling arises and wisdom is separated. Now this teaches one to know the mind and unite with the substance, to realize the root
and forget feeling. Therefore this scripture is spoken in order to make it manifest. Explanation says: This also explains
the reason sentient beings are deluded about the true. It is like a person of complete blessings, virtue, wisdom, and fine appearance
who suddenly dreams of a poor, sick, suffering body; that is appearance changed. Not seeing the original body,
that is substance differing. Clinging to it and taking it as my body, that is feeling arising. Not believing one’s own
body is blessed, virtuous, and upright, that is wisdom being cut off. A monk asked Bao-ci, Feeling arises and wisdom is cut off;
appearance changes and substance differs. Before feeling arises, how is it? Ci said, Cut off. Monasteries everywhere did not know
the source of this. Many take appearance to mean thought; so I clarify it here. Students should know. Yangshan
tested Xiangyan: Tathagata Zen I allow elder brother understands; Patriarchs’ Zen you have not yet even dreamed of.
Tell me, how far apart is Tathagata Zen? Try taking the Avatamsaka scripture passage and examine my
Tiantong verse intent. The verse says:

lg.l:
Heaven covers, earth bears, made into a lump and a clod, pervading the Dharma realm withoutIt reaches through above and penetrates belowKnives and axes cannot chop it open
edge.In the ten directions there are no walls

Split to adjacent-void dust and there is no inside, reaching the utmost mystery.Even the Buddha eye cannot spy itA fine thing
Better to have none

Who distinguishes toward and away? Buddhas and patriarchs come to repay the debt of verbal karma; askThere is nowhere to evadeToo many words injure practice
Master Wang of Nanquan.Avoid Shanshan

Everyone only eats a single stalk of greens.There is no other business left to manage
For

cb:div.p:
The teacher said: Yantou said, It must flow straight out from your own breast, then bring it forth and with me
cover heaven and cover earth. Now it says heaven covers and earth bears, how upside down it is. Covering
is spoken according to human convention. People all say heaven and earth give birth to man, calling them the three
powers. Buddhism instead takes man as giving birth to heaven and earth. Therefore the three realms are mind-only and the myriad dharmas
are consciousness-only. Here they are beaten into one lump, forged into one clod, pervading the Dharma realm with no boundary
or outer mark. In the Surangama Sutra the Buddha told Ananda: Observe the nature of earth; coarse, it is the great earth.
Fine, it is minute dust. Reaching adjacent-void dust, analyze the boundary-form of that utmost subtle matter; it is made of seven
parts. Further divide adjacent-void dust and it is in truth empty nature. Wansong often cited the Faith in Mind
Inscription: The extremely small is the same as the great, forgetting all boundaries; the extremely great is the same as the small, no boundary is seen.
If someone asks what is greatest in the world, one should say true emptiness. Why?
Because the extremely great is the same as the small, so no boundary is seen. If someone asks what
is smallest in the world, one should say true emptiness. Why? Because the extremely small is the same as the great, forgetting all bounds
and realms. Ah, what kind of person was the Third Patriarch? He uttered one saying, and the patch-robed monks of the world
cannot jump out of it. A monk asked Zhaozhou: What is the mystery within mystery? Zhou said: How long have you been
mysterious? The monk said: Mysterious for a long time. Zhou said: If not for this old
monk, you would almost be killed by mystery. Dongshan’s Inscription on the Mystery Within says: As you go, do not depart; returning, do not turn your back
on the source. Since it pervades the Dharma realm and is beaten into one clod, why toil to seek some separate subtle mystery beyond the dust?
How could there be before and after, toward and away? Before Buddhas appeared in the world, it was never diminished. When Buddhas appeared in the world
and pointed out and expounded it, it was never increased. Praising the ocean’s breadth or boasting of the sun’s brightness
is not worth speaking of; all are added words. Nanquan and Shanshan, in a communal work period picking bracken greens,
at that time, Nanquan picked up one stalk and said: This is excellent for an offering. Shanshan said:
Not only this one; a hundred flavors of delicacies he also would not regard. Nanquan said: Even so,
each and every one must be tasted before it is so. Dayang Ming-an ascended the hall and said: Do not
travel the road of mind-made places. Do not sit in the achievement of nowhere. Being and nonbeing both left behind, vast and open, heaven and
earth are empty. Therefore Nanquan said: Everyone eats a single stalk of greens. Seek one more stalk,
and you enter hell like an arrow shot. Wansong said: Today’s people are seven parts...

cb:div.head: Case Sixty-Eight: Jiashan Wields the Sword
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Within the realm, the Son of Heaven issues edicts; beyond the frontier, the general gives commands. Sometimes at the gate
there is power; sometimes within the room one is called the honored one. Tell me, who is this?

Raised: A monk asked Jiashan, When brushing away dust and seeing Buddha, how is it? Shan said:Why must it be so
You must directly wield the sword. If you do not wield the sword, a fisherman will nest there. The monkJust so indeedIf sitting, then not Buddha
then raised this and asked Shishuang, When brushing away dust and seeing Buddha, how is it? Shuang said:If seen, do not stir it; if stirred, it is not seen
He has no country; where will you meet him? The monk returned and reported this to Jiashan. Jiashan ascended the hall and said: In gate-front arrangements he does not match this old monk; in deep talk of entering principleNot sitting, then BuddhaComing and going
Not easy
he is still a hundred paces beyond Shishuang.Each gets a stake

The teacher said: Chan Master Shishuang Qingzhu of Tanzhou was at first rice steward at Weishan.
Once while sifting rice, Wei said: Donors’ goods must not be wasted. Shuang said: I am not wasting
it. Wei picked up a grain from the ground and showed it, saying: You say you do not waste it. This one,
where did it come from? The master had no reply. Wei said: Do not slight this one grain; hundreds of thousands of grains
come from this one grain. Shuang said: Then this one grain, from where is it born? Wei
laughed loudly and returned to the abbot’s quarters. That evening he ascended the hall and said: In the assembly’s rice there are bugs. Later he studied with
Daowu and asked, Bodhi before the eyes. Wu called a novice to add water to the pure-water vase. He awakened as in the
earlier cited case. Shuang was with Daowu for two summers and received the seal. During the Huichang persecution he hid his traces
at Taojia Ward in Liuyang, Tanzhou, roaming by day and lodging by night. At the beginning of Dazhong, a monk came from
Dongshan and recounted: At the end of summer, early autumn, Dongshan said, Brothers, whether east or west, you must directly
go to a place where for ten thousand miles there is not an inch of grass. After a long pause he said, But if in a place for ten thousand miles with no
inch of grass, how do you go? Shuang said: As soon as you go out the gate, it is grass. The monk again
reported this to Dongshan. Shan said: This is the speech of a good teacher of fifteen hundred people.
In the Great Tang, how many such men can there be? As the awl in the bag first showed its point, he took up residence at Shishuang’s
monastery, truly matching Wuben’s prediction. For twenty years the ocean-like assembly numbered over a thousand, often practicing long
sitting without lying down, rigid like stumps. The name Dead Tree Hall was established from this. A monk asked about
brushing away dust and seeing Buddha; the question asked was one and the same. Jiashan said, If you do not wield the sword, a fisherman
will nest there. Shishuang said, He has no country, where will you meet him? Wansong said: In
deep talk of entering principle Jiashan is not equal to Shishuang; in gate-front arrangements Shishuang is still a hundred paces behind Jiashan.
Is there no one who can match both colors in one contest? What did Tiantong say? The verse says:

lg.l:
The sword-energy brushes the Dipper and Ox, heaven washes the weapons; martial might settles disorder. To whom does the merit return, after all?Peace was originally brought by the general
Yet the general is not allowed to see peace

Once the dust and haze are cleared throughout the four seas, with robes hanging down the imperial transformation is of itselfJust exhaust ordinary feelings
effortless.There is no separate holy understanding

cb:div.p:
The teacher said: In the Linji school style, the vajra-king precious sword kills Buddha and kills patriarchs. Jiashan
cuts off old vines and smashes fox dens. In the Jin History, Lei Huan was skilled in astronomy.
Zhang Hua observed that between the Dipper and Ox there was often a strange vapor, so he invited Huan, and at night they climbed a tower and looked up.
Huan said: I have observed it long. The essence of a precious sword rises to heaven; it is in
the bounds of Fengcheng County in Yuzhang. Hua then recommended Lei as magistrate of Fengcheng. While repairing the jail and digging foundations,
they found a stone casket with a pair of swords. Their light shone brilliantly. He sent one to Hua and kept one for himself to
wear. Hua was executed and the sword was lost. After Huan died, his son, serving as a provincial officer, wore his father’s sword and came to
Yanping Ford. The sword leapt and fell into the water. He sent someone to dive after it, and two dragons with patterned markings were seen.
The diver, frightened, returned. In the Garden of Sayings, when King Wu attacked Zhou and met rain, San Yisheng said: This
is not an omen? The king said: No, Heaven is washing the weapons. This verse on if one does not wield the sword
and the fisherman nests. The Yi says: The Yellow Emperor, Yao, and Shun let their robes hang down and thereby ruled the world.
The transformation of nonaction proceeds without command. This verse says there is no nest to perch in; he has no country.
There is no sword to wield; where will you meet him? Chan Master Tong-an Cha said: The wondrous
body originally has no place. Through the whole body there is no further trace or cause. Wansong said: I allow
that you have personally seen Shishuang, yet you are still a hundred paces from Jiashan. Want to see Jiashan? The sword
leaves the jeweled casket because of injustice; medicine comes out of the golden bottle because of the sickness cast before it.

cb:div.head: Case Sixty-Nine: Nanquan’s White Ox
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Becoming Buddhas and patriarchs is still burdened with a tainted name. Wearing horns and hair, one is instead placed
in the highest seat. Therefore true light does not shine, and great wisdom seems foolish. There is another convenient one,
deaf and pretending not to notice. Do you know who it is?

Raised: Nanquan addressed the assembly saying: The Buddhas of the three times do not know there is the fox-slaveOnly because one knows there is
white ox, yet the white ox does know there is.Only because one does not know there is

The teacher said: Dharma Master Jiezhu of Feishan wrote Discussion on the Mind-Dharma of Separate Transmission and criticized Nanquan,
saying: Men like Yuan do not delight in learning and do not know the root; they are unfit to speak of the Tathagata’s
teaching. The Supplement to the Infinite Lamp, relying on common understanding, casually narrates that Nanquan first studied the Vinaya,
then listened to Huayan and Lankavatara, entered the Hundred Gates Contemplation of the Middle, and hearing that Mazu transmitted a teaching beyond words,
repeatedly questioned him on its meaning and suddenly attained forgetting the fish-trap. One day while serving gruel, Mazu asked: In the bucket
what is there? Quan said: This old man ought to shut his mouth, speaking such words.
Quan did not yield to Mazu in the moment; yet later he had to repay the debt in Zhaozhou’s hands.
Quan asked a scripture-seat master: In the Nirvana Sutra, what is taken as the ultimate rule? The master said:
Taking thusness as the ultimate rule. Quan said: Calling it thusness is already a change. Now
a shramana must practice among other kinds before it will do. In front of the monks’ hall, Zhaozhou
asked: I will not ask about other; what is kind? Quan then placed both hands on the ground. Zhou with his
foot gave one stomp and Quan fell to the ground. Zhou ran into the infirmary, shouting: Regret, regret!
Quan had an attendant ask Zhou: What do you regret? Zhou said: I regret not giving him two more
stomps. Quan ascended the hall and said: Teacher Wang, from childhood, raised a water buffalo, intending
to graze it east of the stream, but it could not avoid eating the state’s water and grass. Grazing it west of the stream also could not
avoid eating the state’s water and grass. Now I cannot avoid paying some portion according to my share; in all,
it cannot be seen. One day Quan saw the bath steward heating the bath and said: After the meal, invite the water buffalo
to bathe. The bath steward went to invite it. Quan said: Did you bring rope? Zhou with his hand
tugged Quan’s nose. Quan said: Yes indeed, but it is too rough. Zhou asked: A person who knows there is,
where does he go? Quan said: To the donor’s house before the mountain, to become a water
buffalo. Zhou said: Thanks for the master’s instruction. Quan said: Last night at the third watch the moon came to the
window. As Quan was about to accord with the world and pass away, the head monk asked: After the master’s hundred years, where will you go?
Quan said: Below the mountain, I will become a water buffalo. The head monk said: May I follow the
master or not? Quan said: If you follow me, you must bring a single stalk of grass.
This talk of other kinds was first sung by Nanquan, echoed by Weishan, and transmitted by Daowu and Yunyan.
Now it became Caoshan’s three falls. Daowu came to Nanquan. Quan asked: What is your name, Reverend?
Wu said: Zongzhi. Quan said: Where wisdom does not reach, how do you make lineage there?
Wu said: By all means avoid speaking of it. Quan said: Exactly; if spoken of, horns at once grow. Three
days later, while with Yunyan in the back room threading a needle, Quan passed by and asked: The other day
we said where wisdom does not reach, avoid speaking of it; if spoken of, horns grow. How should one
practice and conduct oneself? Wu immediately withdrew into the monks’ hall. Quan then left. Yan asked Wu:
Brother, why just now did you not answer the master? Wu said: You are so
quick and sharp. Yan did not get it, and went to ask Quan: In the case just now, why did Head-Monk Zhi
not answer the master? Quan said: He is one who walks among other kinds. Yan
said: What is walking among other kinds? Quan said: Did you not hear it said? Where wisdom does not reach,
by all means avoid speaking of it. If spoken of, horns grow. You must directly walk among other kinds.
Yan still did not understand. Wu, knowing Yan did not get it, said: This person’s affinity is not here.
So they returned together to Yaoshan. Yan then raised the previous story. Shan said: How do you
understand him at such a moment, that you came back? Yan had no reply. Shan then laughed loudly.
Yan then asked: What is walking among other kinds? Shan said: I am tired today.
Come another time. Yan said: I came back specially for this matter. Shan said: Just
go for now. Yan then went out. Wu was outside the abbot’s quarters; hearing Yan did not get it, he unconsciously bit
his finger until blood came out. Then he came down and asked: Elder brother, in that exchange when you asked the master, how did it
go? Yan said: The master would not explain it for me. Wu then lowered his head. The two
stood attending. Shan asked about where wisdom does not reach, avoid speaking of it, if spoken of horns
grow. Wu then bowed and withdrew. Yan then asked Shan: Why did Brother Zhi
not answer the master? Shan said: My back hurts today. He is the one who understands. You
go ask him. Yan then asked: Brother, why just now did you not answer the
master? Wu said: My head hurts today. Go ask the master. Later Yunyan
passed away and sent a messenger with a farewell letter. After reading it, Wu said: Yunyan did not know there is.
He regretted that at the time he had not said it to him. Even so, still it did not go against Yaoshan
’s lineage. Xuanjue said: When the ancients spoke thus, did they know it or not? Yunyan
did not understand at the time. Where exactly was the place of not understanding? Cuiyan Zhi said: Daowu
said Yunyan did not know it, and regretted not telling him then. But speaking this way,
did Daowu himself know it or not? Wansong said: Yunyan was Dongshan’s
teacher, the source of one whole branch. Again and again, he did not know of this matter. Wansong records it in full
also to help later people from the side, lending half their effort in inquiry. Is it only Yunyan who did not
know it? Cuiyan Zhi said: Did Daowu know it or not? Wansong said: Not only
Daowu alone, did Cuiyan know it or not? Have you not seen a monk ask Changsha: Why
do the Buddhas of the three times not know it? Changsha said: Before entering Deer Park, they were still somewhat
closer. The monk said: Then why do the cat and the white ox know it? Changsha said:
How can you blame them for it? At this point Wansong can only stand by and watch. Great or small, Tiantong
has a share in praising it. The verse says:

lg.l:
Limping, dragging, ragged and loose, in a hundred ways not worth taking. OneIf you are not near, stop hurryingPeople do not like to look
fit for nothing.Open the door and it is soft; light the fire and it is damp

Silently he himself knows the ground is stable; easygoing,Wiggle the toes inside the boots
easygoing, who says his belly is stupid?Slyness scattered inside stupidity
Universally filling the dharma-realm, all turns into rice,Cannot spit it out, cannot swallow it down
his nostrils droop down, proof he has feasted on practice.Throw half away and scatter half

cb:div.p:
The master said: Limping, dragging, withered and weak, an unremarkable look. When Yaoshan was reading a sutra, Baiyan
said: Master, stop making monkeys of people. Yaoshan rolled up the sutra and said: What time of day is it now?
He said: The sun is right at noon. Yaoshan said: You still have this bit of literary polish. He said: I
have none at all. Yaoshan said: You are greatly airing your cleverness. He said: I am only thus;
what is the venerable master’s meaning? Yaoshan said: I limp and drag along, with a hundred uglinesses, a thousand
clumsinesses, and just get by like this. Guanxi Xian’s verse says: Shaken out for many years, this tattered robe
half its threads frayed away chasing the clouds. I pick it up and throw it over my shoulders;
still better than people of the time wearing brocade. In a hundred ways not worth taking, fit for nothing; among the assembly, just like
a hundred clumsy ones, all life long he is an idle man. Silently he himself knows the ground is stable; easygoing, who
says his belly is stupid? Wang Fanzhi wrote it in reverse, and people all said it was wrong. Better to stab
your eyes than hide my feet. Wang Fanzhi was a strange man; these words spread widely among people.
It may be called a skin lantern-ball, bright within and dark without. In the final line of the verse, full practice
reaches the ground of rotten odds and ends; on eyebrows and eyelashes are all grains of rice. One day
when the scabby belly bursts open, all the five viscera and heart-liver are spewed out.

cb:div.head: Case Seventy: Jinshan Asks About Nature
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Hearing of the fragrant elephant crossing the river, you have already gone along with the current. Knowing the unborn nature of birth and non-birth,
you are still detained by birth. Then you go on discussing before samadhi and after samadhi, making shoots and making bamboo strips.
The sword has long since fallen away, yet you are only now marking the boat. Turning the pivot wheel underfoot, how will you travel
another road? Try bringing it up and see.

Raised: Head Monk Jinshan asked Head Monk Xiu, saying: Clearly knowing the nature of birth and non-birth, why
is one detained by birth? Xiu said: Shoots in the end become bamboo,As before, twist the wooden nose
but can you use them as bamboo strips right now? Jin said: Later on, you yourself will realize it.The nostrils are in another's hands
Xiu said: I only see it this way. What is the venerable one’sBig and small both despise the good and take the base
meaning? Jin said: This one is the steward’s room; that oneThrusting your head into another's bosom
is the cook’s room. Xiu then bowed.Hit the ball off somewhere elseFor now, treat him with goodwill

The master said: Chan Master Hongjin, head of Qingxi Mountain in Xiangzhou, was the chief seat under Master Dizang Chen
and served as first seat. At that time two monks both bowed to Dizang. Dizang said: Both are wrong.
The two monks had no reply. Later they asked Head Monk Xiu for instruction. He said: You yourselves are majestic and upright,
yet you bow to someone else; is that not wrong? Jin heard this and did not agree, saying:
You yourself are confused and dark; how can you guide others? Xiu, indignant, went to the hall to ask. Dizang
pointed to the corridor and said: The cook has gone down into the storehouse. Xiu then awakened. One day Jin
asked Xiu: Clearly knowing the nature of birth and non-birth, why is one still detained by birth?
Amtizhe Woman was of Brahmin stock, the daughter of elder Posi-ni, in Changti Village over twenty li west of Shravasti.
Because her household held a great assembly, they invited the Buddha
and the sangha, and so Tizhe’s good name was widely renowned. Great Bodhisattva Manjushri asked whether there were any
who clearly know the marks of birth and non-birth yet are detained by birth. The woman said: There are.Previously taken as nature
Though they see it clearly themselves, their strength is not yet full, and so they are detained by birth.
Head Monk Xiu answered: Shoots in the end become bamboo, but can they be used as strips right now?
Bamboo strips are bamboo skin used to bind things, bamboo cords. Shoots are tender and lack strength; mature bamboo has strength. A shoot’s
strength is not yet full, so it cannot serve as binding strips. In Juefan’s Hymn to Guanyin: Pity me, my mind is clear but my strength
does not reach it; again and again old seeds manifest in action. Like a man who goes mad from wine, though he forbids
drinking, he keeps encountering fine brew. This too speaks of strength not yet full. For shame, the ancients
had the teachings thoroughly digested; in every word and breath they matched the sutras. Head Monk Jinshan
wanted to turn the saying around, so he would not allow it and said: Later on, you yourself will realize it.
Xiu said: My understanding is only this. What is the venerable one’s intent? Master Xiu
had first settled himself in a place all even and complete. Master Jin, making him rise, surely had
another living road. So he pointed and said: This is the steward’s room; that is the
cook’s room. Tell me then: Does this clearly know the nature of birth and non-birth, or not clearly know it?
Tell me also: Is it detained by birth, or not detained by birth? Xiu then bowed. He studied
the living phrase, not the dead phrase. This and The cook has gone down into the storehouse are
no different. Everywhere they say the Fayan school is uniformly plain and real, mysterious within the body. Please
look at the foregoing story. Tiantong saw this saying as extraordinary and unique, so he fully and thoroughly
brought it out in verse. The verse says:

lg.l:
Open and free, with no reliance; lofty ease, unbridled. The homeland lies level and calm,The plank overturns the donkey tethering stakeSnap the golden lock
few are the ones who arrive.Set your foot where it is steady

A little strength marks off stages and ranks; vast and open, body and mindForcing sections and details into being
cut off right and wrong.See the strange and do not be startled

With right and wrong ended, one stands alone in the great square, without ruts or tracks.Then the strangeness collapses by itselfPeace
With no taboos, where is not free and graceful

cb:div.p:
The master said: This verse on Head Monk Jinshan’s This is the steward’s room, that is the
cook’s room means: when reliance is gone, there is naturally openness; where nothing binds, there is inherently lofty
ease. How many can reach the level and peaceful homeland? One must have no afflictions to cut off, no
Zen path to investigate. In the twelve hours of the day, apart from putting on clothes and eating, this is the place of idle mind-use.
Judging from Master Jin’s question, there is also a slight tracing of old and testing of present, dividing stages and ranks, fixing
lines and hooks of strength. Yet his vast and open body-mind is originally beyond right and wrong. Xiu
also may indeed understand the Tathagata-Zen, in plain and straightforward discussion of fundamental talk. But
as Baizhang said, To explain meaning by relying on scripture wrongs the Buddhas of the three times. Therefore a slight division of strength,
provisionally setting up steps: to speak of shoots is tender and strengthless use; bamboo as strips can pull a thousand
jun. When words and traces arise, right and wrong are thereby formed. Master Jin cut that away for him,
separately pointing to a road of living function. This is not at all opening windows and doors, setting tracks and ruts, deceiving fellow
practitioners. Master Xiu offered a bow to thank him for what he had missed. Do you yet recognize these two masters’ household forge and bellows?
Enter the fire and you still must be finely tempered; only on the anvil can you endure heavy hammer and chisel.

cb:div.head: Case Seventy-One: Cuiyan’s Eyebrows
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Spit blood at others and you foul your own mouth. Greedy for a cup, all your life you repay others’
debts. Sell paper for three years and still owe ghost money. Wansong asks on behalf of you all: Is there
any way to bear the pole and settle the account, or not?

Raised: At summer’s end Cuiyan addressed the assembly, saying: All summer long, for you brothersStill complain it is too little
I have spoken to you. Tell me, are Cuiyan’s eyebrows still there? Baofu said: APublicly air the family shameNever mind the mouth feeling rough
thief has a guilty heart. Changqing said: They grow. Yunmen said: Barrier.Also a person in the fireAdd frost to snow
。Blocking streets and cutting off alleys

The master said: Great Master Yongming of Cuiyan in Mingzhou, personal name Lingcan, was from Huzhou. He received
Snowpeak’s recognition and transmission, and widely spread the Dharma seat. One day he ascended the hall and said: Since one summer
ago, I have talked east and talked west for you brothers. Look: are Cuiyan’s eyebrows still there? Everywhere
many say he originally meant to present himself, but unknowingly made a double koan. Then seeing Baofu say,
A thief has a guilty heart, they keep saying he tucked firewood behind his back to hide his verbal fault. Foguo
said: Many people misunderstand and say that in broad daylight he spoke words not fitting the occasion, making
trouble where there was none; first he confessed his own fault, so others would not inspect him. Fortunately there is no entanglement.
Changqing said, They grow. Wansong said: They are still right above the eyes. Yunmen’s Barrier
is the one-character Zen, universally famed. All three were heirs of Snowpeak. Seeing the householder
speak with no outside talk, and seeing Cuiyan’s address to the assembly as extraordinary, they all joined in response. The ancients’
comments did not violate the gesture. A lecture master from a Huayan teaching seat invited Cuiyan
for a meal. Cuiyan said: This mountain monk has a question; if you answer it, then I’ll attend. He then picked up
a sesame cake and said: Does this possess the dharma-body? The lecturer said: It does. Cuiyan said: If so, then
you are eating the dharma-body. The lecturer had no reply. A teaching master then answered in his place: What fault is there?
Cuiyan did not approve. Yunmen answered in his place: Special thanks to master for condescending to come to this empty feast. Juefan
called Great Master Yunmen the king among monks. Indeed, Tiantong strings it all together
and from start to finish brings it out in verse. The verse says:

lg.l:
A thief’s heart, a man’s courage; clear and sharp, crossing freely, responding to the occasion.The stolen goods are already exposedActing as if no one else were thereBarehanded snatch
Clever theft

Baofu and Yunmen too let their noses hang and deceive their lips; Cuiyan and Changqing tooSticking your head out too far
have long brows reflecting the eyes.Pretend to strike and feign ignorance

Blocking Zen worthies are countless, stubbornly saying:How does Tiantong's fabrication compare with Wansong
Meaning and phrase are all cut away at once.Wanting to hide it only reveals it more

Burying themselves, they too swallow their breath and voice.Raising a son
He does not measure up to his father
Dragging down the ancestral school, they too face the wall carrying a board.The family line declines in one generation

cb:div.p:
The master said: Xuedou said, Of a good thief, even ghosts and spirits do not know. Since Baofu and Tiantong
saw through it, Cuiyan was not a good hand. But they do not realize that as great as Yunmen and Chang-
qing were, Cuiyan used a single eyebrow hair to pierce all their nostrils at once. If you
hide the head and show the shadow, what sort of strong-handed fellow is that? Thus he could clearly and freely respond
to conditions. Baofu said, A thief has a guilty heart; Yunmen said, Barrier. Both are long-nosed
monks indeed. A thousand miles away one already smells dry shit; through three winters one still sniffs rotten
melon fragrance. Cuiyan said, Are the eyebrows there? Changqing immediately said, They grow. Like a painted monk on the wall,
called once, he responds at once; like a goose raised in a bottle, following the sound, already out at just that
moment. How could it be measured by conscious thought? Therefore it says, Long brows reflect the eyes.
Looking at people from a cold place, not one bit can deceive him. Some say: All summer the vine-talk
sprouted branches and spread tendrils, and now he cuts the grass and pulls the roots, wiping it all out at once. They do not know
that thirty years later this saying will be greatly current. Why hide it in the sack and cover it with a quilt?
Swallowing breath and voice like this is facing the wall carrying a board, dragging down the ancestral school. Various places
say: Xu Liu carrying a board sees only one side. The minister did not imitate wall-facing. Shu
says wall-facing sees nothing at all. Have you not seen? On Vulture Peak the white tuft mark was emitted, illuminating
the eastern direction for eighteen thousand.

cb:div.head: Case Seventy-Two: Zhongyi’s Monkey
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Contesting wits across the river, hiding armor and burying troops; face to face they hold real spears
and actual swords. This is why patchrobed monks prize complete function and great use. From loose to tight,
try speaking it out and see.

Raised: Yangshan asked Zhongyi: What is the meaning of Buddha-nature? ZhongyiThis seat master is fit to uphold an argument
said: I will give you an analogy. Suppose a room has six windows, and insideBetter false than true
there is a monkey. Someone outside calls, Monkey, monkey, and the monkey immediatelyWill you consent to rest yet
responds. In this way, all six windows are called at once, and all respond at once. Yangshan said:Come again and it will not be worth half a coinIt only needs the beloved to recognize the voice
But when the monkey is asleep, then what? Zhongyi then got down from the Chan seat,Do not talk in sleep
grabbed him and said: Monkey, monkey, now I meet you face to face.Have you awakened yetWhy not say so earlier

The master said: Chan Master Hong’en of Zhongyi in Langzhou was one of Mazu’s more than eightyCommanding Dingzhou
great spiritual friends. He was Yangshan’s grand-uncle in the Dharma. After Yangshan received
the precepts at Jiangling, he returned to offer thanks for the precepts. Seeing Yangshan was young, Zhongyi used the monkey at the six windows as a metaphor to explain
the meaning of buddha-nature, like clapping one’s hands and jingling things to scare a little child. Yangshan had hidden troops and a ready blade; he endured it
as long as he could, but his sharpness could not be contained. After bowing in thanks, he said: Now that I have received your analogy, there is nothing I do not understand.
But there is one more matter. Suppose the monkey inside dozes off, and the monkey outside wants to see it—
at such a time, what then? Marvelous indeed! A true lion cub at once bared the claws and fangs of the Dharma cave. Zhongyi
unconsciously stepped down from the Chan seat, took Yangshan by the hand, danced, and said: The ape has met with you,
it has seen you already! Why not give an analogy for that? Yunju Xi said: If, at that time, Zhongyi had not
gotten that one sentence from Yangshan, where would Zhongyi have been? My late teacher and Master Shengmo,
my Dharma-uncle, were only in their twenties, yet the monasteries respected and feared them. Reverend Bao of Zhengzhou, whose name
shook the He-Luo region—my late teacher, while traveling to visit teachers, went to see him. Bao said: Brother, you are young and gifted; this is precisely the time to
knock and inquire. In my younger years, this old monk, moment after moment, always took the Buddha-Dharma as his business. My late teacher left his seat and stepped forward,
saying: And how is it for you now, Reverend? Bao said: Like meeting a living enemy.
My late teacher said: If I had not gotten this saying, I would almost have traveled a thousand miles in vain. Bao stepped down from
the Chan seat, grasped my teacher’s hand, and said: A true adept! He then stayed there several days. Jiashan called this
a bean popping in dead ashes on Buddha Sun Mountain—surely this is what he meant. Xuanjue said: If it were not for Yangshan,
how could one ever see Zhongyi? Tell me then, where was it that Yangshan
got to see Zhongyi? Wansong said: In Bao’en’s Dharma hall. Chongshou Chou said:
Is there anyone who can settle this principle? If you cannot settle it, you are only
manipulating ghostly tricks and gestures. Where is the meaning of buddha-nature in that? Wansong said: Discern it right on those ghostly
tricks and gestures. Fojian said: Yangshan played the fool; Zhongyi sold cleverness.
Cleverness, when sold, comes out as foolishness; foolishness, when released, comes out as cleverness. Though the monkey may be asleep,
still, in its belly it is wide awake. Even if you seal off the six windows completely, where could the ape
fail to meet? Do you all want to see the old fellows’ playful deception? Every one of you has a face skin
three inches thick. Wansong said: I cannot find even one man who knows shame. Only old Tiantong
is a little better. The verse says:

lg.l: Frozen sleep in a snow hut, the years wear all down; the deep vine-covered gate stays shut through the night.The hibernating door does not openA dragon with no dragon phrase
In the cold withered grove one watches transformation; spring wind blows up the ash in the pitch-pipe.Almost killed deadHappily revived again

cb:div.p:
The master said: The Records of Worthies of Runan says: At a time when heavy snow had piled more than a zhang deep on the ground, the magistrate of Luoyang
personally went out on inspection. He saw people clearing snow, and among them one begging for food. When he came to Yuan An’s
gate, there was no sign of traffic, so he thought Yuan An must be dead. He ordered men to clear the snow, entered the house, and found Yuan An
lying stiff. He asked why he had not gone out. An said: In a great snow everyone is hungry; it is not fitting to trouble
others. The magistrate took him for a worthy man and recommended him as filial and incorrupt. This verse on Zhongyi’s analogy and his sleepy talk—
once it rises, in the end it is still not fully awake: the deep vine-covered gate does not open at night. But suddenly, when
Yangshan punctured it, Zhongyi immediately functioned with his whole being. Dry wood in double-yang; spring wind
blows up the ash in the pitch-pipe. In Cai Yong’s Monthly Ordinances, bamboo is cut into tubes, called pitch-pipes.
They are placed in a sealed chamber, and reed membrane is made into ash to fill the ends. When the monthly qi arrives,
the ash flies out and the tube is emptied. Yang energy is born—life within death. Just like Zhongyi and Yang-
shan, adepts meeting adepts. After meeting, why trouble to stand long?

cb:div.head: Case Seventy-Three: Caoshan’s Mourning Fulfilled
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Clinging to grass and attaching to trees, one becomes a spirit; bearing grievance and wrong, one comes as a haunting ghost.
Call it, and you burn paper money and offer paper horses; send it off, and you chant over water and write talismans. How
can the household gates ever be at peace?

Cited: A monk asked Caoshan, When the mourning garments are not worn, what then? CaoshanThe cicada sheds its shell yet still clings to the cold branch
said, Caoshan today has fulfilled mourning. The monk said, After mourning is fulfilled, what then? Caoshan said, Caoshan loves drunken wine.Not betraying a lifetimeWalk broadly
With big stridesWhat is not possible

The master said: A monk asked Chan Master Tong’an Wei, Before Niutou met the Fourth Patriarch, how was it?
Tong’an said: A roadside shrine—everyone who sees it raises clasped hands. The monk said, After he met him,
how was it? Tong’an said: Inside the room there is no spirit-bed; the whole household wears no mourning. This monk
asked, When the mourning clothes are not worn, what then? Dongshan once said first: Take off the grease-stiffened cap,
strip off the fox-smelling cloth jacket, and become a free-and-easy patchrobed monk. Later, when monks asked him
What is Buddha? he answered, Three pounds of hemp. If you arrive at this point, then you will understand Caoshan’s
today has fulfilled mourning. This monk too was good—he wanted to see Caoshan’s conduct today.
He asked, After mourning is fulfilled, what then? Caoshan said, Caoshan likes wild drunken wine. Juefan said: The mind
is like a bright mirror; the mouth is like a drunkard. One day a monk said, Qingrui is lonely and poor, begging the master
for rescue. Caoshan called, Qingrui! The monk answered, Yes. Caoshan said, Three cups of the Bai family wine of Qingyuan,
and you still say your lips are not yet wet. There was another monk who asked Jinfeng, When the golden cup is filled and poured, what then?
Jinfeng said: Jinfeng cannot withstand deep drunkenness. Foguo commented: To understand the school by following words,
