and to respond everywhere with clear function—Jinfeng is not without that. But examined carefully, what he brought out still
leaks too much. If someone asked Jiangshan, When the golden cup is filled and poured, what then? I would only
answer him: This mountain monk has from the start kept the heavenly precept. Again a monk asked Caoshan, Through the twelve
hours, how does one preserve and sustain it? Caoshan said: It is like passing through a land of poison—one must not let even a drop of water
touch it. Caoshan was sometimes sober within drunkenness; sometimes awake
without dividing day and night. For him, the millet dream was cut off and the chambered affections forgotten. Dongshan asked
Yunju: A great icchantika kills father and harms mother—where is filial support then? Yunju said:
Only then is filial support fulfilled. This is called a person of fulfilled mourning and topsy-turvy drunkenness. Dongshan said: Before the eyes,
a wild forest—year after year free and unrestrained. Tiantong was long accustomed to wandering and playing there. The verse says:

lg.l:
A pure white household, cut off on all four sides from neighbors; year-round the gate is barred and swept, allowing no dust.If you see the cheeks from behind the head, do not associate
Suppose there is one point with nowhere to attach

Where the light turns, the waning moon tilts; when the line-signs divide, thenWhen obstruction reaches the limit, flourishing is born
the month of Jianyin is set.Yin is bleak, yang unfolds

Newly finished mourning, one immediately meets spring; drunk, the steps go wild,The tear marks are not yet goneCalling one another to swing
singing as the headcloth falls at will.Familiarity dispenses with ceremony

Hair loose, roaming at ease—who can bind him? In great peace,A thousand freedoms, a hundred easeful ways
an idle man crazed on wine.In the seven villages this fellow is merry

cb:div.p:
The master said: If sand gets in your eyes, you cannot bear it—too cramped and narrow. Caoshan said: If it is the worldly coarse heavy greed, anger, and delusion,
though hard to cut off, still they are light. But if it is idle
purity and stainless quietism, this is heavier than anything. Therefore Dongshan said:
Before the bright moon hall, it is always midsummer. The utmost Way cannot be given form. The ancients
took examples from the body nearby and from things afar, linking images and analogies to illustrate the utmost Way. Bao-
ci praised Longya’s Half-Body Verse, saying: The sun rises over linked mountains; the moon is full at the door. It is not
that there is no body; it is just not willing to show itself entirely. Both elders were descendants of Dongshan. Juefan said:
In that house style, the subtle mechanism values mutual turning, so as not to violate the central position. Their speech avoids complete fullness,
so as not to fall into present-time fixation. Yet Bao-ci’s artisan mind is uniquely wondrous, and his words do not lose the school—this is what is
precious. This line, Where the light turns, the waning moon tilts, is a metaphor for mourning fulfilled meeting spring. In the Zhou
Changes, Hexagram Qian, the second nine says, Seeing the dragon in the field; it is beneficial to see the great person. The commentary says the second nine corresponds to
the interval between Jianyin and Jianchou, when the first shoots of earth just begin to emerge.
This is precisely the meaning of yang energy appearing and issuing forth; this is as the image of the Qian hexagram should be. Old
Du in his Song of the Eight Immortals of Wine says: He drops his cap and bares his crown before princes; when summoned by the Son of Heaven,
he will not board the boat. All of them forgot form and slighted ceremony; they cannot be constrained by ordinary bounds.
Danxia Tianran one day lay crosswise on Tianjin Bridge. Zheng, the military governor, with his attendants in front,
shouted at him, but the master paid no heed. Asked about it, he slowly said: I am an idle monk. Zheng respectfully
marveled at him. At Xuefeng’s evening assembly, Xuefeng was lying in the middle courtyard. Senior monk Fushangzuo of Taiyuan
said: In all the five prefectures, only this monk is a little something. Xuefeng then got up
and left. These are all open-robed, loose-haired, idle wine-drinkers. How does Caoshan’s fulfilled mourning
function and enjoy itself? In all four seasons it is springtime wealth and honor; all things are wine and elegance.

cb:div.head: Case Seventy-Four: Fayan’s Substance and Name
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Rich with ten thousand virtues, washed clean of even a mote of dust, apart from all forms and yet precisely all
dharmas. At the top of a hundred-foot pole, step forward; the worlds of the ten directions are your whole body. Tell me, from what place
does this come?

Cited: A monk asked Fayan, The teaching says, From the root of non-abiding all dharmas are established.
What is the root of non-abiding? Fayan said: Form arises before substance;Shut that dog's mouthDo not let your eyes go dazzled
name appears before naming.In the end, what do you call it

The master said: Manjushri asked Vimalakirti, What is the root of the body? He answered, Desire is the root. Asked,
What is the root of desire? he answered, False discrimination is the root. Asked, False discrimination—
what is its root? he answered, Inverted thinking is the root. Asked, What is the root of inverted thinking? he answered,
Non-abiding is the root. Asked again, What is the root of non-abiding? he answered, Non-abiding has no root.
Manjushri then said: From the root of non-abiding all dharmas are established. Sengzhao’s commentary says: The mind
is like water. When still, it reflects; when stirred, it does not mirror. Muddy from delusion and craving, fanned by the winds of error,
it surges and billows, never dwelling even for a moment. Viewed this way, in observing dharmas, where would one not
be upside down? It is like facing a gushing spring and trying to inspect one’s original face—such a thing has never been. He also
says: If one takes the stirring of mind as root, then there is the mark of causation. But tracing life to the very first stirring,
there is no further root. If one takes non-dharma as root, then there is non-arising through a cause; yet even non-being is not apart from causes,
so again there is no root. He also says: From non-abiding comes inverted thinking; from inverted thinking comes discrimination;
from discrimination comes desire; from desire comes body. Once there is body, then good and evil
both appear. Once good and evil appear, then the myriad dharmas arise. From this point on,
words and categories cannot exhaust them. Sengzhao takes the very first stirring of thought, the root ignorance, as
the root of non-abiding. In the Transmission of the Lamp, National Teacher Qingliang, answering the Crown Prince on the essential point of mind, said: The utmost
Way is rooted in mind; the Dharma of mind is rooted in non-abiding. The substance of non-abiding mind is numinous knowing,
never obscured. National Teacher An cited the Diamond Sutra: One should produce that mind without abiding anywhere.
Not abiding anywhere means not abiding in form, not abiding in sound; not abiding in delusion, not abiding in awakening;
not abiding in essence, not abiding in function. Producing that mind means that in every place
the one mind is manifest. If one abides in good and produces mind, then good appears; if one abides in evil and produces mind, then
evil appears. Then the original mind is hidden. If one does not abide anywhere, then the worlds of the ten directions are only
one mind. The Sixth Patriarch asked Heze: You have come from afar, friend, with great hardship. Have you brought your original thing
with you? If there is an original, you should know its master—try to speak. Heze said: Taking
non-abiding as root, seeing is the master. In Heze’s Record of Revealing the School it says: Since the World-Honored One’s
passing, the twenty-eight patriarchs of the Western Heaven all transmitted the mind of non-abiding. This non-abiding
root is simply the fundamental matter itself, named non-abiding. If one fuses true and false, then the one
has many kinds, while the two are not two kinds. Fayan’s answer comes from the Treatise on the Treasury: Form arises
before substance; name appears before naming. Once form and name give signs, wandering vapors disturb the clear. Xuedou raised
his staff and said: Great assembly, this staff is form and name both presented. Form is precisely no-form;
name is precisely no-name. A whole class of blind men without understanding only recognize some no-form piece
and no-name thing and take that as the ultimate rule. They let Fayan down and go against Yongming. Chan Master Shou’s
Formula of Mind-Only says: There is not a single name that does not proclaim the Tathagata’s title; there is not a single
thing that does not reveal Vairocana’s form. Then there is another sort, narrow and ill-informed, unwilling
to investigate principle or inquire with teachers, who only say: What was there originally? Wansong said: Already too much!
They say: How can we avoid that? Wansong said: Originally what was lacking? You just
understand it this way and go on. Because he had studied Fayan and also seen Tiantong, the verse says:

lg.l:
No tracks, no traces; news cut off—white clouds without root, pureThe antelope hangs by its hornsLong burdened, never meetingThe wondrous body originally has no fixed place
wind, what color?Through the whole body, where could there be any trace

It spreads the heaven-canopy yet is not mind; it upholds the earth-carriageIt can still emerge from the mountain cleft
and yet has power.It costs no effort

It penetrates the source of a thousand ages and fashions the model of ten thousand forms;All of it flows out here
the pattern and rule.What is sealed by the one dharma

When the paths meet in dust-mote lands, everywhere are Samantabhadra’s towers and pavilions;Barricading streets and blocking alleys
their gates stand open.
At every point, Maitreya.Bumping into it and knocking against it

cb:div.p:
The master said: Look at it—without form, filling heaven and covering earth. Listen to it—without sound, yet the perfect tone has no end.
Meanwhile: though clouds have no root, vast space is marked by a patch of cloud. Though wind has no color, the great
earth is upheld by the wheel of wind. Duke Liu Yu asked Yunju, "From where does the rain come?"
Yunju said, "It comes from where the Duke asks." The Duke was delighted and thanked him. Yunju then asked,
"From where does the question come?" The Duke had no reply. Xichan was seated with an official and said, "The wind—
what color is it?" The official had no reply. Chan then asked a monk. The monk lifted his patched robe and said, "In the
prefectural storehouse cloth." Chan said, "How many bolts of silk did it use?" The monk said, "No entangling." Chan
had no reply. Yunmen answered on his behalf: "Tut! This talk falls on the teacher." Xuedou then composed two
verses together: "From where comes rain? What color is wind? At Dragon Gate, ten thousand fathoms high, a traveler once lodged.
Advancing and retreating together—who gets marked on the forehead? What color is wind? From where comes rain? No need
to snap fingers; the tower gate opens. Billowing, jagged and sharp, he has not yet returned from the south." Tiantong
versifies the root of non-abiding: white clouds have no root; what color is the clear wind? He versifies establishing all dharmas.
It scatters the heavenly canopy yet is not mind; it holds up the earth-carriage and has power. In the Mind-
King Inscription by the Great Being Good Wisdom: "Observe the mind, empty king—mysterious, subtle, hard to fathom; without form, without appearance, with great spiritual
power." The Guanzi says: water that emerges yet does not flow is called deep water; when it flows far, it is called a source.
Before high antiquity—this is the abyssal source through a thousand ages. From this the myriad forms take shape. In the Huayan,
in the Samantabhadra Practices and Vows chapter: Buddha speaks, bodhisattvas speak, lands speak, beings speak; the three times—
all speak. Also, Universal Eye does not see Samantabhadra; seeing and not seeing are both Samantabhadra
too. If where one does not see were absent, it would not be called universal. In the Entry into the Dharma Realm chapter, Maitreya
Bodhisattva goes before the tower and snaps his fingers with a sound; its gate immediately opens. He bids Sudhana
enter, and thereupon he sees completely the three-thousand great-thousand worlds, the hundred million four-continents, and the Tushita
heaven. In each and every one there is Maitreya. There is also a verse: "Maitreya, true Maitreya,
manifests in hundreds of billions of bodies, constantly appearing to people of the time; people of the time all do not recognize him." This
is all a signboard for establishing all dharmas. Do you still see Dharma Eye? "Often because at the place of seeing off
a guest, one remembers the time of parting from home."

cb:div.head: Case Seventy-Five: Ruiyan's Constant Principle
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: Calling it suchness is already change. Where knowledge does not reach, above all avoid
speaking of it. Is there still any share for investigation here, or not?

Raised: Ruiyan asked Yantou, "What is the original constant principle?"When reason is present, it is not in loudness
Yantou said, "It moves." Ruiyan said, "When it moves, then what?" Yantou said, "You do not seeThen one may know the principleA second offense will not be tolerated
the original constant principle." Ruiyan stood there thinking. Yantou said, "If you assent, you have not yet escaped the sense-roots andPrice it by the thing itselfYet he knows shame
sense-dusts; if you do not assent, you sink forever in birth and death."In this there is no road of assentIf you do not sit upright in the hall, how will you meet the two-sided occasion

The teacher said: Chan Master Shiyan of Ruiyan in Taizhou was a Min man, surname Xu. When he first asked
Yantou, he set a name and coined a term, first labeling it "original constant principle." Yantou at times
let him pass, only illuminating and shattering it by saying, "It moves." Ruiyan was lucky to escape thirty blows.
How fortunate he got off. Yet he still did not regard danger or ruin, and said, "When it moves, how is it then?"
Stroking the tiger's head, braiding the tiger's tail. Yantou, half drunk and half sober, again let him pass, only
illuminating and shattering it, saying, "You do not see the original constant principle." Such is the magnanimity of a great house, able to contain others.
Ruiyan then stood thinking. Right here he had reached the place before the dead-tree cliff where side paths are many.
Yantou was already sparing no life of his own, cutting off the running road for him, splitting open the broad avenue of Chang'an before the sun,
saying: "If you assent, you have not yet escaped roots and dusts. If you do not assent, you sink forever in birth and death."
Guishan called this the claws and fangs of the Dharma cave—seizing alive, capturing living; there is not the slightest spare-time practice
involved. A monk from Jia-shan's assembly went to Shishuang. As soon as he entered the gate he said, "I respectfully inquire." Shuang
said, "No need, reverend." The monk said, "In that case, take care." He then went to Yantou and, just as before,
did the same. Yantou merely gave two huffs. The monk said, "In that case, take care," and had just turned to leave.
Yantou said, "Though a youngster, he can still carry it through." The monk returned and related this to Jia-shan. Jia-shan
ascended the hall the next day, had the monk properly repeat the earlier exchange, and said, "Great assembly, do you understand?
If no one can speak, this old monk will not begrudge two eyebrows to speak it out."
Then he said, "Though Shishuang has a sword to kill people, he does not yet have a sword to bring people to life. Yantou, moreover,
has a sword to kill people and also a sword to bring people to life. In Linji's line this is called the seven things always at hand.
Yantou saw Ruiyan's sincere resolve in seeking instruction; it was not a time for sharp repartee and testing discrimination,
so, pitying the man and hanging down compassion, he met him with the Dharma eye. Ruiyan understood with the words, and later
he called to himself, "Master!" and said, "Do not be deceived by others!" It was because he had once met a deadly hand and forever
could not forget through kalpas. This story, past and present, no one has brought up. If not Tiantong, on whom could one rely
to appreciate it? The verse says:

lg.l:
The round pearl is unbored, the great jade block uncarved; what the Wayfarer esteems is being without edgesWhere do you set your handWhat a pity for the effort
or corners.In accord with principle, hide the edge

Lift away the road of assent, and roots and dust are empty; body dropped free, with nothing to rely on—In the twelve fields, idle traces and echoes are forgotten
vividly alive, bright and clear.Through the three thousand worlds pure light shines forth

cb:div.p:
The teacher said: In secular records, Pan Yue and Xiahou Zhan were friends; both men were of fine appearance.
Zhang Wei praised the He clan jade as lustrous and flawless, and the Sui lord's pearl as round
yet unbored. To shave a square bamboo staff round, to cut off the purple felt, to grind white jade into
an ivory comb, to hammer gold into brass ribs, to tie knots on a bowstring, to put a handle on an alms bowl—
just turn the light around and examine steadily: who is not like this? Baizhang
said, "The numinous light shines alone, far transcending roots and dust." If one assents, one has not yet escaped roots and dust.
Lift away the road of assent, and roots and dust are themselves empty. Once the six roots and six dusts are empty, the six consciousnesses
return of themselves to the sea of awakening. Anything with sharp corners cannot turn freely. If you want to be vividly
alive, clear and bright, without sticking or leaning, just place your eye on the point of assent and non-assent. Then naturally you
do not abide on this shore, do not abide on the other shore, do not abide midstream. This is why Dongshan
was half-assenting and half-not-assenting, and why Shushan's assent could not be complete. Do you yet recognize
this person's homeward reckoning? The golden lock and dark gate cannot hold him. He travels on a different road and yet
still turns in the wheel.

cb:div.head: Case Seventy-Six: Shoushan's Three Sentences
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: One sentence clarifies three sentences; three sentences clarify one sentence. Three and one do not
intermingle. The upward road is perfectly clear. Tell me: which sentence comes first?

Raised: Shoushan instructed the assembly, "If you grasp the first sentence, you can be teacher to Buddhas and patriarchs.
If you grasp the second sentence, you can be teacher to humans and gods. The thirdStill Wansong's descendantsSpoiling other people's sons and daughters
sentence grasped, you cannot even save yourself." A monk said, "Master, by which sentence do youTalking like this, that noisy fellow
grasp it?" Shoushan said, "At third watch, with the moon set, I pass through the market."You try to reckon it outThree phrases can discern one arrow flying across the sky

The teacher said: The making of the three sentences begins with Baizhang Dazhi and is rooted in the Diamond Prajna.
Baizhang said, "Teaching words are all linked in three sentences: good at first, middle, and end. At first one must directly
make him arouse a good mind; in the middle, break the good mind; only afterward is it called truly good. Thus 'a bodhi-
sattva is no bodhisattva; this is called bodhisattva.' Dharma, non-dharma, and not non-dharma—all are like this.
If one speaks only one sentence, one makes beings enter hell. If the three sentences are spoken at once,
he himself enters hell; it does not concern the teacher. To say that present illumining
awareness is one's own Buddha—that is the first good. Not clinging to present illumining awareness—that is the middle
good. Not even making an understanding of not clinging—that is the final good. Yunmen at times said:
"Within heaven, it envelops heaven and earth; in the eye's function, fine as grains, not touching any conditioned ties. How do you
bear it?" He answered for himself: "One arrowhead smashes three barriers." Yet though he had this meaning, he never
established it as three sentences. Later there was in Dingzhou, at Deshan, the ninth-generation Perfect Illumination Master,
named Yuanmi, who ascended the hall and said: "Deshan has three sentences: one sentence envelops heaven and earth,
one sentence follows the waves and rides the swells, one sentence cuts off all streams." Later in Dingzhou
Chan Master Dao of Pu'an Mountain versified these three sentences. Verse on enveloping heaven and earth: "Heaven
and earth and the myriad forms, hell and heaven too—thing by thing all are true seeing; in every case function
does no harm." Verse on cutting off all streams: "Piled mountains and heaped peaks come; each and every one is exhausted to dust.
If one still intends to discuss mystery and subtlety, ice melts and tiles shatter." Verse on following waves and riding swells:
"Eloquent mouths and sharp tongues ask; high and low are answered without fail. It is like medicine suited to illness, diagnosis
depending on the moment." Outside the three sentences, when the person himself raises the tune, how could the three sentences contain it? If there is
a question, "What is this matter?" Nan'yue and Tiantai often point to this verse as Yunmen's
composition. This is all from careless reading. Dao was heir to Deshan Mi, and Mi was heir to
Yunmen. Though Yunmen had the sayings of "enveloping heaven and earth" and "one arrowhead, three barriers," because
Master Mi brought them up and Master Dao versified them, across three generations of ancestral transmission the three sentences first became clear.
This and Dayang's three sentences, and the three mysteries and three essentials, are largely the same with minor differences. Shoushan instructed
the assembly: "If you grasp the first sentence, you are teacher to Buddhas and patriarchs." Huangbo was Nanquan's
head monk. One day he occupied Nanquan's seat. Nanquan came and asked, "Head monk, in what year did you begin
practice?" Huangbo said, "Before King Sounding-Wisdom Buddha." Nanquan said, "Still the descendants of old teacher Wang—
step down!" Huangbo then withdrew and returned to his own place to sit. Jingqing said, "Vairocana has a teacher,
the Dharma-body has a master"—this is called a person beyond Buddhas and patriarchs. Therefore it is said the first
sentence, when grasped, makes one teacher to Buddhas and patriarchs. Lingshu left behind a sealed casket saying, "The hall's
head monk is the eye of humans and gods." That head monk was Yunmen. Therefore it is said
the second sentence, when grasped, makes one teacher to humans and gods. Don't, on hearing Wansong speak like
this, then say Nanquan can only be teacher to Buddhas and patriarchs while Yunmen can only be teacher to
humans and gods. Truly, before a fool one must not speak dreams. Wansong is only
briefly citing one or two examples as models. As for those who cannot save themselves, they do not enter the ancestral
rank—what is there to say? The monk then asked, "Master, by which sentence do you grasp it?" In rotten mud there is a thorn.
Shoushan said, "At third watch, with the moon set, I pass through the market." This is a traveler
still beyond the green hills. Tiantong saw that no one dared open his mouth on this saying, so he
struck in where it pricks the hands and stabs the feet, and brought out an interwoven verse. The verse says:

lg.l:
Buddhas' and patriarchs' skulls threaded on one string; the palace clepsydra drips deep in the night, secretly passing the arrow.Let him jump as he pleasesNot allowed
For outsiders to know

The pivotal essentials for humans and gods release a thousand-catty force; cloud battalions flash and flash like swift flying lightning.Using the light to labor over the heavy
In a blink, it slips by

The person in the midst sees the turns and changes: meeting the lowly, it becomes noble; meeting the noble, it becomes lowly.The plan lies in the critical momentThe heart knows
Originally they are the same, so there is no joy or resentment

Getting the pearl through Wangxiang, ah—the utmost Way goes on and on, unbroken;When not a single thought arises, the whole body appears
gliding the blade where the ox is gone, ah—a red heart in piece after piece.Tears come forth and the guts ache

cb:div.p:
The teacher said: Only after threading the skulls of Buddhas and patriarchs on one string can one be teacher to
Buddhas and patriarchs. This may be called walking out beyond Vairocana's crown, then returning to sit on the Buddha's tongue.
"The palace clepsydra drips deep in the night, secretly passing the arrow." The Yin Kui method of water clocks says: the instrument is made in three tiers,
all circular, each one chi in diameter, set in graduated levels upon a square base and stand, with a golden dragon mouth
spouting water, which turns and pours into the mechanism of warp and weft. On top is cast a golden timekeeper,
fully clothed and capped, both hands holding an arrow. Also, in the army, secret orders are by night passed with an
arrow. This means: before signs and traces have divided, if one grasps it, one can be teacher to Buddhas and
patriarchs. But as soon as it falls into the present moment, it becomes the second level—then one makes a small resting place
on the road of humans and gods. In the Mind-Ground Contemplation Sutra, the lightning samadhi; monks call it the fleeting-flash
place. If one is a person of the midst, at times he walks atop the heads of Buddhas and patriarchs; at times he runs upon the road of humans and gods.
He moves within the herd of water buffaloes, travels among different kinds. Wang Jinggong, watching actors,
wrote a poem: "Among the players on the stage, one noble and then one base; in their hearts they know they are originally
the same, and so have neither delight nor resentment." In Zhuangzi's "Heaven and Earth" chapter, the Yellow Emperor wandered north of Red Water,
climbed Kunlun Hill, looked south, and on returning lost his dark pearl. He sent
Knowledge to seek it and could not get it; he sent Lih Zhu to seek it and could not get it; he sent Chigou
to seek it and could not get it. He sent Wangxiang, and Wangxiang got it. The Yellow Emperor said, "Strange indeed!
Wangxiang could get it." In the "Nourishing Life" chapter, Cook Ding was butchering an ox for Lord Wenhui
and said: "At the joints there are gaps, while the blade's edge has no thickness. Using what has no thickness
to enter what has gaps—vast, vast! In the play of the blade there is surely ample room. Therefore
after nineteen years, the blade is as if newly sharpened on the whetstone." Lord Wenhui said, "Excellent indeed!
Hearing Cook Ding's words, I have gained the way of nourishing life." These two stories verse the line "moon set, third watch,
passing through the market." The utmost Way is continuous and fine, like the palace clepsydra passing the arrow. A red
heart, piece by piece, is for people, like the broad free blade and like getting the pearl through Wangxiang. People nowadays
see Tiantong use Zhuangzi and then lump Lao and Zhuang together with the utmost Way. They do not know
that the ancients merely borrowed the road to pass through, only for the moment's scene. If suddenly someone comes out and says,
"Did not Zhuangzi know the place where Shoushan walked?" just say to him: "Moon set, third watch—"
passing through the market. Is this the outer section or the inner section?

cb:div.head: Case Seventy-Seven: Yangshan, According to Portion
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: If a man draws in empty space, the moment he puts down the brush he is already wrong. How much worse to set up patterns and make forms.
What can that amount to? Wansong has already exposed the tethering rope. If there is a branch, climb the branch; if there is no
branch, climb by precedent.

Raised: A monk asked Yangshan, Does master still recognize characters or not? Yangshan said, According toWhat character is this?
portion. The monk then turned once to the right and said, What character is this? YangshanWhen it comes to what is right, yield to no one.The slant is already evident.
wrote a cross on the ground. The monk turned once to the left and said, WhatAdd more strokes and dots.
character is this? Yangshan changed the cross into a swastika sign. The monk drewHalf and full are both distinguished: form, sound, derivative sense, and semantic extension.Where the gears turn, even the eye of wisdom is still confused.
a circle sign, supporting it with both hands, like an asura holding the sun and moon, and said, This is
what character? Yangshan then drew a circle sign enclosing the swastika.Look closely at the critical point.Zen monks everywhere cannot jump out of it.
The monk then made the posture of Ruci. Yangshan said, Thus, thus. You should wellThe vajra guardians outside the gate laugh at you.
protect and maintain it.The barrier is empty; it locks dreams fast and holds them firmly in hand.

The master cited this. In Cijue's Admonition to Filial Piety, the opening verse says: Before father and mother were born, congealed
there is a single round form. Even Shakyamuni did not understand it; how could Kashyapa transmit it? The Fourteenth Patriarch,
Nagarjuna, on the Dharma seat concealed his body and manifested a circle sign. Deva said, This venerable one
manifests the Buddha-body form to show us. This formless samadhi takes shape like
the full moon; the meaning of Buddha-nature is vast, empty, and bright, just like this. The making of circle signs in
the eastern land began with National Teacher Zhong, who entrusted them to his attendant Danyuan, receiving the prophecy and transmitting them
to Yangshan. Thus now they are regarded as the Guiyang house style. Monk Lianghe of Wufeng in Mingzhou
once composed forty cases; Song Mingjiao wrote a preface praising them. Liang said, Circle signs
have six general names: first, circle sign; second, sea of meaning; third, hidden device; fourth, study of characters; fifth,
language of intent; sixth, silent discourse. The Guiyang lineage record says: Danyuan told Yangshan, The National Teacher
transmitted ninety-seven circle signs of the six generations of patriarchs. Thirty years after my passing,
in the south there will be a novice who will come and greatly flourish this way. I examined this prophecy in detail;
it concerns your person. After Yangshan received them, he burned them with fire. One day Danyuan again said
to him, The transmitted circle signs should be kept deeply secret. Yangshan said, I already burned them. Danyuan
said, That works for you, but what about those who come later? Yangshan said, If the monk wants me to copy
another copy, I can. Yangshan then recopied and presented it, without a single error. One day Danyuan ascended the hall;
Yangshan came forth from the assembly, made a circle sign, supported it with his hands, presented it, then stood with hands crossed. Danyuan
used both hands to show a crossed-fist gesture. Yangshan advanced three steps and made a woman's bow. Danyuan
nodded, and Yangshan bowed. Among the ninety-seven kinds of circle signs, the crossed fists are called Rakshasa Samadhi,
and the woman's bow is called Woman Samadhi. These are all samadhis, flowing forth from the King of Samadhis
as universal manifestations. Also, there was an Indian monk who came to visit. Yangshan on the ground
drew a half-moon sign. The monk stepped forward, added to it to make a full circle, and wiped it away with his foot. Yangshan spread
both hands. The monk flicked his sleeves and went out, saying, I came to the eastern land to pay respect to Manjushri, but instead I met
a little Shakyamuni. Again, a monk finished bowing, and Yangshan paid no attention. The monk asked, master, do you still recognize
characters or not? Yangshan said, According to portion. The monk drew a circle sign and held it up. Yangshan brushed at
it with his sleeve. The monk then made a half-moon sign and held it up. Yangshan used both hands to make a backward-throwing gesture.
The monk looked at him. Yangshan lowered his head. The monk circled the master once, and Yangshan struck him. The monk
then left. Here Yangshan stands like a cliff a thousand fathoms high, no different from Deshan and Linji in their steep devices.
While Yangshan was seated, another monk came and bowed. Yangshan paid no attention. The monk asked, Master,
do you know characters? Yangshan said, According to portion. The monk turned once to the right and said, What
character is this? With setups like this, if you wait for understanding and feeling, what doctrine could it become? If there were altogether no
principle, then in west and east, ordinary and sage could not study together. A monk from Guanyin's assembly came
to visit Yantou. With his hand he made a circle sign on the left, then on the right made another circle
sign, then in the center made a third circle sign, not yet complete. Yantou brushed with his hand once.
The monk had no reply. Yantou shouted him out. As the monk was stepping across the threshold, Yantou called him back and asked,
Are you from Guanyin in Hongzhou? The monk said, Yes. Yantou asked, Then just now,
what was the circle sign on the left? The monk said, It is the phrase of being. Yantou said, And the right-side circle
sign? The monk said, It is the phrase of nonbeing. Yantou said, What about the center circle sign?
The monk said, It is the phrase of neither being nor nonbeing. Yantou said, Then what about my doing this?
The monk said, Like cutting water with a knife. Yantou beat him and drove him out. This monk did not grasp the circle-sign
purport, but vainly produced forced interpretations. If not for Yantou, he would almost have confused and misled people once.
Judging from this monk meeting Yangshan and asking whether he knew characters, then turning once to the right, his tricks
were already exhausted. Yangshan's cross annotation was already annotated; what was said was already laid bare. Why still want all the later
porridge-and-rice breath? Why wait until this point? At the start, as soon as he
asked whether the master knew characters, he should simply have said, I have always been sparse in letters, and see what he would do. Have you not seen:
Formerly there was a monk who spent his time idly. Another monk urged him, Elder brother, you are young;
it is a pity to waste every inch of time. The monk said, What exactly do you want me to do? The other urged him,
Why not read sutras? The monk said, I do not know characters. The other urged him, Why not ask someone? The monk
said, What character is this? The one urging him had no words. This can be called writing needing no punctuation, music
needing no added tones. The monk then turned once to the left and said, What character is this? This is like
the usual going from east to west and then again from west to east, slapping the left knee saying this is
the teaching intent, slapping the right knee saying this is the patriarchal intent. Are they the same or different? Yangshan shifted the stars
and turned the Dipper, changing the cross into a swastika sign. In Sanskrit asura means non-heavenly being.
In Sanskrit Rahu means obstructing, because with his hands he obstructs sun and moon. This monk drew
a circle sign like the posture of an asura holding sun and moon. Among the ninety-seven kinds of circle signs, this is called
Asura Samadhi. In Sanskrit Ruci means weeping. In the Bhadrakalpa there were a thousand Buddhas, originally
a thousand royal sons. He drew the lot last and became the final Buddha, so he wept and said, Why am I
so thinly blessed, to draw the lot at the very end? Then suddenly he laughed and said, I shall take all the adornments and skillful means of the nine hundred and ninety-nine
Buddhas. The Dharma-protecting deity now holding the pestle is he. At the end the monk
made the Ruci posture; his meaning can be known. Yangshan said, Thus, thus. This is
what all Buddhas protect and keep in mind. You are thus; I too am thus. You should guard it well. Good,
good indeed. Go well. That monk bowed in thanks, then rose into the air and departed. At that time there was a
Daoist who, having seen this, came after five days and asked. Yangshan said, Did you see it? The Daoist
said, I personally saw him leave the door and soar into the air. Yangshan said, This was a western land arhat
who came deliberately to test me. The Daoist said, Though I saw various samadhis, I cannot distinguish their
principle. Yangshan said, I will explain them to you in terms of meaning. These are eight kinds of samadhi; this is
the sea of awakening transformed into the sea of meaning. Their substance is the same, yet these meanings have cause and effect,
immediate and delayed time, total and particular, not departing from the hidden-body samadhi. Therefore it is said, Nirvana
mind is easy to attain; discriminating wisdom is hard to clarify. Just watch how Tiantong sets his hand to it.
The verse says:

lg.l:
The Way circles in emptiness, never filling; the sky-seal's character not yet formed, subtly movingCarry snow to fill the riverAbove all avoid carving
heaven's wheel and earth's axle.The balance scale is in hand

Hidden net, martial weft and civil warp; releasing and gathering.The full talent of generals and ministers
Muzhou is still here

Standing alone, moving everywhere; the pivot of mystery fires, like lightning striking the blue sky.Old Master Lao comes to life againSet your hand to it
Not equal to it
The eyes contain purple light; in broad daylight stars are seen.Shines through the four quarters of the world

cb:div.p:
The Way circles in emptiness, never filling: this is where neither man nor ox is seen, precisely when the moon is bright.
Cijue said, Who knew the very last move is actually before any division? Way-circle:
Zhuangzi says, when the hinge first obtains its ring-center, it responds without end. Tiantong borrows this to praise the circle
sign held up in presentation. The sky-seal's character: though the cross was changed into the swastika sign, in truth it is not
a worldly character to be grasped. Daofu replied to Bodhidharma, As I see it, one does not
cling to words, yet does not leave words, but uses them for the Way. Chan leader Kuan of Daning came
to Fachang and met the elder making this sign. Kuan then went out to work. The next day, ascending the hall,
before the Dharma seat he said, How about yesterday's case? Kuan made this sign and then
again wiped it away with his foot. Chang said, Chan leader Kuan did not get his name in vain. Then he ascended the seat
and said, Suddenly in a clear sky a thunderclap roars. At Yu Gate the three-foot waves crash. How many
horned heads turn into dragons and go; shrimps and crabs still strain their eyes as before. This verse and Tiantong's
pivot of mystery firing, blue-sky lightning, are of one company. Heaven's wheel and earth's axle, martial weft and civil
warp, all are the two circles right and left, the bloodline of cross and swastika sign. Releasing and gathering:
standing alone and moving everywhere praises the asura holding the sun, and Ruci raising fists encircling the swastika sign, praising
Well done indeed. The Chunqiu title gloss says: Heaven in its substance envelops earth within; sun,
moon, stars, and constellations belong to it. Yet heaven and earth have forms of high and low; the four seasons have rising
and falling principles; sun and moon have measured courses; stars have fixed stations.
Even all the stars revolve, their circuits like a wheel; therefore it is called heaven's wheel. The Hetu
Guadi Xiang says: Beneath the earth are eight pillars, each one hundred thousand li wide. There are three thousand
six hundred axles, pulling and restraining one another; famous mountains and great rivers, caves and openings, all connect. The Family Sayings says:
East-west on earth is weft; north-south is warp. Also, civil order can warp heaven, martial power can weft
earth. Without civil order there is no way to bring the distant near; without martial power there is no way to resist disorder. Muzhou instructed the assembly:
Splitting open is in me; gathering up is also in me. A monk asked, What is splitting
open? Zhou said, Three nines are twenty-seven: bodhi, nirvana, true suchness, liberation, the very
mind is Buddha. I speak thus for now; how do you speak? The monk said, I
would not speak thus. Zhou said, The cup falls to the ground and the dish breaks into seven pieces. The monk said,
What is gathering up? Zhou drew in his hands and sat. Laozi says: Silent and still,
standing alone and not changing; moving everywhere and not failing. The pivot's activation is like lightning
or flint-fire. When the eye has spiritual light, it is called cliff-lightning. Seeing stars in daylight is like
a tree's shadow in darkness or fish traces at the bottom of water, not something the flesh-eye can see. Jiaofan wrote to Lingyuan:
Tree shadows in darkness are the intent of a lifetime; fish tracks at the water's edge are the function after illness. I imagine
your gaunt form without attachments, leaning on a vine and idly watching evening clouds return. Do you yet know where Yangshan
walked and stood? A black hen embroidered on black satin in the deep night; a single thread in the dark is truly hard to pass through.

cb:div.head: Case Seventy-Eight: Yunmen's Flour Cake
cb:div.p:
Instruction to the assembly: Bargaining with heaven for a price, grappling with earth in repayment, scheming a hundred ways and pursuing them all ends in one whole
muddle. Is there anyone who knows advance and retreat, and understands fortune and misfortune?

Raised: A monk asked Yunmen, What is speech that transcends Buddha and goes beyond the patriarchs? Yunmen said, Flour cake.This question is too lofty
AriseIn one lift, forty-nine

The master said: Yunmen once ascended the hall and said, As soon as one word is raised, a thousand differences share one track. It includes and gathers
even dust motes, yet this is still talk of the gate of transformation. But what should a patchrobed monk do? If
you bring patriarchal intent and Buddha intent to discuss here, the whole road to Caoxi sinks flat. Is there
anyone who can say it? Speak, if you can. A monk asked, What is speech that transcends Buddha and goes beyond the patriarchs?
Yunmen said, Flour cake. The monk said, What has this got to do with it? The master
said, Obviously, what does it have to do with it? Then he said, Do not make this into something. When you hear people
mention the patriarchs' meaning, you immediately ask about the principle of speech transcending Buddha and surpassing patriarchs. First just
tell me, what do you call Buddha? What do you call patriarch? Then you talk of transcending Buddha and surpassing patriarchs.
Then you ask about leaving the triple world. Bring me the triple world here. What seeing,
hearing, awareness, or knowing is obstructing you? What sounds, forms, or dharmas are there for you to settle? Settle
what bowl, exactly? Another monk asked, What is speech that transcends Buddha and goes beyond the patriarchs?
Yunmen said, Ephedra from Puzhou, aconite from Yizhou. He also said, Come, come, let me further
ask you all: you shoulder your staffs crosswise and say, I study Chan and learn the Way, then seek
some principle of transcending Buddha and surpassing patriarchs. I ask you: throughout the twelve hours, walking, standing,
sitting, lying down, shitting and pissing, even worms in the privy pit, buying and
selling over the butcher's mutton block in the market, is there any principle there of transcending Buddha and surpassing patriarchs? Foguo
said: Some make a circle sign here, adding mud on top of dirt, adding cangues and wearing shackles. Wansong
said: If you want the hammer and chisel that break cangues and smash shackles, ask Tiantong's verse on the ancient case. The verse
says:

lg.l:
Flour cake, he says, is speech beyond Buddhas and patriarchs; tasteless in the phrase, how will you investigate?The whole canon with all its commentaries cannot reach it
Where can one open the mouth

If one day a patchrobed monk knows what fullness is, only then will he see Yunmen's faceOnly then do you know flour cakes, ghee, and poison are one
without shame.Yunmen has no eyes to see people

cb:div.p:
The master said: Chan Master Chu of Dongshan, pointing out the penetrating function, composed a verse saying: Dongshan is desolate and solitary, a
There is nothing to grasp; tasteless talk chokes off people's mouths. Even if there are a thousand clever dishes,
what can be done when the full do not care for them? But if they are hungry ghosts greedily gobbling, biting crosswise and lengthwise,
it is like dogs gnawing dry bones. Only after they chew their tongues raw do they throw them aside.
Then they may meet Yunmen. After meeting, what then? Each and every one has a face three
inches thick.

cb:div.head: Case Seventy-Nine: Changsha Advances a Step
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: On the Golden Sand bank, Lady Ma-lang is a spirit of another sort. In a crystal bottle
pounding cakes inside, who dares stir it? If you do not enter startling waves, it is hard to meet a fish to your liking.
Stride with broad steps; in one phrase, how is it?

Raised: Changsha had a monk ask Master Hui, How was it before seeing Nanquan?
Hui was silent for a long while. The monk said, How was it after seeing him? Hui said,In the morning there is porridgeAsk about it and at once it stinks like shitAnd he even picks at it further
It cannot be otherwise. The monk returned and reported this to Changsha. ChangshaHe just stumbles into the shit heapA fellow loose with mouth and tongue
said, One sitting atop a hundred-foot pole, though he has gained entry, he has not yetUnder the pole, one whole bout of vexation
made it real. At the top of the hundred-foot pole, he must advance one more step; the ten directionsWhen solitary peril is not set up, the Way is truly highWhat could be so great as this letting go
world is the whole body. The monk said, How does one advanceOnly then believe the meditation cushion is not heaven
at the top of the hundred-foot pole? Changsha said, The mountains of Langzhou, the waters of Lizhou. The monk said, I do notThere truly is this hereBump into it and strike against it
understand. Changsha said, Within the king's transforming rule are the four seas and five lakes.Clever indeedJump as you please

Wansong said: Great Master Zhaoxian of Changsha in Hunan, personal name Jingcen. Juefan said: Among Chan masters,
he was Mazu's grandson, Nanquan's son, and Zhaozhou's elder brother. At that time among patchrobed monks,
even one as formidable as Yangshan ranked beneath him and called him Great Tiger Cen. Ascending
the hall, he said: If I only keep expounding the school and teaching, grass will grow a yard deep in the Dharma hall.
But with no choice, I tell you all: The entire world of the ten directions is the monk's
eye. The entire world of the ten directions is the monk's whole body. The entire world of the ten directions is
one's own radiance. The entire world of the ten directions is within one's own radiance. The entire
world of the ten directions has not a single person who is not oneself. I often tell you all: The buddhas of the three
times, together with beings of the dharma realm, are the light of great prajna. Before that light shines forth,
where do you beings rely? Before the light shines forth, there is not yet even news of buddhas
or beings. From where then come mountains, rivers, and lands? Changsha had a monk ask
the hermit-master Hui. This hermit was one under Nanquan who did not appear in the world, one of hidden accord and secret realization.
In the Lamp Record he is listed at the end among those with no recorded encounters or sayings. Yet since this story exists,
making a biography for him would not be excessive. The monk acted as a special envoy and went to see the hermit-master,
conveying Changsha's meaning: Hermit-master, how was it before seeing Nanquan? Hui remained
silent. The monk pressed on: How was it after seeing Nanquan? Hui said, It cannot be any different
at all. Wansong said: One death, no second life. The monk returned and reported to Changsha, and Changsha spoke a verse,
saying: One sitting atop a hundred-foot pole, though he has entered, it is not yet real. This and
Yantou saying Xuefeng and Deshan did not understand the final phrase are the same ailment. Wan-
song often says to people: It is like someone taking the ancestral family gates, the estate, and
even his kin and his own body, selling them all in one contract, and buying a crystal bottle, which all day long
he guards wherever he goes, like his own eyes. If Wansong sees it, he will surely smash
it for him, making him let go and swing his arms, becoming a carefree fellow with no taboos. Better than silently
saying: Let go beneath the cliff, divide the body amid the myriad forms. Only then are the mountains of Langzhou and the waters of Lizhou
and the four seas and five lakes within the king's transforming rule fit to match Tiantong's water buffalo
dragging the plow. The verse says:

lg.l:
The jade man wakes from a dream at a single rooster's cry; with a turn of the eye, all modes of life are equally complete.Open the eyes and do not notice dawnEndless
The treasury cannot be exhausted in use

Trust it: wind and thunder urge the hibernating out; wordless, peach and plum naturallyThe seasons do not spare one another
form a path.When water arrives, the channel is formed

In season, put strength into plowing and tilling; who fears the spring fields burying the shinsThe one who avoids it does not do it
in mud?The one who does it does not avoid it

cb:div.p:
Wansong said: Tiantong attained the samadhi of transcending forms and briefly passed over the hermit-master's tight clutch on the pole,
the place where he dared not move. If you stir it and just keep going like that, grass in the Dharma hall
grows a yard deep. Upagupta once had a man attached to body-view seek deliverance from the patriarch. The patriarch
said: In seeking liberation, you must trust my words and not go against my instruction. The man said: Since
I have come to rely on a teacher, of course I should obey. The patriarch then conjured a perilous cliff, with a tall tree rising on it,
and had him climb the tree. Then beneath the tree he conjured a great pit, a thousand cubits deep and wide. The patriarch
told him to let go his feet. The man obeyed and let go both feet. Told to let go one hand, he immediately let go
one hand. Told to let go the other hand too, the man replied: If I let go again, I will fall into the pit
and die. The patriarch said: You agreed first to obey; how can you now defy me? At that moment the man's attachment to the body
vanished at once. He let go and fell, but saw no tree and no pit, and immediately realized the fruit of the Way. Changsha's
Mountains of Langzhou, waters of Lizhou is called skillful use of the perilous-cliff phrase. If not for the jade man
waking from the dream and having another mode of life, how could there be brand-new sun and moon across the four seas and five lakes?
In the Mao Poems, the soft valley wind stirs the awakening from hibernation. In the first pentad after the spring equinox, thunder then
sounds. In the Han History, in the appraisal of Li Guang: Peach and plum do not speak, yet beneath them a path forms itself. The Zong-
jing says: Once virtue and conduct are stored within, trust arises without words, like peach and plum naturally making a path.
Again, Mountains of Langzhou, waters of Lizhou: this saying is a matter of dragging mud and trailing water.
When Sansheng was with him, he had Senior Monk Xiu ask Changsha: When Nanquan passed away, to what place did he go?
Changsha said: Shitou, when a novice, went to see the Sixth Patriarch. Xiu said:
I am not asking about Shitou as a novice seeing the Sixth Patriarch. When Nanquan passed away,
where did he go? Changsha said: I am telling you to go ponder it. Xiu said: Master, you only
have a thousand-foot cold pine, but no stone shoot putting out branches. Changsha did not reply. Xiu said, Thanks
for the master's answer. Changsha still did not reply. Xiu reported this to Sansheng. Sansheng said: If it was really like that,
it still surpasses Linji's seven steps. Sansheng personally went to the abbot's room and said: Master, your earlier answer
may be called illuminating the past and cutting off the future. Changsha did not reply. Sansheng said: I have long
suspected this fellow. Foyin's verse says: A traveler meets Changsha and they share the same road; it makes one
dimly probe the family style. Sumeru, ten thousand fathoms, grinds past and present; breaking grass to measure the sky is vain
effort. Where the ancients held fast, they could let go and move, advancing on the pole's tip; when letting go and moving,
they could still hold fast, sheer as a wall a thousand fathoms high. Why are they so free and at ease? In Hu-
nan city it is easy to nourish the people; rice is cheap and firewood plenty for all the neighbors.

cb:div.head: Case Eighty: Longya Brings the Board
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Great sound is rarefied; great vessels are completed late. Amid full bustle and a hundred clamors,
feign dullness and wait seven times seven years, a thousand years after. Slow down. Tell me, what kind of
person is this?

Raised: Longya asked Cuiwei, What is the Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West? Cuiwei said, Bring me the meditation board. Longya took the board and handed it toEach time it is brought up
Each time it is newActing from capital to seek profit
Cuiwei. Cuiwei received it and immediately struck him. Longya said, Strike if you like,Dull and fixedClearly knowing it is so
but there still is no Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West. He also asked Linji, HowHalf approving, half not approving
is the Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West? Linji said, Bring me the sitting cushion. Longya took the cushion and handed it to Linji; Linji received it and struck him,Tough skin and mangy fleshA good edition
Mostly the sameMake the mistake and follow through with itWith a smooth hand, disjoint the bones
and Longya said, Strike if you like, but there still is no Patriarch's meaning. Later, when Longya took up residence,
So soft and stubborn
a monk asked, When you formerly asked Cuiwei and Linji about the Patriarch's meaning, did those two venerable ones
understand or not? Longya said, They understood indeed, but there still is noA poor boy broods over old debts
Patriarch's meaning.A burnt brick strikes and even the bottom freezes

Wansong said: Chan Master Juidun of Longya Mountain in Hunan first studied with Cuiwei and Linji,
and later studied with Deshan and Dongshan. One day he asked Dongshan, What is the Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West?
Dongshan said, When the stream of the cave flows backward, then I will tell you. At this the master awakened
and entered. Foguo said: When Longya fetched the meditation board back then, how could he not know they would hit
him? After he became abbot, a monk asked, Regarding those two venerable ones, did you approve of them or not?
Longya said, I approved of them, yes, but still there is no Patriarch's meaning. Foguo said:
This mountain monk is not so. If I approve, I still do not approve; but there still is no Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West.
Chan Master Fori Gao's verse says: Su Wu did not bow to the Chanyu; from first to last he always followed
the Han emperor's rites. Only after snow do you know the pine and cypress character; only in hard things do you see a true man.
Xuedou consistently pressed Longya down. Zhenru Zhe said: Cuiwei and Linji may truly be called proper
lineage masters. Longya's sort merely parts grass and peers at the wind, serving later people as
a tortoise shell for divination. Citing the monk's question after Longya became abbot, he then said: Longya looked ahead and behind,
matching illness with medicine. Great Wei would not be so. If asked whether those two venerable ones understood,
he would strike straight across the back. Not only would this support Cuiwei and Linji, it also would not
fail the one who came asking. Wansong said: Zhenru is exactly Linji's hammer-and-tongs style and cannot
let it pass. To see the magic tally hidden behind Longya's elbow, you must use Tiantong's eye
from the proper line. The verse says:

lg.l:
Cushion and meditation board faced Longya; why, at the moment, did he not act as a master?For a secure plan, one accepts the butcher's lotBites people
The dog does not bare its teeth

Unintended, it became stripping beneath clear eyes; he feared it might spread abroad and drift to the ends of the earth.If a person has no foresight
There must be troubles near at hand
How could empty space hang a sword? Yet across the Milky Way a raft still floats.No need for spears and halberdsThere is another road that goes beyond

Unsprouted grass can hide a fragrant elephant; a bottomless basket can hold a living snake.Even the Buddha eye cannot spy itThe same
But how I handle it for you is different

In today's rivers and lakes, what obstruction is there? At the crossing for those who know the way there areIn peace there are no taboos
boats and carts.What place is not graceful and free

cb:div.p:
Wansong said: Longya's cushion and meditation board, with Cuiwei and Linji before the assembly telling him to bring them,
why could he not use them? After Baizhang had told the wild fox story, Huangbo asked: If an ancient
made one wrong reply and fell into a fox body for five hundred lives, then if every reply were correct,
what should happen? Baizhang said, Come closer and I will tell you. Huangbo stepped forward and first gave
Baizhang a slap. Baizhang said, I thought only the fox's beard was red; here is another red-bearded fox. This is truly
mastery in the moment. Longya was not lacking in mastery, but he did not intend to strip it bare beneath clear eyes.
He did not want, at the critical moment, thunder rushing and lightning sweeping, all at once severe and precipitous. An old poem says: A thousand miles of color in the mid-autumn moon,
the sound of a hundred thousand troops in the midnight tide. This is called a cold beggar, with nothing held
back. A monk asked Jingqing, This student has not yet reached the source; please, Master, use an expedient. Jingqing
said, What source? The monk said, The source. Jingqing said, If it is the source, how could it
receive expedients? The attendant asked, Just now, did you strip it for him? Jingqing said, No. The attendant
then said, Then did you fail to strip it for him? Jingqing said, No. The attendant said, What then is the venerable master's meaning?
Jingqing said, A single dot of watery ink makes dragons in two places. Stripping it is still a kind of completing and wrapping up,
and I fear that if it spreads around it will disgrace the family style. Dongshan entrusted Caoshan, saying:
At my late teacher Yunyan's place, I personally received the seal of the Precious Mirror Samadhi, whose matter reaches the essential point.
I now transmit it to you. Guard and uphold it well; do not let it be cut off. Later, if you meet a true
vessel of Dharma, only then may you transmit it. It must be kept hidden and secret, not outwardly displayed, for I fear that if it is
spread abroad, later people will be hard to receive. A monk asked Tong'an Cha, What is no weapons of war? Tong'an
said, Empty space does not hang a sword; the jade rabbit is not covered in scales. It is said in worldly tradition that the heavenly river and
the sea connect. On the seashore, every year in the eighth month, a floating raft comes and goes without fail. The learned
Marquis Zhang Qian then brought ample provisions and rode the raft away. Hurried on, he did not notice
day or night. Suddenly he arrived at a place and saw city walls and houses. In the houses were many weaving women.
There was only one man, leading an ox by the water's edge. Startled, he asked, Who has come here? Zhang Qian
asked, What place is this? He said, You may go to Shu and ask Yan Junping. He did as he was told,
and Junping said, In such-and-such year and month there was a guest star that trespassed on Ox and Dipper. So the tale was recorded.
The Han History records only that Zhang Qian traced the river source, speaking of the distance of his mission; in truth it has no heavenly river
account. Only the Bowu zhi says a man carried provisions and rode a raft to the heavenly river, where he saw
the man watering an ox, and when he asked Junping, the guest star offending Ox and Dipper was this very man. This
praises how Longya, at the moment of use, let it pass, and after letting it pass, made a different mastery. A monk
asked Caoshan, Why can unsprouted grass hide a fragrant elephant? Caoshan said, Reverend,
lucky you are a master hand. He also asked Caoshan, What is unsprouted grass? A bottomless basket. This is
Longya's great function of not using. Thus the fragrant elephant is not trampled by donkeys and mules, and the living snake
does not die beneath words. Longya addressed the assembly: Those who travel and study must penetrate buddhas and patriarchs
before they can do so. Master Xinfeng said, The words and teachings of buddhas and patriarchs are like living enemies; only then is there
a share in study. If you cannot penetrate them, you are deceived by buddhas and patriarchs. A monk then asked, Do buddhas
and patriarchs still have a mind to deceive people or not? Longya said, Tell me, in the rivers and lakes is there still
an intention to obstruct people? It is also said: Though rivers and lakes have no intention to obstruct people, because people of the time
cannot get through, the rivers and lakes become an obstruction to them. You cannot simply say rivers and lakes do not obstruct
people. Longya penetrated the ancestral intent as if facing a sworn enemy. Therefore it is said: Clear is clear,
indeed; yet in the end there is no meaning of the Patriarch coming from the West. How could rivers and lakes obstruct people? A common saying
says: If you yourself cannot float, you blame the bends of the river. An old adept said: If you yourself
cannot float, you blame the pit for being hot.

cb:div.head: Case Eighty-One: Xuansha Arrives at the County
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Move, and shadows appear; awaken, and dust is born. Raised up, it is clear; set down,
it is steady and hidden. When genuine people of the Way meet, how do they speak?

Case: Xuansha arrived at Putian County, and a hundred entertainments welcomed him. The next day he asked Elder Xiaotang,
Yesterday there was so much noise and bustle. Where did it all go? XiaotangMaking a racket again
lifted up the corner of his robe. Xuansha said: That is completely unrelated.Sure enough, hands and feet in a flurryThanks for the proof

The teacher said: Chan Master Xuansha Zongyi of Fuzhou, personal name Shibei, wore straw sandals and cloth robes,
ate plain food and was content. Xuefeng esteemed his ascetic practice highly and often called him Bei the dhutanga monk.
It is said in the world: Xuansha did not go beyond the ridge; Baoshou did not cross the river. Once he stumbled and injured a toe.
He sighed and said: This body is not truly existent, so whence comes pain? This body is suffering; ultimately there is no
birth. Enough, enough. Bodhidharma never came to the East; the Second Patriarch never went to the West. Thus he turned back.
Later, because of reading the Shurangama, he had an awakening. Therefore his responses to situations were swift and keen, and in accord with the sutras.
Even when questioning and debating with Xuefeng, he would not yield. Xuefeng said: Head-Monk Bei
is one who has come again. The Min commander Wang Shenzhi and Lord Wang Yanbin both treated the master
with reverence. His assembly regularly numbered eight or nine hundred. When Xuansha came to Putian County, a hundred entertainments welcomed him. The next
day he asked Elder Xiaotang: Yesterday there was so much bustle and noise; where did it all go?
Xiaotang lifted up the corner of his robe. Not bad, sharp and steep. First of all, you must not fix your gaze on
noise and stillness, on yesterday and today. If you do, you miss the great matter right before you.
Xiaotang spent no effort of mind, just casually picked up his robe. Xuansha said: Picked up, it is
unrelated. Where is this place of no relation for Xiaotang? Did Xuansha approve or not?
Dawei Zhe said: Dawei is not like that. If someone asks, I simply snap my fingers once.
If a patch-robed monk comes forth and says, Picked up, it is unrelated, then I would approve him. Why?
Because for a great man, even stroking a tiger's whiskers is part of his proper work. But tell me, where do advantage and danger lie?
In what place? He also said: Xiaotang harbors a supreme treasure; meeting a discerner only adds to its brilliance.
Xuansha's fundamental hammer and chisel struck once, and its light flowed through a thousand ages. Fayan separately said:
How much noise was there yesterday? Fadeng said: Today is even more laughable. Look at these
two venerable ones, true descendants of that line, who saw through Xuansha's move. All along they
spread a broad net outside while closing in within, shooting hidden arrows at each other. Tiantong had eyes that see all sides,
and seeing all their long and short points, he sang it out to the bottom. The verse says:

lg.l:
In the night ravine a boat is hidden; on clear depths an oar is set. Dragons and fish do not yet know water is theirPatch-robed monks are hard to foolWilling to fall into dead water
life.The one in the game is confused

With a broken switch, no harm in stirring once for sport. Master Xuansha and old XiaotangBeat the grass and startle the snake
Receive it all under one charge

Lid and box, arrow point, probing pole and shadowing grass; in hidden withdrawal tooEasy to open the mouth at beginning and endHard to keep a steadfast heart in winter cold
an old turtle nests in the lotus.Where the body is hidden, no trace remains
In play as well, bright-scaled fish sport among the waterweeds.Where no trace remains, do not hide the body

cb:div.p:
The teacher said: Xuansha asking about yesterday's bustle is like what the Great Master chapter of Zhuangzi says:
If one hides a boat in a ravine and hides a mountain in a marsh, one calls it secure. Yet at midnight a strong man
may carry it off and run, and the dim-witted do not know it. Hiding small and large things has its proper way, yet still
there is somewhere for them to escape. But if one hides all under heaven within all under heaven, and there is nowhere to escape,
that is the great truth of constant things. Tiantong used Xuansha's question of yesterday and today,
secretly carrying off the hidden boat to test Xiaotang. Yet he still came back to row a lonely boat on clear and deep waters.
In the Inscription of the Mystery, this fears stagnation in dead water. In Xuansha's phrase the eye is alive, wanting
people to recognize the root-source of movement and stillness. Monk Wolong Qiu said: If you want to grasp the essence, it is precisely
mountains, rivers, and the great earth that reveal it to you. Since this matter is constant, it can also be brought to completion.
If you enter through Manjushri's gate, all conditioned things, earth, wood, tile, and stone assist you
in arousing your function. If you enter through Guanyin's gate, all sounds, shrimps and earthworms,
assist you in arousing your function. If you enter through Samantabhadra's gate, you arrive without moving a step. I
use these three gates as expedient means to show you, like taking a single broken switch and stirring the great ocean
waters, making those fish and dragons know that water is life. If you constantly know the root-source of movement and stillness, speech and silence, coming
and going, then early on you will not pass by empty. This verse is where Xuansha worked for people. If one is a true adept,
lid and box, arrow point, probing pole and shadowing grass, holding fast and letting go—look well at lifting the robe
and at unrelated. See what principle this is. In the Records of the Historian, in the Treatise on Tortoise Divination, the Grand Historian
said: When I went to Jiangnan and observed their practices, they said that a turtle at a thousand years then roams upon lotus
leaves. This is unrelated. In hidden withdrawal it does no harm to play; in play
it does no harm to withdraw and hide. Now in painting fish-and-weed scenes, the weeds are water plants; swaying with the waves,
they naturally form patterns. Also, zao means water plants with markings. It is seen in the Analects: Mountain
brackets and painted beam-heads. Do you want to know these two old men? When there is spirit, they add more spirit; where there is no elegance,
there too they are elegant.

cb:div.head: Case Eighty-Two: Yunmen on Sound and Form
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Not cutting off sound and form is to fall wherever you are. Seeking by sound and seeing by form, one does not see
the Thus Come One. Is there anyone who returns home right on the road?

Case: Yunmen addressed the assembly, saying: Hear sound and awaken to the Way; see form and clarify the mind.Two pellets plug the ears
Guanyin Bodhisattva brings money to buy a flat cake. When he puts down his hand, insteadTwo leaves cover the eyes
it turns out to be a steamed bun.Again blown by the wind into another tune

The teacher said: Tiantong cited the saying and spoke of its spirited excellence, omitting its dark and yellow details. In the original record, Yunmen
addressed the assembly and said: Hear sound and awaken to the Way; see form and clarify the mind. How is it
hearing sound and awakening to the Way, seeing form and clarifying the mind? He raised his hand and said: Guanyin Bodhisattva brings money
to buy a flat cake. He puts down his hand, and originally it is a steamed bun. National Teacher Yuantong
said: The old man of Shaoyang may be said to sing ever higher while fewer can harmonize. Now instead,
on the tip of Yansheng's whisk, he enters the samadhi of the square-net. In the east one enters concentration, in the west
one rises; even to the point that a male body enters concentration and a female body rises. Do you understand? The colors of the wild are no longer
cut off by mountains; moonlight goes straight through and joins with the water. Wansong said: Search in the sea,
and you do not find it; on the shore instead you meet it. Look further—where does Tiantong meet it? The verse
says:

lg.l:
Going out the gate, mounting a leaping horse, he sweeps away baleful stars; the dust and smoke of ten thousand lands are of themselves stilled and cleared.The general's command beyond the borderWind moves
Grass bends down

The twelve fields vanish, idle shadows and echoes gone; throughout the three-thousand worlds pure light shines forth.Merged into one familyNo longer any
Two kinds

cb:div.p:
The teacher said: Hear sound and awaken to the Way—does the Way have sound? See form and clarify the mind—does the mind have
form? This is ritual, music, campaigns, and punishments issuing from the Son of Heaven; the army of benevolence and righteousness is without enemy under
heaven. The baleful stars and broom stars, their omen is one and the same. Taking sound and form as shadows and echoes indicates
their unreality. Shadow means mirror-images and moon in water; echo means sound transmitted in an empty valley. All these
within the Way-mind are baleful stars. Ten thousand lands are like the ten thousand dharmas. The twelve fields
are like the six faculties and six objects. The light of the three-thousand worlds illumines and shatters shadows and echoes. By removing
shadows and echoes, light is released. Have you not seen Baizhang and Guling say: The numinous light shines alone,
far transcending faculties and dusts. But if suddenly every faculty and every dust pervades the Dharma realm, then how
is it then? You were about to call it a steamed bun, but instead it is a flat cake.

cb:juan.cb:jhead: Old Man Wansong's Commentary on Tiantong Jue's Verses on the Ancient Cases, Record of the Congrong Hermitage, Volume Five

Old Man Wansong's Commentary on Tiantong Jue's Verses on the Ancient Cases
Record of the Congrong Hermitage, Volume Six

cb:div.head: Case Eighty-Three: Daowu Visits the Sick
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: The whole body is made into sickness; even Vimalakirti is hard to cure. This herb can heal; Manjushri
knows how to use it well. How does this compare to investigating and attaining a place of ease and joy from the person beyond? What is
the place of ease and joy?

Case: Weishan asked Daowu, Where have you come from? Daowu said: From visitingThe place it comes from must be clear
the sick. Weishan said: How many are sick? Daowu said: There areThe foremost field of merit is not absentYet you still want two grades
the sick and the not sick. Weishan said: Is the one not sick perhaps Zhitouduo?Then it is you who have a second moon
Daowu said: Sickness and not sickness altogether have nothing to do with him.The device for trapping a tiger
Speak quickly, speak quickly. Weishan said: Even if spoken, it is still unrelated.Yet the gourd gets tangled backward in the vineCalamity does not enter the cautious
Household gate

Chan Master Lingyou of Weishan in Tanzhou visited Baizhang Dazhi at age twenty-three.
He served as cook for twenty years, and because of poking the fire he awakened to the Way. Later, with Hualin, he wagered over the pure
bottle phrase and won Weishan. The regional commander Li Jingrang memorialized and obtained the temple name Tongqing Monastery.
Minister Pei Xiu once consulted him on profound mysteries. Once the master saw a wildfire and asked Daowu, Do you see the fire or not?
Daowu said: I see it. Weishan said: Where does it arise from? Daowu said: Put aside
walking, standing, sitting, and lying down—please, Master, ask another question. Weishan let it go. Fojian commented:
Blazing wildfire—everyone sees it, but only Daowu saw it in a way utterly
different. Wansong said: Though both are visiting the sick, it is not like Daowu, who peered through heart, liver,
and all the organs. Weishan was skilled in expedients; right there on the mat he urged him on, saying: Even if you speak it, with him it is
unrelated. Only Tiantong said: Precisely at the place of no relation, that is the right place to speak. The verse
says:

lg.l:
The wondrous medicine has never passed the mouth; even a divine physician cannot seize the hand. IfCannot swallow it, cannot spit it outNowhere to feel for it
it exists, it was never truly absent.They only speak of all under heaven

In utmost emptiness, it was never truly present.Yet do not see a single filament
Hair's breadth

Not extinguished yet alive, not perishing yet long-enduring, it wholly transcendsEmpty like the valley spirit, ever undyingThe Way precedes the Lord on High and lives long of itself
before King Sound of Awe.Unrolled, it does not reach the head

It walks alone after the kalpa is empty. In stillness it becomes level: heavenRolled up, it does not reach the tail
covers, earth supports.Hold fast heaven and earth
In movement it turns and revolves: the crow flies, the rabbit runs.Turn and transform creation

cb:div.p:
The teacher said: The red-thread pulse is cut off; medicine and sickness are both gone. Taking medicine, one forgets the mouth; feeling the pulse,
one forgets the hand. This is the sickness that cannot be pieced together. Hua Tuo folds his hands;
Bian Que knits his brows. To say it is, it penetrates through, yet the body has no image. To say it is not, throughout the whole realm it is never
hidden. In the Qi dynasty, Master Tanluan obtained ten scrolls of immortal scriptures. Later he met Tripitaka Bodhiruci
and asked: In the Buddha Dharma, is there a method of long life and deathlessness better than the immortality arts of this land?
The Tripitaka spat on the ground and said: How can there be a method of long life in this land? Even if one gains
extended years, when the karmic reward is exhausted one still falls again. He then drew out the Contemplation Sutra of Infinite Life from his robe and gave it
to Luan, saying: This is the great immortal formula. One always attains liberation and forever leaves birth and death. In the Shurangama,
when the rewards of the ten immortals are exhausted, they return and enter the various destinies again. Laozi said: To die yet not perish
is long life. Su Dongpo wrote in a poem to Foyin: I have no leisure yet to study long life; for now study long not-dying.
Not dying wholly transcends before King Sound of Awe, prior to heaven and not yet formed; once formed, it walks alone after the kalpa
is empty, after heaven, already ruined yet not ruined. In stillness it becomes level: heaven covers, earth supports.
In movement it turns and revolves: the crow flies, the rabbit runs. This is called the functioning of the whole body, gathering function back to
essence. Stillness is the root of heaven and earth; movement accords with the mind of sages and worthies. Do you understand this kind of talk
or not? Open up the wondrous, pure, perfectly bright eye, and recognize the person of auspicious ease and joy.

cb:div.head: Case Eighty-Four: Judi's One Finger
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: One hearing, a thousand awakenings; one understanding, a thousand follow. A superior person decides once and all is
settled. Those of middling and lower capacity hear much and trust little. At the precise and apt point, try to bring it forth and see.
Case: Whenever Master Judi was questioned, he only raised one finger.Why spend so much effort

The teacher said: Chan Master Judi of Jinhua Mountain in Wuzhou first lived in a hermitage at Tiantai. There was
a nun named Shiji. She came wearing a bamboo hat and carrying a staff. She circled the master three times and said: If you can speak it,
I will take off my hat. She asked three times, and Judi had no reply at all. Shiji then left. Judi said:
It is getting late in the day; stay the night. Shiji said: If you can speak it, then I will stay. Judi again had no
In reply. After Jì left, Juzhi sighed to himself, Though I have a man's form, I lack a man's
spirit. He planned to leave the hermitage and go study in various places. That night the mountain spirit told him,
Master, you need not leave this mountain. A great bodhisattva will come and preach the Dharma for you,
indeed. Sure enough, after about ten days Master Tianlong arrived. Juzhi welcomed and bowed with utmost sincerity, and fully recounted the earlier
events. Tianlong raised one finger to show him. Juzhi was greatly awakened on the spot. From then on, whenever a monk
came, he only raised one finger, with no other instruction. A boy attendant he kept was outside and was asked by someone,
What essential Dharma does your master teach? The boy raised one finger. Returning, he told this to
Juzhi. Juzhi cut off his finger with a knife. The boy cried out and ran out. Juzhi called to him once.
The boy turned his head. Juzhi then raised one finger. The boy suddenly understood. Juzhi
