Raised: Linji asked the monastery superintendent, "Where have you come from?" The superintendent said, "From the prefecture seat,With a palm, he said, Come here.
selling yellow rice." Ji said, "Sold it all?" The superintendent said, "SoldYet it is solid through and through.Entering the weeds to seek someone.
it all." Ji drew one stroke with his staff and said, "Can you also sell thisTwo loads, and not turning the head.
one?" The superintendent immediately shouted; Ji immediately struck him. Next the cook arrived, and he relatedWhat deadly urgency is there?A toad croaks.Hands folded, bone-scraps.
the previous exchange. The cook said, "The superintendent did not understand master's meaning." Ji said,Show off less.The mouth is the gate of misfortune.
"And you—how would you do?" The cook immediately bowed. Ji also struck him.It comes right up onto your body.The more you turn it over, the less you can bear.At hand.
Quick.

The master said: In the original record there is no cook; instead he asked the alms steward, "Where have you been?"
The steward said, "I went to the prefecture seat to sell yellow rice and came back." Ji said, "Sold it all?" The steward
said, "Sold it all." Ji again drew one stroke with his staff and said, "Can you sell this all up
too?" The steward immediately bowed. Ji said, "Still a little closer." Wansong said: What
is not allowable here? The superintendent got the stick—reward does not avoid enemies. The steward received grace—punishment
does not choose kin. Tiantong proceeds according to the command to the fullest, wanting to show the total mechanism and great function.
The verse says:

lg.l:
Linji's full mechanism, his style and measure are lofty; the tip of the staff has eyes, discerning even autumn hairs.It is also worth giving a thrashing.One point.
Hard to deceive.

Sweeping away foxes and rabbits, his house style is severe; transforming fish and dragons, lightning and fire blaze.The lion in full majesty.big and small
spiritual powers

The life-giving sword, the killing knife; leaning on heaven, shining on snow, sharp enough to cut blown hair.still a bit betterthis lacquer bucket
who dares look straight at it

Though the same command is carried out, the flavor differs; in the place of deepest pain, who is it thatthis vinegar can be dried into strength
gets struck?struck and said, yes, yes, it is you

cb:div.p:
The master said: Linji at times takes away the person but not the situation; at times he takes away both person and situation.
If he meets the one in between, then the whole body acts in function. This is the highest point of Linji's style and measure.
When it comes out through the hand, he strikes on the hand. When it comes out through the eye, he strikes on the eye.
Coming from all four sides and eight directions, he strikes like a whirlwind. Li Lou was a man of the Yellow Emperor's time, seeing for a hundred li
even the tip of an autumn hair. The staff-tip has eyes, bright as sun and moon, not allowing even half a speck.
Not only does it sweep away foxes and rabbits, it can also transform fish and dragons. When the fish leaps the Yu Gate
three tiers, thunder and lightning burn its tail and it becomes a dragon. The swift mechanism and miraculous function of staff and shout are like this. The seven
things follow the body; there is the killing knife and life-giving sword. In Fushan Yuanyuan's Record, Master Yuanjian
set out sixteen topics and had Chan master Touzi Qing verse them; there too are the killing knife and the life-
giving sword. Xuedou, in his verse on Baling's hair-cutting sword, said: wanting levelness, yet not level; great skill seeming
clumsy. Sometimes a finger, sometimes a palm; leaning on heaven, shining on snow. In Song Yu's Great Words Fu: "Square earth as the carriage,
round heaven as the canopy. Bend the bow and shoot Fusang; the long sword leans beyond heaven." A monk
asked Linji, "What is the hair-cutting sword?" Ji said, "Trouble! Trouble!" Do you not
see it said: Deshan's vinegar is strong—once tasted, one knows its sourness. Linji's use came out of Huangbo; what was transmitted
down is not weak either. Zhu'an said: Three punches to the face, seven slaps across the cheeks—throughout the whole
earth people do not know pain or itch. Only one recognizes the pain, and he is still under treatment.
Have you not seen Linji say: It is like brushing with a wormwood twig.

cb:div.head: Case Ninety-Six: Jiufeng Refused
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Yunju did not rely on precept-pearls and relics. Jiufeng did not prize sitting and casting off.
dying upright. Niutou did not want the flowers of a hundred birds. Huangbo did not envy floating a cup across
the water. Tell me, what other excellence is there?

Raised: Jiufeng served as attendant under Shishuang. After Shishuang passed away, the assembly wanted to invite
The head monk in the hall was to continue the abbacy, but Jiufeng would not approve.then one may study skill without tricks, not like Xiou brushing away dust
He said, Wait till I question him. If he understands our late teacher's meaning, then I will serve him as I served our late teacher.
So he asked, Our late teacher said, Rest, cease; in a single thought, ten thousandseeing injustice on the roadwhy waste effort
years; go like cold ashes and dead wood, go like a strip of white silk. Tell me, what side of the matter does this clarify? The head monk said, It clarifies the side of one color.forgetting the front and losing the back, Hanwhat breath is therestrictly avoid
stainonly seek no trouble
Jiufeng said, If so, then you have not yet understood our late teacher's meaning.both kinds are settledonce power is in hand
The head monk said, If you do not approve of me, then bring incense. The head monk then burned incense and said,truly does not understand
If I do not understand our late teacher's meaning, then when the incense smoke rises, may I be unable to cast off this body.rage can kill a person
When he had finished speaking, he immediately died sitting. Jiufeng then patted his back and said, Dying seated,what sort of place is this, to go on like that
dying standing, that is not absent; but as for our late teacher's meaning, you have not even dreamed of it.getting out is still easyspeaking beyond the body should be hard

The teacher said, Chan Master Daoqian of Jiufeng in Yunzhou personally received Shishuang's Way and obtained
the staff of killing and giving life; he had the vital nose of a patchrobed monk. The head monk was carrying a board on his shoulders, grasping only a single stump.
At that time, when he saw him and said, If so, then you have not understood our late teacher's meaning, he should simply have said, I am not
equal to you. Then Jiufeng would have had nowhere to hide and would have bowed in wholehearted submission. Have you not heard it said,
Contesting is not enough; yielding leaves more than enough. People studying nowadays only say that the ancients
could die seated or standing, while people now at the end are all hands and feet in confusion. Also, Ouyang Wenzhong
saw an old monk on Mount Song and said, People now are in confusion in thought after thought; at death, how
can they gain steadiness? This lot only seeks quick birth and quick death. Jiaofan wrote in verse,
When it should end, just let it end; dying seated or standing is bragging for children. Curd comes from milk by no other method;
there is no separate dharma. Why painfully want to know beforehand when you will die? Two court officials asked Baofeng Zhaohe
the monk, The ancients were free in coming and going at death; by what Way did they achieve this? Baofeng said, Former monks
brought this up and then hanged themselves. At his death, the monks asked Baofeng for final instruction. Baofeng uttered some harsh
words and then died. If Shishuang's head monk had reached this ground, he would have avoided being
driven to death by Jiufeng. Foguo, in instruction to the monk Gao, said, Alas, seeing one stream of
blind-slapping wild fox breeds, who have never even dreamed of the patriarchs, yet falsely transmit that Bodhidharma
passed on the embryo breathing method to people, calling it transmitting Dharma to save deluded feelings, and even cite
the most senior ancient masters, such as National Teacher An and Zhaozhou, saying they all practiced this
breathing. They boast of the First Patriarch's single sandal and Puhua's empty coffin, all claiming this technique has proof.
Thus they arrive at casting off the whole body, calling it both form and spirit wondrous. Yet people dearly love this
body, and fear panic on the thirtieth day of the twelfth month. They compete to transmit methods of returning to the real; on New Year's Eve
they watch their shadow and call to the master within, to divine days and months, listen to the tower drum, test the jade pool, spy on
the light in the eyes, taking this for a method to escape birth and death. Truly they deceive and frighten the common people, fabricating false nests.
They leave worthy people with mockery and contempt. There is another sort, falsely relying on the First Patriarch's embryo breathing teaching, and on Zhaozhou's
separate songs of the twelve hours, and Layman Pang's turning the river cart verse, passing them back and forth in instruction,
secretly transmitting practice and discipline, seeking long life and complete bodily departure, some hoping for three or five
hundred years. They do not realize this is truly delusive thought and attachment. Wansong said, Nowadays,
those who look down on all quarters mostly want people watching at their last moment; smearing smoke on a swelling
what is there to rejoice in? Shishuang spent his life establishing the Dead Wood Hall, settling a dead-wood assembly.
They often always sat and did not lie down; many died seated or standing. Only Jiufeng did not
approve of the head monk. Those nowadays who prize dying seated or standing, why not investigate Jiufeng's
point of refusal? Tell me, what function did Jiufeng possess? Ask Tiantong.
The verse says,

lg.l:
In Shishuang's lineage, personally transmitted to Jiufeng, he cast off at the incense smoke's rise.bees swarm and ants gatherice melts and tiles fall apartfree in life and death
not absent

The true vein is hard to pass through; the crane in the moon nest dreams a thousand-year dream.the former master's meaning has not even been dreamed ofthe tree falls
does not fly

In the snow hut a man is lost in the work of one color; though sitting off the ten directions, he is still only dottingafter sunrise, one whole bout of vexation
the forehead.strictly avoid taking root
Secretly shift one step and see the flying dragon.another kind of creation

cb:div.p:
The teacher said, Tiantong is humane and just, first cutting at the poor side; worldly feeling always leans toward the wealthy
house. Wansong said, In gate-and-court devices, he is not equal to Jiufeng; in deep discourse entering principle,
he is still a hundred paces beyond the seat-monk. Monk Shengmo composed a verse on patriarchal court history,
The head monk died vainly in one stick of incense smoke; Jiufeng was not suppressing a worthy man. If one takes one color
as succession, one betrays the late teacher's unborrowed condition. Shishuang once instructed the assembly, saying,
If one has not yet forgotten illumination, that is still external succession, the seed of a minister; it is also called borrowing. If one is born forth,
with not the slightest separation, like a prince newly born, then he can inherit the great seat. This is called inner
succession, named the royal seed; named the phrase of no borrowing. Borrowing is merely the side of one color.
If there is no choice, responding to conditions to benefit beings is a carried accompaniment. Dotting the forehead, the flying dragon, is also like fish transforming at Yu Gate
into dragons. It is also the Qian hexagram in the Zhou Yi, Nine-Five, the flying dragon in the sky, the image of attaining position.
How could it be compared to the moon-nest crane's dream or the snow-hut man's delusion? Do you yet recognize where Shishuang
transmitted to Jiufeng? Tear open the incense pouch and scent the great nation; pry open the heavenly aperture and roar
the true wind.

cb:div.head: The Ninety-Seventh Case: Emperor Guang's Headcloth
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Bodhidharma met Emperor Wu of Liang, fundamentally to transmit mind. Yanguan recognized Dazhong.
He truly had eyes. When the realm is at peace and the king lives long, not offending heavenly majesty, the sun
and moon pause their brilliance, the four seasons are harmonious, and there is radiant transforming influence. When the king of men and the Dharma king meet,
what should they discuss?

Citing the case: Emperor Tongguang said to Xinghua, I have obtained one treasure of the Central Plain.
Only no one can appraise its value. Xinghua said, Let me borrow Your Majesty's treasure to see. The emperor pulled the ends of his headcloth with both hands. Xinghua said, The ruler's treasureshow off lessnot exchanged even for a kingdomaccording to the opportunity
follow the momentumluckily met the right person
who would dare appraise it?handed over all at once, with not the least lacking

The teacher said, Chan Master Cunjiang of Xinghua in Wei Prefecture first relied on Linji. When Linji passed away,
he became head monk under Sansheng. Later he met Dajue. At his opening Dharma hall he held up incense and said, This one stick of
incense, by rights, is for elder brother Sansheng. Sansheng is too exacting for me. By rights it is for
elder brother Dajue. Dajue is too distant for me. Better to offer it to Linji, our late
teacher. A monk asked, When one comes from the four directions and eight sides, what then? Xinghua said, Strike the middle
one. The monk bowed. Xinghua said, Great assembly, Xinghua yesterday went to a village feast.
Halfway he met a sudden wind and violent rain, and took shelter in a spirit shrine to get through. In Later Tang,
when Zhuangzong made an imperial tour to Hebei, a monk asked, When the royal itinerary is limited, what then? Xinghua said, Daily
travel five hundred li. Returning to the temporary palace in Wei Prefecture, he summoned Xinghua. After granting him a seat and tea,
he then asked, I have taken the Central Plain and obtained one treasure; no one has yet appraised it.
Xinghua said, Let me briefly borrow Your Majesty's treasure to see. The emperor pulled the ends of his headcloth with both hands. Xinghua
said, The ruler's treasure who dares appraise? The emperor was greatly pleased and bestowed a purple robe and a title.
Xinghua accepted none of them. He also bestowed one imperial horse. Wansong said, First one must recognize
the ruler, and also know where the Central Plain lies; only then ask where your treasure is. Xuanzue
challenged, saying, Tell me, did Xinghua approve of Tongguang, or not? If he approved, Xinghua's
eye was in what place? If he did not approve, where was Tongguang's fault? Wan
song said, Subhuti did not understand the Diamond meaning, and questioned till doubt filled the world. Xuedou
said, What the highest sovereign obtained can only be viewed from the side. If not for Xinghua as a master hand, it would often
have been appraised at a high price. Wansong said, Just like coming without fasting. Cuiyan Zhi
said, Xinghua's move at that time could be called dead drunk. How would you judge it now?
Wansong said, Take a beating, then decide the case. Yunfeng Yue said, The true does not hide the false; the crooked
does not conceal the straight. Those with eyes should discern it. Wansong said, Asked a blind fellow. Huang
Longxin said, For one period Xinghua acted upon the occasion, but in the end he buried that one
court emperor. At that time, if he had only told him, A pearl from clam and mussel, even if obtained, is of no
use, then he would have given him another way to live thereafter and spared him mutual dull stumbling. And
now if someone asks, how then would you appraise it? Wansong said, Seven times nine, six
thirteen total. This whole gang of old fellows had none who dared set a price. Only Tian
tong matched the thing and made a valuation, with no shortfall on either side. The verse says,

lg.l: The ruler's meaning speaks to one who knows the tune; all under heaven pour out sincerity like mallow and sunflower hearts.one good word spokenblessing responds for a thousand li

He lifts out the priceless treasure of the Central Plain, unlike Zhao's jade disk and Yan's gold.entrusted with both handsa different lineage altogether
precious

The treasure of the Central Plain presented to Xinghua, one whole piece of brightness hard to fix a price for.entrust it properlybuy oneself
sell oneself

The imperial enterprise can serve as teacher for ten thousand ages; the golden wheel's splendor shines over the four quarters.splitting open past and presentstill has
transformation remains

cb:div.p:
The teacher said, Tongguang was a reign title, just as the Dazhong emperor was Xuanzong. The Later
Tang Zhuangzong was on the throne three years. In the guǐwèi year he changed it to the first year of Tongguang. Calling it the Central Plain's
one treasure is already a forced name. After all, what should it be called? Even so, Tiantong
only managed to say, The ruler's meaning speaks to one who knows the tune. Look at Zhuangzong's campaigning:
from Zhending he subdued Zhongshan, took Yuyang and Wei-Bo as well, rode across the river,
and the Liang house lost the state. A detached force pointed west, and Jiange was not held. Therefore he himself
called it obtaining one treasure of the Central Plain, only no one could appraise it. This is because on the side of patchrobed monks,
if they do not meet one who knows the tune, if they do not encounter one who can discern, they never open the fist. Xinghua poured out
sincerity and dared not betray the great treasure of the heavenly storehouse. Mallow and sunflower turn toward the sun and incline, guarding their
feet. Therefore the foot-amputated Bian He was not equal to mallow. Lifting out the Central Plain's
priceless treasure, this verse refers to pulling the headcloth ends. The wheel-turning king's topknot jewel cannot lightly
be entrusted. Among emperors ancient and modern in direct exchange, none matched Zhuangzong in skillfully using the occasion.
Buddhist matter and human feeling were both fully complete at once. An official visited Guizong. Guizong picked up
the two ends of his cap and showed them, saying, Do you understand? The official said, I do not. Guizong said,
This old monk suffers head wind, do not blame me for not taking off my cap. The official was speechless. Wansong
said, Han Xin's merit was so high, who could compare? He turned and tore down the cloud-linked plank roads. The state of Zhao
had He Shi's jade disk. King Zhao of Yan built a terrace and placed a thousand gold pieces upon it to invite
worthy men from under heaven, so it was called the Golden Terrace. Again the verse says, The treasure of the Central Plain, one whole
piece of brightness, shines on heaven and mirrors earth. Nanquan said, Do not make it noble, do not make it base. How do you
buy it? Therefore it is hard to fix a price. The golden wheel king rules four quarters; the silver
wheel, three; the bronze, two; the iron, one. Following this, you can know the rest. Though the golden wheel has seven
treasures, it can only shine over four quarters. It does not equal the one treasure of the Central Plain that fills the ten-direction Dharma
realm, all one whole piece of brightness. But that Xinghua, too easily he said, Let me briefly borrow
Your Majesty's treasure to see. Tongguang was emperor of one dynasty, acting as a great donor, making an offering
to him. He can be called a ruler of true caliber, naturally possessing it. Wansong often laughed at San
jiao, who instructed the assembly saying, In general, expounding Dharma must match the time and season. But when he came to live in a hermitage,
he met a bandit holding a knife who asked, Monk, do you have any treasure? Sanjiao said, A monk's
treasure is not fitting for you. The bandit said, What treasure is it? Sanjiao then shouted. The bandit did not
understand and struck him with the blade. Fayu wrote in verse, Sanjiao dwelt in a thatched hut on the peak; his family
treasure he could not keep deeply concealed. In haste he opened the sack and misled the appraiser. Bian He lost his feet
for no reason but error. To meet Jie and Zhou and offer rites and music is a mistake; unlike Xinghua and Zhuangzong,
they did not injure harmony. A monk asked Xiutiebi of Fayun Yuantong, I know teacher has long stored
the treasure in the sack. Today before the assembly, let me briefly borrow it to see. Bi said, A gentleman loves wealth, but takes
it by the Way. Do not say Fayun cannot produce it; you must know Xinghua dared to negotiate.

cb:div.head: The Ninety-Eighth Case: Dongshan's Constant Concern
cb:div.p:
Addressing the assembly, he said: Jiufeng cut off tongues, echoing Shishuang. Caoshan chopped off heads and did not wrong
Dongling. The ancients' three-inch tongue was so close-knit. But where, after all, is the means for dealing with people
found?

Citing the case: A monk asked Dongshan, Among the three bodies, which body does not fall into the various categories? Dongshan said, I am always concerned about this.front three-three, back
three-threerage can kill a person

The teacher said, In the original record the question is, Among the three bodies, which one preaches the Dharma? Dongshan said, I
am always concerned about this. Chan Master Renshu of Shushan first asked Dongshan, I have not yet had the word;
please, teacher, instruct me. Dongshan said, Not granted; no one approves. Shu said, Is it still something to be concerned about
or not? Dongshan said, Right now, can you concern yourself with it or not? Shu said, I cannot concern myself with it;
then there is no place to conceal. Dongshan approved him. Later a monk asked Caoshan, Our late teacher said, I am always
concerned about this. What is the meaning of this? Caoshan said, If you want a head, chop it off and take it away. The monk then asked
Xuefeng. Xuefeng split his mouth with the staff and struck him, saying, I too have been to Dongshan.
Chengtian Zong said, One turning phrase and seas are calm, rivers clear. One turning phrase and wind is high, moon
cold. One turning phrase and you ride the bandit's horse to chase the bandit. Try and discern it. Suppose there were a patchrobed
monk who came forth and said, None of it is like that. One might allow he has one eye. It turns out
Under Yaoshan, the descendants all alluded from the side and guarded the tabooed name; many good examples are alike. Have you not seen: a monk asked
Shishuang, What is the Patriarch's meaning in coming from the West? Shishuang then gnawed his teeth to show it. The monk
did not get it. After Shishuang passed away, he asked Jiufeng, What was the intent of the former teacher's teeth-gnashing?
Feng said, I would rather cut off my tongue than violate the state taboo. The monk then asked Yungai.
Gai said, What grudge or enmity do I have with the former teacher? Master Yong of Baoning versified it, saying:
This most deserves pity, seeking outside. Why does the closest kin seem like enemies? From first to last, full
faced and without shame, and then Caoshan even begged your head. Good indeed, very good, but it offends too much
in the wind and dust. Look now how Tiantong is subtle and dense in so many ways. The verse says:

Not entering the world, not yet following conditions; in the empty place of the kalpa-pitcher there is a family transmission. White water-plants, fine wind, autumn river at dusk; at the old bank a boat returns in a strip of mist.placing oneself outside thingsscraping together a householdcat
urine on the roofclear, empty, and austere
。gaze broken off at the horizon

The master said: Not entering the world, not yet following conditions, cheeks seen behind the head; do not associate with him. Kalpa-
pitcher empty, there is family transmission. Dongshan sang thus, Caoshan harmonized thus, Xue-
feng dispatched it thus. The three stations must all be urged by everyone; for the Yellow River was muddy already at the source.
It was turbid from its very headwaters. In Zhou Qi's Record of Local Customs: ping and pin are names of water herbs; the larger is
pin, the smaller is ping. As for white pin, in Liu Yun's poem: On the sandbar lake we gather white pin; at day's
end, south of the river is spring. In the cave there is a returning traveler; by Xiaoxiang he meets an old friend. Later people named
that place White-Pin Islet. As for fine wind on the autumn river at dusk, in Song Yu's Fu on the Wind: Now
the wind is born at the tips of green pin, gradually spreads through streams and valleys, and follows along the slopes of Mount Tai.
It dances beneath pines and cypresses. It is called white pin because the shoots are green while the flowers are white.
I dare ask: to what place does the boat returning to the old bank go? Who knows? In the distant mist and waves there is another
fine matter for reflection.

cb:div.head: Case Ninety-Nine: Yunmen's Bowl and Bucket
cb:div.p:
An address to the assembly said: Chess has a special wit; wine has a different gut. The crafty hare has three burrows; the sly clerk has
ten thousand dodges. And there is yet one more slippery fellow. Tell me, who is it?

Cited: A monk asked Yunmen, What is the samadhi gate in every dust?having a vow, not scattering sand
He said, Rice in the bowl, water in the bucket.bumping the head, striking the brow, blocking the cheeks, smacking the face

The master said: In Huayan, one dust enters right absorption and the samadhi of all dusts arises. It also says:
Each dust thus, each dharma thus. What this monk asked for was that Yunmen directly, face to face, bring it out.
Yunmen said, Rice in the bowl, water in the bucket. Tell me, did he bring it out or not bring it out?
Some immediately take it as matching the sound and passing the matter along, showing their device on the phrase. Some say:
The rice in the bowl, every grain is round; the water in the bucket, every drop is wet. There is yet another sort
who in deadly haste says: The bowl has rice in it, the bucket has water in it. Foguo
said, Rinse your mouth for three years, precisely because of you people. Xuedou said, This many-mouthed teacher is hard
to get a bite on; turns out you are timid. Look now: Tiantong splits open the face-skin. The verse says:

Rice in the bowl, water in the bucket; open your mouth and your gall is seen. Seeking to know, once thought is set in motion, you fall into second and third-rate devices; face to face, suddenly it becomes a thousand myriad miles.bowl-filled and ladled outonly because utmost clarity turns over
causing delay where it should arriveTiantong, fourth
Master Shaoyang was a little sharper; the meaning of cutting metal, with whomsurely return earlynot yet daring to guarantee
is it the same? A heart not of stone, only he could be like this.the heart does not fail peoplethe face shows no shame

The master said: A monk asked National Teacher Zhong, What is the great meaning of the Buddha Dharma? The teacher said, In Manju-
shri Hall are ten thousand bodhisattvas. The monk said, I do not understand. The teacher said, Great Compassion has a thousand
hands and eyes. In Fogjian's verse: In these years vegetables are cheap; the ground is full of radish tops. For one coin
you buy one, and whoever gets it is stuffed and snoring. Right here you can see Tiantong and Yunmen
extending one hand together, lifting the broken-legged pot. In the Appended Phrases of the Zhou Yi: The way of the noble man,
at times he goes forth, at times he stays; at times silent, at times speaking. When two are of one mind, their sharpness cuts metal.
Words of those of one mind, their fragrance is like orchids. The note says: Metal is hard and firm, yet they can
cut it; this speaks of extreme sharpness. In the Mao Odes, Bei, Bozhou: My heart is not stone,
it cannot be turned. My heart is not a mat, it cannot be rolled. The note says: Though stone is hard it still
can be turned; though a mat is flat it still can be rolled. This says one's resolve is firmer and flatter than stone
or mat. Tell me then, National Teacher Zhong, Fogjian, Yunmen, Tiantong, why are they all so urgent and heated
for? Red heart, piece by piece, few know it; piled up right before the face, few are those who see it.

cb:div.head: Case One Hundred: Langya's Mountains and Rivers
cb:div.p:
An address to the assembly said: One word can raise a state; one word can ruin a state. This medicine
can also kill a person; this medicine can also revive a person. The humane see it and call it humane.
The wise see it and call it wisdom. Tell me, where do benefit and harm lie?

Cited: A monk asked Master Langya Jue, Purity is originally so; how then do there suddenly arise
mountains, rivers, and the great earth? Jue said, Purity is originally so; how then do there suddenly arisewhen deluded, the three realms exist
mountains, rivers, and the great earth?after awakening, the ten directions are empty

The master said: Chan Master Wude Zhao of Fenyang, in the bitter cold of the north, because he stopped the evening assembly,
a foreign monk rode in on clouds and urged him, Do not miss the time. Though this assembly is not many, six
are great vessels; their Way will shade humans and gods. The next day Fenyang ascended the hall and said: The foreign monk's golden staff
shone; for the Dharma he came to Fenyang. Six men will become great vessels; he urged and invited me to expound. At that time
Dayu Zhi, Ciming Yuan, Langya Jue, Fahua Ju, Tiansheng Tai, and Shi-
shuang Yong and others were all seated below. On Langya Mountain in Chuzhou, the Chan Master Guangzhao of Kaihua,
whose taboo name was Huijue, was a man of Western Luo. His father was governor of Hengyang. When he died, they escorted the coffin
back to Luo. Passing through Lizhou, he climbed the ancient monastery on Yaoshan to pay respects; seeing the places he had walked,
it was just like an old home. Because of this he left home, received Dharma from Fenyang, and responded to conditions at Chuzhou.
Together with Xuedou Mingjue, he proclaimed the Way at the same time; the world pointed to them as the two gates of sweet dew.
Down to the present, the remaining influence in Huainan is as before. Master Zhilin of Hunan, whenever he merely saw a monk
come, would at once say, A demon comes, a demon comes, and brandish a wooden sword at him, then slip into the abbot's room. Thus
it went for twelve years. Later he put aside the sword and said nothing. A monk asked, Twelve years ago, why
did you subdue demons? Lin said, Thieves do not rob a poor household. The monk said, After twelve years,
why do you not subdue demons? Lin said, Thieves do not rob a poor household. This is called
the meaning of one sword stroke dividing the body. In the fourth scroll of the Surangama, Purna asked: If
all roots and dusts in the world, the aggregates, sense-fields, and realms, are all the Tathagata-store, originally pure,
how then do mountains, rivers, and the great earth, all conditioned forms, suddenly arise, successively changing
and flowing, ending and beginning again? Commentators say: If understood, then one knows the body of awakening is originally
wondrous, ignorance is originally empty, and mountains and rivers and the great earth are like flowers in the sky. If deluded, then subject and object
are falsely divided, forced awareness suddenly arises, the three subtle aspects become the world, the four wheels form the realm. Langya
said, I am not like that: Purity is originally so; how then do mountains, rivers, and the great earth suddenly arise?
This is called riding the thief's horse to chase the thief, seizing the thief's spear to kill the thief. Xin of Jianfu said, At first
it does not go far enough; in the end it goes too far. Wansong said, Xu Liu on the eaves-board, each sees one
side. If you want to remove the seepage in your seeing, you must see Tiantong. The verse says:

lg.l:
Seeing being, not being; turning the hand, overturning the hand. The man in Langya Mountainthe same kind of dough and strawmade by peoplehands folded
says Huijue
does not fall behind Gautama.one word wounds a person, like a thousand knives stirring the belly

cb:div.p:
The master said: See being as not being, and being decays of itself. See the strange as not strange, and the strange itself
collapses. The Mahayana Treatise, composed by the Fourteenth Patriarch Nagarjuna, says:
All dharmas, because of all causes and conditions, should be said to exist. All dharmas, because of all causes
and conditions, should be said not to exist. This is turning the hand and overturning the hand. Langya, ascending the hall, said: Seeing,
hearing, awareness, and knowing are all causes of birth and death. Seeing, hearing, awareness, and knowing are all the basis
of liberation. It is like a lion wheeling and throwing itself, south, north, east, west, with no fixed stopping place. If you people
do not understand, then do not betray old Shakyamuni. Hum! This is why
he does not fall behind Gautama. Gautama is Sanskrit, fully spoken as Qiaodamo.
Here it means Earth-Supreme, because apart from the heavens, among people on earth he is the most outstanding. Now
in the later five hundred years, far removed in time from the sage, people are mostly lax. How can one avoid falling
behind? Split apart past and present.

cb:juan.cb:jhead:
Elder Wansong's comments and verses on Master Tiantong Jue's verses on ancient cases, Record of the Hut of Calm, scroll
sixend

cb:div.head: textual notes

app.lem: QianBook of the Later Han, Wuying Hall Twenty-Four Histories edition, fascicle 118, page 20a.

obtainWansong the Elder’s Comments and Chanting on Tiantong Jue the Master’s Verse Commentary on Old Cases, Record of Congrong Hermitage (Korea, Kim Ja-hyeon collection; Guangxu 7, China, Gusu Sutra Carving Office edition, vol. 8, p. 19a).T51n2076_p0274a11

cb:div.head: CBETA textual notes

p.note: Taisho has dian; the checked original omits it.
Taisho has heat; the checked original has cooked.
Taisho has eye; the checked original has clear.
Taisho has have; the checked original has have not.
Taisho has staff; the collated original has hazel.
Taisho has point; the collated original has instead.
Taisho has come; the checked original has not yet.
Taisho has blade edge; the checked original has knife.
Taisho has lattice; the checked original has exquisite.
Taisho has chuo; the Jia source has chuo variant.
Taisho has leave; the checked original has difficult.
Taisho has use; the checked original has name.
Taisho has want; the checked original has and.
Taisho has have; the collated original has attached.
Taisho has if; the checked original has says.
Taisho has rain; the checked original has cloud.

cb:div.head: Taisho canon textual notes

p.note: Original: Komazawa University copy, the three characters Congrong Lu are newly added.
The collator says the original table of contents was at the head of each fascicle and is now gathered here.
Case titles newly added, same below.
Dian omitted in the original.
Heat equals cooked in the original.
Eye equals clear in the original.
Add have after no in the original.
Staff is written as hazel in the original.
Point is written as instead in the original.
Come is written as not yet in the original.
Blade edge is written as knife in the original.
Lattice is written as exquisite in the original.
Chuo is written as the variant chuo in the Jia source.
Leave is written as difficult in the original.
Use is written as name in the original.
Want is written as and in the original.
Have is written as attached in the original.
If is written as says in the original.
Rain is written as cloud in the original.
